<<if ($PlayerOccult + $PlayerIntelligence) gte 15>><<if $Read gte 7>>Pulling the musty old tome from its resting place inside my heavy coat, I shiver as the cool air hits the place it was resting.  In contrast, the book is warm against my fingers like a living thing.

Once I have sat the book on the small table in my room, I look at it for a long while.  Concern etches my face as I realize just how precisely I have placed the book on the table, //Its placement is every bit as precise as when I first found the book.//  I dismiss the thought, then reach for the front cover.

The book creaks softly as the cover is opened, revealing the book's contents.  //I've spent a lot of hours with this book already.  I'm growing familiar with a lot of its content.//  I'm uncertain that my familiarity with the book's contents is a good thing.  Even so, I carefully turn the pages until I reach the last section of the book that I have not looked at in much detail.  

With each turn of a page, I become more and more aware of the way my fingertips linger against the coarse paper, caressing the pages as each horrid page moves past my vision.

I'm in a trance like state when I finally reach the section I was working my way toward.

It is almost like I am possessed when I reach those final pages.  I know that something is wrong, I can feel it deep down inside of myself like some clawed beast trying to savage its way out of my body.

My vision begins to fade along the edges, shifting to something completely foreign to me as I look at the page before me and see, . . . . something truly divine.  The words I have spent so many days struggling to translate comes to me in a rush of understanding even as it drives me mad.

passages.name: Changelog:
passages.content:
<<back>>

Changelog 1.15
*Added one new branching event to the trail.  This one introduces a new horror to the island.
*Added the "Strange Plant" to the case notes for reference.
*One new prostitution scene added to the game.
*One new ending added to the game associated with the prostitution.
*Includes the usual assortment of bug fixes, spelling/grammar fixes, and all that fun stuff.
*Changelogs have been changed.  We have blind players who play the game using a text reader and their text readers do not read dashes properly all the time, but WILL read asterisks.  Short of this somehow breaking the game for other players, all future changelogs in all of our games will use asterisks instead of the dashes that have been used up to this point.
*It has been reported that sometimes Mr Morichi acts like you haven't met Azumi during the ending when you in fact have.  I spent a couple of hours trying to find the source of this bug but was unable to locate it.  In the end, I made some adjustments that will hopefully resolve the issue.
*Updated "Special Thanks" area with Patrons who pledged at the $10 and up levels.  1/8/19
*Added about 9k words of content to the game bringing the game's total size up to about 477k words of content.


Changelog 1.14
-Wrote a DP scene for the prostitution function.  Even managed to work a line into the scene that was a nod to one of my favorite games.  ;)
-Wrote a slightly degrading scene for the prostitution function.
-Wrote a new blowjob scene for the prostitution function.
-Wrote a titjob scene for the prostitution function.
-Includes the usual assortment of bug fixes, spelling/grammar fixes, and all that fun stuff.
-Updated "Special Thanks" area with Patrons who pledged at the $10 and up levels.  12/12/18
-Added about 8k words of content to the game bringing the game's total size up to about 468k words of content.


Changelog 1.13
-Added a second way into the mansion for those who get thrown out.  Includes new scene

"Not at all,"  He finally looks at me, a sad smile on his face, "Take a seat, be sentimental with me."

I start to step around to chair beside him.  At the same time, he half closes the photo album in his hand and moves his own chair closer to the one I am settling in to.

"My daughter might hate me,"  His tone is sad, "But she really is the one bright spot in my life,"  A hint of a smile crosses his face, just touching his eyes, "Well, her and this room on the rare sunny day."

[IF $WhoreEnd gte 10]沒有前戲，只有男人原始的需求，但這對我來說沒關係，因為我也感同身受。我不在乎他是誰，我只在乎他的陰莖以及它即將帶給我的快感。

他把我推倒在他坐著的桌子上。他的一隻手粗魯地抓住我的屁股，但我只是呻吟，一想到即將發生的事，我的小穴就湧出液體。

我的褲子被粗暴地拉下，將我的臀部暴露在房間涼爽的空氣中。我顫抖著，但這與寒意關係不大。

隨著他踢了一下我的腳踝，男人分開了我的雙腿。我能感覺到空氣輕撫著我裂縫上的濕潤。

「幹我，」我放蕩的呻吟充滿空氣，懇求他佔有我。

他照做了。他堅硬的陰莖觸碰我的大腿內側，然後抵在我的裂縫上。他甚至不需要等我濕透，我已經能感覺到我的汁液順著大腿流下。他向前推進，他的龜頭分開了我的陰唇，卻錯過了我的穴口。我像個普通的妓女一樣呻吟，伸手到雙腿之間試圖引導他進入。當他第二次向前挺進時，他的陰莖找到了目標，他一次插入就滑了進來，直抵深處，整根沒入。

我擺動臀部，享受著被如此填滿的感覺，同時又向他乞求更多。

他設定了快速的節奏，他的臀部每次向前挺進都用力到將我在桌上推得更遠一點。如果他沒有如此緊抓我的臀部，他可能會把我幹得直接穿過「酒保的女兒」。

毫無疑問，除了能在我陰道深處找到的快感外，他對我毫不在乎。但我不再在乎了，他可以用任何他想要的方式使用我。除了此刻感受到的強烈滿足感，其他一切對我都不重要。

他的速度持續加快，每一次挺進都比上一次更快。他的臀部撞擊我的濕潤聲響充滿空氣。

下一秒，他的節奏變得不規律，當他接近高潮時，喘息聲充斥四周。

我能感覺到男人的陰莖在我貪婪的小穴內搏動。感受體內溫暖顫動的肉體是極樂，但當他開始將他熱燙黏稠的種子注入我的深處時，我幾乎因快感而失去理智。

喘息著，我能感覺到我的整個身體因似乎永無止境的高潮而顫抖。我為什麼會想讓這結束呢？我想。在我腦海的某處，有什麼試圖反駁這個想法，但我不再在乎了。在我存在的核心深處，我知道我迷失了，除了想讓我的身體被用來帶給他人快感之外，我不再想要任何其他東西。

當我感到他的陰莖從我的穴口滑出時，一聲女性的嗚咽從我唇間逸出。我的身體渴望它的回歸，即使我知道這男人暫時已經耗盡了。

即便如此，我還是跪下來，用嘴唇包裹住他沾滿精液、濕滑的陰莖，希望能誘使他重新振作。男人對我的努力輕笑，然後像丟棄無用之物般推開我。「別擔心，妓女，很快就會有人來給你需要的東西。」

等他離開後站起來，我能感覺到男人的精液順著我的大腿內側流下。

[繼續。][ELSE][IF $WhoreEnd gte 1]當我感到有人拉扯我臀部附近的夾克時，我的第一反應是退開。我迅速退了一步，同時轉身面向拉扯的方向。

我完全預期會看到一個看起來憤怒、傲慢、甚至驚訝的人。相反，我看到一個中年男人，穿著格子襯衫和吊帶褲，就像島上許多人一樣，用友好的表情看著我。

我的緊張感緩解了，但並未完全消失。

我的理智告訴我應該走開，我也想這麼做，但我的身體和嘴巴都背叛了我。我朝那兩個男人走了幾步，那個還沒說話的男人把手伸進我的夾克下，牢牢抓住我的一邊屁股，「如果你們兩個都想玩，你們兩個都得付錢。」

我到底怎麼了？我為什麼會說這種話？

Aaron, the bartender at the Barmaid's Daughter, and I suspect the owner of the establishment. passages.content:
<<if $MorichiMet gte 1>>Silhouetted by the light outside, I see Mr Morichi standing in the doorway of the Barmaid's Daughter, "Where is she?  Where is my daughter?"  He shouts, then falls silent when he notices that the tavern is empty with the exception of myself.  

I can see from his expression that he's distraught, nearly in a panic.  When he sees me, he starts moving rapidly toward me, leaning heavily on the cane in his left hand, <<if $Taint gte 70>>"Miss <<else>>"Mr <</if>><<print $PlayerName2>>!  You have to help me!"

Having never seen him standing before, I'm a little surprised at just how small the man is, "Slow down,"  I begin, "Take a deep breath and tell me what happened."

Mr Morichi closes his eyes, then takes a deep breath.  Letting it out slowly, I can see that his expression of near panic has calmed just a little bit toward worry, <<if $MotichiDaughter gte 2>>"Azumi has been taken."

"Are you sure she was taken, or did she just get out?"  I try to keep my voice calm and reasonable as I speak.

"Taken,"  There's a cold certainty in Mr Morichi's voice when he says the word.

"Ok, walk me through what happened, from your perspective.  When did you realize something was wrong?"

Mr Morichi looks as if he is about to protest, but then gives a curt nod, "Breakfast, it is my routine to bring her breakfast myself.  She'll rarely eat with me, but I always try.  When I arrived at her room, her door was open.  She was gone, her blankets were strewn across the floor between the bed and the door."

When he pauses, I ask, "Why does that lead you to believe she was taken?"

Mr Morichi sucks his lips, making them a thin line across his face for a second before replying, "She never treats her bedding like that, and there was blood on the door handle."

//Shit,// "Who could have taken her?"  I ask, suddenly concerned.

"My manservant,"  He replies flatly.

"Why do you think it was him?"

"All of the girls in my home are accounted for, only
passages.content_zh:
[IF $MorichiMet gte 1]在門外光線的映襯下，我看到 Morichi 先生站在「酒保的女兒」的門口。「她在哪？我的女兒在哪？」他喊道，接著注意到酒館裡除了我之外空無一人，便陷入了沉默。

從他的表情我能看出他心煩意亂，幾乎驚慌失措。他看到我時，開始快速朝我走來，左手沉重地倚著手杖，[IF $Taint gte 70]「[ELSE]「[/IF]$PlayerName2 小姐！你得幫幫我！」

從未見過他站著的樣子，我有點驚訝這男人竟是如此矮小。「慢點，」我開口道，「深呼吸，告訴我發生了什麼事。」

Morichi 先生閉上眼睛，然後深深吸了一口氣。他緩緩吐氣時，我能看出他近乎恐慌的表情稍微平復了一些，轉為憂慮，[IF $MotichiDaughter gte 2]「Azumi 被帶走了。」

「你確定她是被帶走的，還是她自己出去了？」我試圖讓自己的聲音保持冷靜理性。

「被帶走的。」Morichi 先生說出這個詞時，聲音裡帶著冰冷的確定。

「好，從你的角度告訴我事情經過。你什麼時候意識到不對勁的？」

Morichi 先生看起來像是要抗議，但隨後簡短地點了點頭，「早餐時間。我習慣親自為她送早餐。她很少和我一起吃飯，但我總是試著邀請她。當我到達她房間時，門開著。她不見了，她的毯子散落在床和門之間的地板上。」

他停頓時，我問道：「為什麼這讓你認為她是被帶走的？」

Morichi 先生抿緊嘴唇，讓它們在臉上形成一條細線，片刻後才回答：「她從來不會那樣對待她的寢具，而且門把上有血。」

該死。「誰可能帶走她？」我問道，突然擔憂起來。

「我的男僕。」他平淡地回答。

「你為什麼認為是他？」

「我家裡所有的女孩都在，只有他不見了。」他的眼神充滿自信。

「那他為什麼要帶走她？」我疑惑地問。

Morichi 先生看起來快要哭了，眼中盈滿淚水，「我早該料到會這樣。他以前為她母親工作。」他喃喃道，接著臉色迅速刷白，快得讓我準備好在他明顯腳步不穩時扶住他。

「那個洞穴，」他看著我，眼中充滿恐懼。「大教堂，他們會把她帶到那裡去。」

我甚至不用問，從他的語氣我就知道，無論發生什麼事都不妙，Azumi 的生命很可能正處於危險之中。

[IF $cave gte 2]「『他們』是誰？」我問道，已經開始朝門口走去。

「你知道大教堂在哪裡？」Morichi 先生問，似乎忽略了我先前的問題。他正跟在我身後移動，想加入我，但他倚靠手杖的樣子明顯無法快速行動。

「是的，我知道在哪。」我的語氣比預期的更激動。我在門口停下，轉身面對他，「現在，告訴我『他們』是誰，」我清晰地說出每個字，語氣帶著命令。

「每一個人。」Morichi 先生回答。[ELSE]「『他們』是誰？」我問道，已經開始朝門口走去。

「你知道大教堂在哪裡？」Morichi 先生問，似乎忽略了我先前的問題。他正跟在我身後移動，想加入我，但他倚靠手杖的樣子明顯無法快速行動。

「不，不太清楚。」我的語氣比預期的更激動。我在門口停下，轉身面對他，「我在哪裡可以找到這個大教堂，還有『他們』是誰，」我清晰地說出每個字，語氣帶著命令。

「每一個人，」Morichi 先生回答，「大教堂在森林小徑的北岔路走下去。」他模糊地指向鎮上糟糕的那一區。

[/IF][ELSEIF $MotichiDaughter gte 1]「Azumi，我的女兒。她被帶走了。」

「你確定她是被帶走的，還是她只是去散步之類的？」我試圖讓自己的聲音保持冷靜理性。

「被帶走的，」Morichi 先生說出這個詞時，聲音裡帶著冰冷的確定，「她不可能自己離開房間，除非有人放她出去。」

「你把她鎖在房間裡？」我問道，對這個訊息感到有些擔憂。

「她出來時很危險。」他輕描淡寫地說。

這事可以稍後再處理，現在找到她並幫助她必須是優先事項。我的回答帶著激動，近乎憤怒：「好，從你的角度告訴我事情經過。你什麼時候意識到不對勁的？」

passages.name: Ask Azumi about her mother.
passages.content:
<<if $LockedOut is "Played">>"Can I ask you a question, a potentially sensitive one?"  I ask Azumi.  Her body's warmth combined with the strong scent of her arousal is intoxicating, making it difficult to focus.

She looks at me with simple curiosity in her eyes.  Then she does something that surprises me into silence, "I think I love you, but won't make you tell me that you love me too."  Her eyes search my own, looking for something though I am unsure of what it could possibly be.

I open my mouth, but no words come out.  I'm completely uncertain of what to say or do next.  Lacking any better ideas, I pull her into a half hug.

"One of father's serving girls used to come to my room and talk to me every day.  I told her that I loved her and she gave me a hug,"  She begins to look awkward, "I couldn't help myself, I fucked her and she cried.  She said she wanted to leave, told me to stop, but I thought she was playing.  I made her tell me she loved me before I let her go.  I didn't understand the fear in her eyes when she left my room, or why she wouldn't stop crying.  I never saw her again."  The last few words are said very quietly.

There's a profoundly sad look in Azumi's eyes as she begins to pull away, "Please don't cry and leave me."

//Well, this whole thing jumped the rails in a hurry.  How the hell did we end up on this subject anyway?//

I can't help myself, I reach out and pull her into a full embrace.  She stiffens at first, then seems to relax in my arms, "I want to ask you a question that you may not want to, or maybe can't answer."  

I feel her lips on my neck, her breath hot as she kisses her way up toward my jawline, "I'm trying //so// hard to be good,"  She whispers in my ear before nibbling at my earlobe.  I squirm away from the sensation, fighting back a smile.

"Please don't stop coming to see me.  You're my favorite person,"  She leans in, her forehead touching mine.  Her eyes are close to mine as she looks into my own, "I wan

[IF $Taint gte 100][ELSEIF $Taint gte 75][ELSEIF $Taint gte 50][ELSEIF $Taint gte 25][ELSEIF $Taint gte 1][/IF]//我的夢境充滿了蠕動肉體的聲音與淫蕩的呻吟。[IF $Taint gte 65]它們誘惑著我，將我從床上引出來。[ELSE]它們讓我顫抖著將我從床上引出來。[/IF]
我僅穿著內衣，赤腳輕柔地踏過小房間，走向門口。空氣感覺異常悶濁，濃厚得彷彿能用刀劃開。

"Your photo album from before, there's a picture of him in there, holding Azumi when she was a baby,"  I begin.

Mr Morichi nods slowly, waiting for me to continue.

"No, unless he has a brother that looks just like him, then I think he may still be alive."

Amusement flashes across his features, but vanishes just as quickly when he realizes that I am serious.  Leaning back with an exaggerated slowness, he raises his cup for a small sip.

The surprise in Mr Morichi's eyes is all the confirmation I need to know its the same man, "He's clearly unwell, ranting, and difficult to understand.  He's got a cabin out there and bones //everywhere.//"

[IF $babyAzumi is "Played" && $AzumiFriend is "Played" && $Cabin is "Played"][IF ($PlayerEttiquette + $PlayerCharisma) gte 11]「恐怕我有件事想問，而你可能不太願意談論。」我謹慎地開口。

Morichi先生嘴角微微上揚，露出一絲笑意，「有時這就是友誼的代價。或許哪天拜訪時，我也會問個令人不快的問題，就為了看看你坐立不安的模樣，換換口味。」他的輕笑聽起來有些勉強。

「我想多問問關於牧師的事。」

Morichi先生唇邊那抹淺笑消失了，眼神明顯染上一層哀傷，「你可以問。」他回答。

「你之前給我看的相簿裡，有一張他抱著還是嬰兒的Azumi的照片。」我開始說道。

Morichi先生緩緩點頭，等我繼續說。

我花了幾秒鐘思考該如何繼續，最後決定直接切入重點，「我不確定他是自殺的。」

Morichi先生的眉毛微微揚起，「你認為有人謀殺了他？」他的目光始終沒有離開我，同時伸手去拿他的茶。

「不，除非他有個長得一模一樣的兄弟，否則我想他可能還活著。」

一絲興味掠過他的臉龐，但當他意識到我是認真的，那表情便迅速消失。他以一種誇張的緩慢速度向後靠，舉杯淺啜一口。

「我在森林裡遇到一個人。他說他叫Vick。」

Morichi先生眼中的驚訝，正是我所需的一切確認——那是同一個人。「他顯然狀況不佳，胡言亂語，難以理解。他在那裡有間小屋，到處都是骨頭。」

Morichi先生放下杯子，朝我傾身，「你這番話裡，有包含任何問題嗎？」

我張嘴想回答，隨即意識到他是對的，「我想沒有。」

「Victor Erkon曾經是，如果所言屬實，現在也仍是，一個好人。如果你再見到他，請讓他知道，我為他可能在我女兒手中經歷的任何不幸感到抱歉，並且如果他願意來訪，我家的大門依然為他敞開。」

Morichi先生陷入沉默，表情困擾，除此之外難以解讀。秒針滴答走成了分鐘，你們兩人靜靜坐著。最終，Morichi先生清了清喉嚨，「請原諒我招待不周。你帶來了老朋友可能仍在人世的消息，我卻以令人不安的沉默回報你。」

「沒關係，我想這是我在島上期間會進一步調查的事。如果我能發現什麼，我很樂意與你分享。」

Morichi先生點點頭，「我會很感激。」他的表情遙遠，彷彿心思仍在別處。

感覺無法再問出更多，我決定或許最好直接換個話題。

「我在森林裡遇到一個人。他說他叫Vick。」

She is conservatively dressed, with a collar that pulls snugly around her throat.  Her dress is of an expensive looking cut, black with shades of gray and just a hint of lace at the neckline and wrists.  

I'm not sure if the sounds of my footsteps draw her attention, or if its something else, but when her eyes raise from the book she is reading to meet my own, the cold certainty in her eyes is enough to break my stride.

//This is not someome to be triffled with.//

While the smile that follows is surely intended to be friendly, it never touches her eyes, and my earlier thought echoes in my mind.  "You're new to Wanton Cove,"  There is little real emotion in her deep feminine voice.

"Just passing through,"  I reply non-commitally.

Before I can increase my pace and make my escape, she continues, "Wanton Cove is a //funny// place to simply 'Pass through' as you put it.  People come here by design."  She tilts her head, considering me.  

//Why do I feel like a rat in some researcher's cage all of a sudden.//

"But forgive my rudeness,"  I can sense no appology in her tone, "My name is Lady Margaret Anne,"  She rises from her chair, walking to the edge of her deck as she says it, her hand thrusting forward as if in greeting when she reaches the deck.

Even though her moving to the edge of her deck only closed about a quarter of the distance seperating us, I feel compelled to close the rest of the distance to shake her hand.  

Standing three steps above me, she looks down her nose at me when I take her hand, shaking it briefly before releasing it.

passages.name: Night7
passages.content:
<<set $Bed to "False">><<set $Day += 1>><<set $CharHP = 10>><<set $Research to 0>><<if $Taint gte 100>><<set $Taint += 5>><<elseif $Taint gte 75>><<set $Taint += 4>><<elseif $Taint gte 50>><<set $Taint += 3>><<elseif $Taint gte 25>><<set $Taint += 2>><<elseif $Taint gte 1>><<set $Taint += 1>><</if>>I've barely laid down for some much deserved sleep when I hear an insistent knock on my room's door.

I start to pull my covers tighter around myself, trying to will whoever it is away.  Only the person isn't letting up as they continue to pound on my door, the pace becoming more urgent.

“Hold on a second!”  I half growl towards the door.  Throwing the covers off of myself, I reach for my .38 Special, then pad across the small room in nothing more than my underwear.  At the door, I pause, one hand resting on the handle and the other pointing my pistol vaguely in the direction of where I figure my unwanted guest would most likely be standing, “Who is it and what do you want?”  Even I can hear the annoyed anger in my tone.

“It's me,”  Tanya's voice comes clearly through the door, “Tanya,”  I can hear an unexpected urgency in her tone, “Let me in before someone sees me out here, we need to talk about Tom.”

Suddenly I'm wide awake, a surge of adrenaline causing my heart to race.  Before I even stop to think about it, I swing the door open to let her in.  She takes a half step before seeing the gun in my hand, her eyes widening at the sight.  I quickly lower it before taking the couple of steps to the table and setting the firearm down.  

By the time I turn back towards her, she's already closed the door behind herself.

I'm still trying to figure out what is going on when I see the first tear fall from her eye, tracing a gentle curve down the side of her nose towards her lips, “<<print $PlayerName>>,”  She says around a soft sob, “Why did you have to come here to save me?”  Her eyes turn slowly towards my own.

passages.name: Go talk with Toby.
passages.content:
<<if ($PlayerEttiquette + $PlayerCharisma) gte 13 and $Mutation lte 4>>Toby //Or maybe its better to call him Sophie?// looks startled when he notices me coming his way.  His eyes dart toward the house then back to me, "Hi,"  He says softly in an overly feminine fashion.  

Even though he tries to be subtle about it, I can tell he is staring at my crotch.  I quickly push the realization aside and try to engage in conversation with the man, "Hi,"  I reply, "Never really got to //talk// with you previously,"  I find myself choosing my words carefully, though that only seems to make my attempt to converse more awkward.

To his credit, Toby takes it in stride, "Yeah, I don't really get to //talk// often,"  He replies.

The two of you lapse into silence for a few seconds, though surprisingly it's not an uncomfortable silence.

"May I ask you a question?"  Toby asks softly, his tone and expression both suggesting that he's scared I could react harshly to the inquiry.

"Sure, ask,"  I reply.

Meeting my eyes evenly for the first time, he asks, "Why did you come to Wanton Cove?"

"That's a complicated question,"  I reply lightly.

"I, I didn't mean to pry, I'm sorry if I'm out of line,"  Toby says in a rush, suddenly scared.

"No, its fine,"  I reassure him.  After a second he visibly relaxes, //Wow, his wife really has him rattled,// "I guess you could say I came here for several reasons.  I came looking for a young man named Tom, but I had a more personal reason as well,"  I avoid fully answering his question.

After a short silence, you continue, "Mind if I ask you a couple of questions as well?"

Looking suddenly nervous again, Toby nods, "I guess the first question is simple enough, do you know anything about Tom?  He's a young man who would have passed through some time in the last few weeks.  I'm trying to learn more about his time here on the island, and I've been asked to bring him home."

passages.name: Cave Entrance
passages.content:
<<if $cave is 3 && $Day gte 7 && $Vick is "Played">>Over the sound of the storm, the rain falling between the leaves of trees in a steady stream to the ground below punctuated by the occasional rumble of thunder, I can hear something else.

//Its coming from the Cathedral.//  I realize, my pace quickening even as my posture changes to move more stealthily.

I can just make out the gaping maw in the earth through the trees when I finally figure out what I'm hearing, //Chanting of some sort.//

When the cave becomes clearer, my pace slows, my hand drifting beneath my jacket to rest on my trusty .38 Special.

Finally reaching the cave, I remain near the side of the opening, looking deeper into the cave.  I can't see a lot from where I am, but at the same time I'm uncertain how visible I would be if I tried to enter.

The interior of the cave is well lit,  I can see several light sources from my vantage point.  There are torches being held by dark robed people in the room, as well as a host of candles lighting the stone table.

My eyes widen at the scene I see unfolding before me.  In addition to the robed figures standing in a loose circle around the raised pillar with the candles,  //Six, eight of them?  Its hard to tell from here with any certainty, there may be more just outside of where I can see.// I can also see three additional people at the raised area itself.  

I want to rush into the cave, gun in hand as I realize that the naked woman is somehow secured to the table, //Looks like chains from here,// She just lies there, unmoving, though I can hear her sobs  blending with the guttural chanting coming from the other people present.

passages.name: Open the trap door.
passages.content:
<<if $Day gte 6>><<if $Compartment is "Not Played">>I don’t know what compels me to venture back into the depths of the Church’s musty old basement, but I find myself descending back into the murky place I found the old leather-bound tome.

About half way down the ladder, one of the wooden rungs groans loudly, accompanied by the sound of splintering wood.  I can feel my foot shift slightly as the ladder seems to shift.

My heart thunders in my chest for several seconds as I wait for the ladder to collapse, but it does not do so.  Carefully, I make my way down the rest of the ladder without further incident.  

Turning my attention to the gloom around me, my eyes slowly adjust to the darkness enough that I can see my surroundings well enough to move around.  The first thing I notice is that the room is not as I last left it.  One of the bookshelves has been torn from the wall, books and trinkets strewn about the room at random.  

<<if ($PlayerPerception + $PlayerIntelligence) gte 12>>The mess makes it difficult to safely move through the room.  <<if $Flashlight is "true">>I flick on my flashlight, bathing the scene in a cone of light.  I’m careful to pay attention, taking in the details.  //There’s something behind the bookshelf.// I realize, passing the light over the dark void where the bookshelf once stood.  

I try to see further, but the darkness seems to consume the light created by my flashlight.  //I can check that in a moment.//  I think, turning my attention back to the room.  //It looks like the bookshelf was pulled over into the room,//  My light passes over the back of the bookshelf, showing splintered wood and what looks like an animal’s claw marks across its surface, //Or maybe something from the other side pushed it over.//  The thought gives me a chill.

As if to emphasize my foreboding, a salty breeze comes from the opening with enough force to make me shield my eyes.

passages.name: Ask Mr Morichi about his family.
passages.content:
<<if ($PlayerEttiquette + $PlayerCharisma) gte 14>>"I was wondering if I could ask you a few questions about your family?"  I ask tectfully.

I can see Mr Morichi's expression tighten a little bit.  He takes a long sip of his tea, then sits the cup down.  His eyes turn out the window when he begins to speak, "Truly unpleasant as much of my family history is, I'll speak of it this once in the name of budding friendship,"  He glances at me only long enough to utter a question before turning his gaze back towards the ocean, "Are you looking for specifics about my family, or is this a simple blanket inquiry?"

"Just a general question.  Curiosity mostly."

Me Morichi nods slowly, "It would seem that my family line will end with the death of my daughter."

Before I can insert a question, he raises a single finger towrds me to silence me, "My daughter is locked away in this mansion because she is a danger to herself, and the family line is sure to end with her because she is barren."

I find myself leaning towards Mr Morichi, curious. <<if $Madness gte 2>>In the back of my mind, a soft whisper becomes a momentary giggle.<<endif>>

"She is a hermaphrodite, and has proven unable to conceive.  And to my shame, the only child I have ever sired is her,"  Reaching out, he takes hold of his tea cup, sipping lightly at the bitter fluid within.

"But perhaps this is too much to burden a near stranger with?"  He does not wait for any reply from me before continuing.

<<if $TomTanya is "Played">>“Can we talk?”  I ask Tanya softly.

Seeming to sense from my tone that the subject might be delicate, I see her eyes flicker towards Arron, then drift over the few others scattered about the tavern's interior, “I don't give discounts,”  Her voice is only loud enough to carry to the nearest people in the room, and none of them seem to pay any mind.

I lean in close, my face inches from hers, “I overheard you and Aaron talking,”

She stiffens momentarily, then lets out a long breath, “Ok, yeah, I kind of guessed as much,”  Her voice is soft, barely loud enough for me to hear now, “How much did you hear?”

“Just the tail end of it,”  I admit, “But it was enough to make me ask some questions.  Questions I expect you to answer for me.”

“I can't, not right now.  I'll find you when we can speak freely.”  Tanya whispers, then leans forward to give me a firm kiss on the lips.  I have just enough time to start enjoying the kiss when then shoves me away from her, nearly knocking over the chair I hit as I stumble back, “Come back when you've got some cash!  You're not the only Joe who wants a bit of my time.”

Standing there, stunned by her actions, I'm brought back to the present by the laughter coming from some of the nearby patrons in the tavern.

With that, she storms off a few paces before leaning against the bar, ignoring me.

passages.name: Ask Mr Morichi about Azumi's Mother.
passages.content:
I am a little surprised when Mr Morichi sits down his cup and turns toward me, "Out with it,"  He says firmly.

"What?"  I begin, but he quickly cuts me off.

"I can tell from your expression that you have something unpleasant you want to say, but don't know how best to say it,"  He inserts, "Out with it,"  He repeats himself.

Taking a deep breath, I let it out slowly, "Alright, I was wondering about Azumi's mother."

<<if ($PlayerEttiquette + $PlayerCharisma) gte 13>>The look he gives me makes me wonder if I've finally gone too far with my questioning, if I'm about to be thrown out of his home and never welcomed back, "My,"  He begins, his voice hard, "Don't we have an aptitude for unpleasant conversation topics."

"I'm curious by nature, its part of what made me a good detective,"  I venture, "And when you were reluctant to talk about Azumi, and pointedly avoided talking about her mother,"   I pause, looking for the right words, "well, it piqued my interest."

Mr Morichi looks angry for another heartbeat, then he lets out a bitter laugh, "Only sound piece of advice my father ever gave me, 'Never avoid a subject if you don't want people to be curious about it.' He must have had a friend like you."

I'm almost ready to speak when he finally decides to start talking again, "Our marriage was arranged.  I, the heir to the Alhazred family line, and she was born into money. I suspect that both families were seeking power through the arrangement.  I won't clam to know her motives, but in hindsight I have a nagging suspicion that she knew exactly what she was doing."

He pours himself a fresh cup of tea, then drinks the entire cup before sitting the empty teacup back down, "At first, it was like a whirlwind romance, and then she got pregnant with Azumi.  Everything changed then, her warm personality turned cold, her generous nature becoming bitter and spiteful,"  He pauses, looking me in the eyes, "But underneath that, I realized that she was happier than

passages.name: Walk around the side of the mansion.
passages.content:
I'm just reaching the top of the bluff when I see a figure framed in a second story window.

<<if $MorichiDaughter gte 3>>Azumi looks down towards me, a sadness in her expression, though if I'm being honest with myself, most of my attention is on her naked breast in clear view of the window.

"<<print $PlayerName>>!"  She calls through the open window, the sound of rain is almost enough to drown out the sound of her melodious voice.    

"Father says I may not see you anymore,"  There's a profound sadness in her tone that nearly makes my heart ache, "Help me escape this place, take me with you, please?"

I open my mouth, prepared to say something, but close it again when I realize that any promise I make could very well be a lie, "I,"  I start, then pause for a moment, "I'll do what I can,"  Even I can hear the lack of conviction in my voice, though she seems to miss it.

<<if $Taint gte 60>>"I will come with you, and I will be a good lover for you, just please free me from my father,"  Her tone is desperate, and I can see even through the rain that she is on the verge of tears.

I ache to put my arms around her to comfort her.

<<else>>"I like you a lot," She pauses, as if she is trying to decide if she should continue, "I could learn to love you, I'd make a good wife for you, just please free me from my father,"  Her tone is desperate, and I can see even through the rain that she is on the verge of tears.

<<endif>>The gravity of her words are not lost on me, even though I have to wonder if she fully understands what she is asking of me.

I'm still looking for the words to continue our conversation when her expression turns to surprise and she quickly vanishes from view.  The pink curtain settling back in place is the only indication she was ever there.

Turning to see what caught her attention, I'm greeted by the sight of Mr Morichi's doorman standing just a few feet behind me.  He seems unconcerned by the rain falling, running in uneven paths

<<if $MotherTalk is "Played">>!!Azumi's Origins.
Mr Morichi did not hesitate to tell me that Azumi was a bit //different// but it didn't take long before I realized he was pointedly avoiding any mention of her mother.  I finally contronted him about it.

He still avoided telling me her name, but he gave me some clues I intend to follow.

It was supposed to be an arranged marriage, but before the wedding she became pregnant.  Apparently she had been nice before that, but her true colors came out after she had a bun in the oven.  Mr Morichi was short on details, but he did tell me that she was mean to him and spoke poorly of her unborn baby.  He used the term 'Evil' which is a word I suspect he does not use lightly.  It sounds like she gave Mr Morichi her baby without a second thought.  Did she do so because Azumi was a hermaphrodite, or was there some other reason?

Guess my next course of action is to ask around, //delicately//, to see if I can't learn a bit more.

passages.name: Second Segment Start.
passages.content:
Several minutes later, I'm jogging down the old forest trail, watching for the north fork of the trail.  

A gunshot, the deep boom of a rifle sounds from somewhere deeper in the frorest.

My pace slows as I listen.  A second gunshot rings out, followed this time by a scream.  

<<if $PastorConfront is "True">>//That sounded like it came from the Pastor's place.//  My pace quickens once more as I run in that direction.  I hear gunfire two more times before I reach the clearing where his house is.

//Boom!//  I look towards the house.  Vick, //Pastor Erkon// is standing over //something// wriething on the ground.  Whatever is it is a mass of tantacles and teeth, blackish blood coating the ground near it and spattered across Victor's front.  

"I don't know how, but it got past the bones.  I've never seen one of these things so big before,"  He says to me when he sees my approach.

"What the hell is is?"  I ask, my eyes scanning the treeline while keeping half an eye on the tentalce creature.

"They're in the book, you're read it right?"  The look in my eyes is all the answer he needs, "Well,"  He gestures at the thing, "What are you doing out here anyway?"

"Azumi's been taken, Mr Morichi says she'll be at the Cathedral."

I'm only a few paces away when he says, "Catch," and tosses the old Parker double barreled 20 gauge to me.  I catch it with one hand, glance at the //thing// dying on the ground, then release the break to fish the spent shell from the one barrel.  The other looks good, so I leave it.  I'm just about to close the break again when Vick tosses a small leather bag to me, "Ammo, I'll go grab my rifle."

I pause just long enough to check the bag, finding it full of additional shells.  I reload the shotgun before stuffing the bag into my jacket and pointing the shotgun towards whatever the thing is on the ground is.

A minute later, the //thing// has nearly stopped moving, and Vick returns with what looks like a hunting rifle in hand

passages.content:
I turn my eyes upward, pondering the ceiling for a moment before I finally speak, "I spoke with Azumi's mother."

The moment the words leave my mouth, I can see the way the muscles around his eyes tighten, his jaw clenching.  He takes a deep breath and lets it out slowly.  

Carefully, he sits his cup of tea down on the little table between us and turns his piercing eyes toward me.  I've stared down a lot of angry, stubborn, or just plain unpleasant men in my life, but the look on his face is enough to sit me back in my chair.  

<<if ($PlayerEttiquette + $PlayerCharisma) gte 13>>With a heavy sigh, Mr Morichi's expression falls in on itself.  Like watching a house of cards tumble and fall.  "Did you know that you can be a difficult person to like?"  His tone is flat.

"I've been told as much more times than I care to admit,"  I reply with a dry humor.

His eyes linger on me long enough that I begin to feel uncomfortable under his scrutiny.  //I wonder if this is how people I've interrogated feel?//

When I do not say anything more, Mr Morichi says with a cold voice, "Were you just seeking to provoke my anger, or are you hoping to accomplish something else with this little stunt of yours?"

I ponder his question for a couple of seconds before finally respond, "Confirmation,"  I say, "It's been a long time since I've had someone treat me well, and longer still since I've done the same for someone else.  I've pushed the bounds of hospitality nearly every time we have spoken, and this, . . . well, this is purposely pushing you toward conversation I knew you didn't want to have even before I started asking about town in the hopes of learning something.  I'd like to hear the whole story, but confirmation will be enough to quench my need for answers for now."

"At least you're honest about it,"  He mutters, his features softening slightly even though I can still see his displeasure just as clearly as before, "Yes, that vile woman was engaged to be my wife.

passages.name: overgrown park
passages.content:
Standing in the park, I let my eyes drift over the overgrown bushes and tall, dead grass.

Towards the front of the park, there are two moss covered stone benches.  Both are heavily weathered and have visible cracks in their surface wherever the stone is visible. Further into the park, I can just see a third bench, nearly hidden within a mass of vines and bushes.

At the center of the park, my eyes fall on a large fountain.  It looks like the fountain was once an impressive sight, with water flowing from pool to pool, down to a large round base.  Now, the fountain is filled with stagnant water and green sludge and spotted with bird feces.

Something primal in the back of my mind urges me to take a closer look.

There are a series of deep lines in the surface.  Part of me wants to look away, but the marks feel too familiar, like something I've seen before, a lifetime ago.

passages.name: Ask about the book from the Church basement.
passages.content:
"I wanted to ask you about something,"  I start, "You're the only person in town that I've met who seemed to know the former pastor very well, so I was wondering if you knew about anything he might have been involved in?"

Ms Sherwood looks into my eyes, as if trying to discern what I'm saying.  <<if $Madness gte 2>>//I wonder if she can see the madness in my eyes?//<<else>>//Guess I'll need to get right to the point.//<<endif>>

<<if ($PlayerEttiquette + $PlayerCharisma) gte 12>>I reach into my jacket, producing the book I found in the church basement, "I was snooping around the church a little bit,"  Her expression turns alarmed, "Dont worry, I wasn't doing anything bad.  I just wanted to see if I could find something that indicated why Pastor Erkon might have done what he did,"  The alarm in her eyes seems to fade, so I continue, "While I was looking around, I found this,"  I unfold the cloth, revealing the book within.

She looks at the book, seeming to shy subtly away from it, "I dont know what that book is, but I'm not sure I'm comfortable with it in my home."  Ms Sherwood's expression is that of someone looking into the face of evil, her features seeming drained of color.

"Please, just look.  Its obvious that you dont recognize it, but maybe you can tell me if it belonged to Pastor Erkon, or to someone else?"  I prompt.

She seems to shy away further as I open the front of the book, revealing the first of many notes within, "What about this hand writing?  It looks like someone was trying to decipher what was in this book.  Do you recognize the hand writing?"

There's a faint tremor in her voice as she answers, "That //is// his hand writing,"  She manages.

Closing the book, I quickly wrap it back up and slip it back inside of my coat, "Thank you, my next question would be, do you know what he might have been doing with that a book like this?"

Ms Sherwood shakes her head, and I can tell that's all the answer I'm likely to get from her without

passages.name: Tavern5
passages.content:
<<set $random to random(0,3)>><<if $Day gte 5>><<if $Madness gte 3>><<if $random gte 2>>Across the tavern, I can see someone sitting in the corner.  Their heavy coat is pulled tightly around himself and the collar is turned up to conceal his neck and lower face.  On his head is a broad rimmed hat.  

//Shadows, he's shrouded in them,//  The thought unsettles me, //Is //she// trying to hide him from me?//

My first impulse is to confront the man, and I set out to do exactly that.  I start to pick my way across the tavern towards him.

He half smiles at me, lifts his class in salute, then downs the remainder of its content.

"//He's with her, he's got to be,//"  I'm not even aware that I'm mumbling my thoughts out loud until several of the people around me give concerned looks in my direction.

I've crossed just over half the distance to the man when he sits his glass down, tilts his hat towards me, then stands.  His grin spreads as he takes a step back into the darker shadows where I loose sight of him.

When I arrive at the corner, I look about frantically, but only see the wall that would have been behind the man.  I spend several minutes searching the wall, looking for any sign of a trap door or other method the man may have used to escape.  Seeing none, I shiver violently, my knees going weak.  I barely manage to catch myself on the table.

Looking at the table, I can see the glass still sitting where the man had put it down.  //What the hell is happening here, happening to me?//

<<else>>My attention is drawn towards the ceiling where I can see shadows moving along the timbers overhead.  I stumble and fall as I try to make my retreat, a shriek tearing itself from my throat as I see watch in horrified fascination while the shadows seem to droop in my direction.  

A small part of my mind realizes that the people in the Barmaid's Daughter have all fallen silent, watching me as if //I// was the peculiarity worth noticing.

I start to point towar

passages.name: Run down part of town.
passages.content:
<<if $HybridFight is 1>>While I walk along the mud caked streets of the run down part of town, I hear a sound that draws my attention.  

The sound comes from inside of a house, one that look abandoned.  I can see patches of moss growing on the walls of the building, and the entire structure leans visibly.

I take a few steps towards it before I see something move through a broken window in the side of the house.  I can't see the figure clearly, but its obvious from the way it pauses at the same time I see it, that it sees me too.

A split second later, the figure bolts for the structure's door.  

Years of serving as a police officer kicks in and even before I think, I'm running around the building to try to cut the figure off before they can reach the front door.

When I round the corner, the figure is just bursting from the door.  I skid in the mud, nearly landing on my ass as I do so.  My hand sinks deep in the mud as I barely avoid my fall.  My other hand is already inside my jacket, fingers closing around the handle of my .38 Special.

"Stop!"  I shout at the figure.

[[He does nothing of the sort.]]

<<else>>This part of town may have looked like it was in poor repair from a distance, but that doesn't even begin to tell the reality of what I see.  All around me, houses that would make a slum look like a high class neighborhood are cobbled together with rotting boards and cracked windows.  

I cringe inwardly at the thought of living in a place like this.

As I walk, the street quickly deteriorates from poor repair, to a mix of mud and potholes.

In the midst of the chaotic collection of houses, there's a [[little church]].  The tall grass and thorny bushes surrounding it, along with the door hanging askew suggests that the Church hasn't seen use in a long time. 

A twisted and rusty old fence stretches from the East side of the Church, encompassing an overgrown cemetery.

<<townlink "Head into the center of town.">>

[/IF]幾次緩慢的抽插後，他的動作逐漸加快，直到每次撞擊時，他的臀部都拍打在我的屁股上，發出清晰的聲響。

沒過多久，他便進入了一種快速而規律的節奏。

這樣持續了幾分鐘，他的節奏開始變得紊亂。我幾乎沒時間意識到他快要射了，他就深深插入。一股液體的暖流突然充滿了我的體內深處。

幾秒漫長的時間後，他滿足地嘆了口氣抽身而出，「抱歉啦，」他咕噥道。

感覺到一股精液從我小穴流出，在我沉重的呼吸聲中，我能聽到他穿回褲子的聲音。

最後，我站起來，開始穿衣。他注視我的方式，讓我忍不住想稍微挑逗他一下。彎腰穿褲子時翹起屁股，在穿上襯衫前擠壓我敏感的乳房。

當我穿好衣服時，我的慾望幾乎和開始時一樣高漲。

[ELSEIF $Depravity gte 5]我張開嘴，話語就在舌尖。我開口時感到口乾舌燥，「求求你，幹我。」我對自己的話感到驚訝。

Vick嘴角勾起一抹淺笑，「乖[IF $Mutation gte 6]婊子[ELSE]女孩[/IF]，」他輕聲說，「俺有妳需要的東西。」

我瞥了一眼他的床，然後朝它走了幾步。他注視著我的動作，像掠食者盯著獵物。

當我走到床邊，我開始用顫抖的手脫衣。當我完全赤裸後，我坐在床沿，向後躺下，抬頭望著他。我的心跳加速，知道接下來會發生什麼。

此時，他也已赤裸，他的陰莖腫脹堅硬。我毫不掩飾地對它可觀的尺寸目瞪口呆。[IF $Mutation gte 5]他以毫不掩飾的厭惡回望著我。[/IF]

他跪在我的雙腿之間，他悸動的肉棒直指我濕漉漉的私處。

我微微顫抖，壓抑著我的慾望。當我看到並感覺到他的龜頭擦過我新生的陰唇褶皺時，我猛地向後縮。他的手牢牢抓住我的臀部，我感到他將我拉回床邊。

[IF $Cherry is "True"]當他的陰莖抵開我的陰唇，開始滑入我處女的穴口時，我全身緊繃。他以近乎折磨的緩慢速度，將他的龜頭開始滑入我體內。

我倒抽一口氣，發現自己無法呼吸。我不確定這是出於期待、恐懼，還是兩者兼有。

我感到一股不舒服的壓力，陰道內不完全是疼痛。隨著一頂，當Vick將整根陰莖連同睪丸都深深埋入我體內時，那不適變成了尖銳但短暫的痛楚。

有好幾秒鐘，他深深地停留在我的體內，然後才抽回並再次插入。幾秒之內，他便開始進入穩定的節奏，認真地幹起我來。

[ELSE]他只花了一瞬間就對準了我飢渴的穴口。輕輕一頂，他向前推進，他的陰莖緩緩滑入我的小穴。

Vick向後抽離，然後向前插入，開始幹我。

[/IF]幾次緩慢的抽插後，他的動作逐漸加快，直到每次撞擊時，他的臀部都拍打在我的屁股上，發出清晰的聲響。

沒過多久，他便進入了一種快速而規律的節奏。

這樣持續了幾分鐘，他的節奏開始變得紊亂。我幾乎沒時間意識到他快要射了，他就深深插入。一股液體的暖流突然充滿了我的體內深處。

幾秒漫長的時間後，他滿足地嘆了口氣抽身而出，「抱歉啦，」他咕噥道。

感覺到一股精液從我小穴流出，在我沉重的呼吸聲中，我能聽到他穿回褲子的聲音。

最後，

passages.name: Continue towards the Log Cabin.
passages.content:
<<if $PastorConfront is "True">>Nearing the clearing to Vick's home, my pace slows as I think of the last meeting I had with him.  

//I'm still not sure if I should meet him in the morning or not.  He's clearly unhinged.//

With that thought in mind, I decide that now might not be the best time to visit the former pastor.

[[Head back to the trail.|Take the West fork of the trail.]]<<else>><<if $babyAzumi is "Played" && $Cabin is "Played">>As I approach Vick's //Pastor Erkon's// cabin, I can feel my pace slowing.  Every step draws me closer to the inevitable meeting with a man who by all acounts is dead.

//I wonder what this means?  How does this all fit into what is happening here in Wanton Cove?//

I feel a sense of trepedation, though the feeling is unfocused, like something terrible is about to be revealed.  I've barely started to raise my hand to knock on the door when I hear it being unlatched from the other side.

When the door swings open, I see Vick look at me with his too wide eye, then past me.  He shuffles from side to side as his head moves, trying to see everywhere at once.

Suddenly, he reaches out, grabbing me by the shoulders and pulling me into his cabin before slamming the door in my wake.  His voice comes out as a hiss, "Sheh be cloose,"  When I look him in the eyes, he looks ashen, terrified to the core, "Sheh commen oon."

Then his expression changes, becoming tranquil like a veil is being lifted.  The sight is every bit as troubling as the madness I am used to seeing in his eyes.

I take several moments to consider my options, whether I should talk with her normally, or if I should ask the question that burns within me to ask.

[[What's your story?]]

[[What can you tell me about Wanton Cove?]]<<if $RuinsOpen is "Played">>

[[Ask Vick about the Ruins.]]<</if>>

[[Who is SHE?]]

[[Do you know anything about a guy named Tom?]]<<if $Pastor is "Played">>

[[Ask Vick about the Pastor.]]<</if>><<if $Taint gte 95>>

passages.name: little church
passages.content:
<<set $random to random(1, 2)>>I turn my eyes upon the old church, I take note of the broken cross perched at the peak of the steeple, looking like an upside down "L" pointing to the West.

The stained glass windows are streaked with years of filth and browning moss, the images they depict no longer visible from the outside of the Church.

I stop at the bottom of the stairs, looking at the two small gargoyles perched to either side of the Church's door like something pulled from a madman's worst nightmares.

A chill goes up my spine at the sight of the stone figures.  Standing barely two feet tall, I find the two statues to be more than a little unsettling.  

The first is a squat, hunched figure.  Wings like a bat's sprout from it's back and are spread wide as if the creature is about to take flight.  It has an octopus-like head, its cold, dead eyes seem to stare at me.  There's something profoundly troubling about that gaze, as if the creature is looking at me the way a child might look at an insect that its about to step on.  Beneath the figures' eyes is a mass of tentacles hanging down in such a way that they drape over most of it's chest.  As my eyes continue downward, I can see a dog-like cock standing erect between its legs, its tapered length pointed straight at me.  I feel an unsettling cold seep into my bones at the very sight of the statue, and in spite of the statue's small size, I feel infinitesimally small looking at it.

Every bit as disquieting, and of considerably more interest is the second statue.  This statue is standing proudly, its feminine body naked, and perfect in every way.  Between its legs dangles a long thick cock, resembling that of a horse, reaching just past its knees.  I almost can't force myself to look upwards at the statue's face, because I already know what I'm going to see.  Crowned in a halo of long twisted hair, //Its not really hair is it?  It looks more like tentacles,// my eyes come to rest on a featureless face t
passages.content_zh:
我將目光投向那座老教堂，注意到尖頂頂端歪斜的十字架，像個倒過來的「L」字指向西方。

彩繪玻璃窗上佈滿經年累月的污垢與褐化苔蘚，從教堂外頭已看不清它們描繪的圖像。

我在階梯底部停下腳步，望著教堂大門兩側各蹲踞著一隻小石像鬼，那模樣簡直像是從瘋子最深的噩夢裡扯出來的東西。

一見到這些石像，寒意便竄上我的脊樑。這兩尊雕像還不到兩英尺高，卻讓我感到極度不安。

第一尊是個蹲伏駝背的身影。蝙蝠般的翅膀從牠背後展開，彷彿隨時準備騰空飛起。牠長著章魚似的頭部，那雙冰冷死寂的眼睛似乎正盯著我。那目光中有種深沉的惡意，就像孩童盯著一隻即將被踩死的昆蟲。眼睛下方垂掛著一團觸手，覆蓋了牠大半胸膛。當我視線繼續下移，看見牠兩腿間挺立著一根狗似的陰莖，錐形的尖端直指向我。光是看著這尊雕像，一股令人不安的寒意便滲入我的骨髓；儘管雕像體型不大，我卻感覺自己在它面前渺小得微不足道。

同樣令人不安、卻更引人注目的是第二尊雕像。這雕像昂然站立，女性身軀赤裸，每一處曲線都完美無瑕。兩腿間垂著一根粗長的陰莖，宛如馬匹般尺寸，長度直抵膝蓋下方。我幾乎不敢抬頭看雕像的臉，因為我早已知道會看見什麼——頂著一圈扭曲長髮的光環（那真的是頭髮嗎？看起來更像觸手），我的視線最終落在那張沒有五官的面孔上，使我血管裡的血液幾乎凍結。

[IF $Day is 5 && $Drunk is "Not Played" && $random is 1]我幾乎走到教堂正前方時，聽見裡面傳來聲響。一聲悶響，像是屍體被扔進溝裡的聲音。

我的手滑進夾克內側，讓襯衫前襟暴露在暴雨中。冰涼的雨滴幾乎瞬間浸透我的襯衫，帶來刺骨寒意。[IF $Taint gte 55]我的胸部——想到這裡我不禁皺眉，是胸膛，我對自己說謊——似乎比從前對寒冷更敏感了。[/IF]

手中握著點三八特製手槍沉甸甸的安心重量，我悄悄靠近教堂前門，正好看見一個身穿深棕色厚重雨衣、頭戴鬆垮寬邊帽的男人踉蹌地從門口跌出來。

我瞬間意識到這男人沒什麼威脅性，他喝醉了，我暗忖——但這點時間已足夠讓我的點三八滑出槍套、完全脫離夾克遮掩。

我迅速將武器收回槍套，清了清嗓子引起男人注意。他還沒完全抬起頭，我便問道：「[介意告訴我你在這裡做什麼嗎？]」

[ELSE][IF $FirstStatue is "Not Played"]

我花了整整好幾秒才壓下看見這兩尊怪物雕像時湧起的恐慌浪潮。

當我幾乎讓心跳恢復正常時，那尊女性雕像周圍的陰影突然如觸手群般蠕動，朝我伸來。

我踉蹌後退，猛眨眼睛，等到再度睜眼時，那又只是一片陰影了。

現在連我的眼睛都在耍我。

我試著深呼吸以控制緊繃的神經，沉重的喘息中仍帶著一絲顫抖。

我真的需要喝一杯，再好好睡一覺。一抹恍惚的苦笑浮上嘴角——但最主要還是需要喝一杯。

[/IF][IF $ChurchBroken is "Not Played"]我再次鼓起勇氣，試探性地踩上一級腐朽的木階。腳下的木板像半乾海綿般下陷，在我鞋底發出濕軟的吱嘎聲。將重心移到那隻腳上時，我幾乎驚訝這階梯竟能承受我的體重。每一級階梯感覺只比前一級稍微結實一點，直到我終於感覺到腳跟下踩到較堅實的東西。

瞥向教堂，我強迫自己不去看兩側的雕像，轉而將注意力集中在大門本身。[/IF]

[IF $ChurchDoor is "Played"]門已成了碎裂的廢墟，碎片散落在教堂前。無論闖入者是什麼，都擁有驚人的力氣。

這教堂已閒置很久。在我開始鎮上打聽消息後才有人闖入，巧合得令人起疑。

[ELSE][IF $ChurchBroken is "Played"]
[ELSE][IF ($PlayerStrength) gte 7][[嘗試強行推開門。|Force1][$ChurchBroken to "Played"]]
[ELSE][嘗試強行推開門。]
[/IF] [IF ($PlayerSecurity + $PlayerIntelligence) gte 10][[嘗試撬開門鎖。|Jimmy1][$ChurchBroken to "Played"]]
[ELSE][嘗試撬開門鎖。]
[/IF][/IF][IF $ChurchBroken is "Not Played" and $ChurchAlternate is true]我開始尋找進入教堂的方法。幾乎繞到教堂後側時，發現一扇未上鎖的窗戶，可以強行推開。
[ELSEIF $ChurchWindow is true]
[/IF][IF $ChurchBroken is "Not Played" && $Day is 3]望向教堂，我立刻察覺不對勁。原本牢固的大門像是被攻城槌撞破般碎裂，木屑散落在教堂前空地上，連門框在原先裝設門扇的位置也都破裂變形。

[/IF] [/

passages.name: AzumiFinal
passages.content:
One Week Later

Sitting at the desk, I turn another page in the old leatherbound tome, . . . not the same one I had found in the basement of the old Church, but one every bit as horrible in its own way.

Several similar books are stacked to one side of the desk.

I sigh heavily, tired in a way that lack of sleep could never account for.  Rubbing my eyes, I glance toward the bed where Azumi is sleeping, //At least she can sleep now, I thought the screaming would never stop for her.  I hope that the nightmares pass as well.//

Over the last week, her body had healed from the terrible ritual in that //other// place.  But something inside of her had changed.  Paranoia, fear, and nightmares now made up her world, punctuated by an aggressive sexual need.

Naked on the covers, her wounds are scabbed in places, but mostly the patterns are clear across her skin, painted in the deep red of fresh scars.

The soft sound of the door unlocking shifts my attention.  When the door swings open, Mr Morichi steps inside holding a tray that contains a pot of tea, a bowl of sugar, and two small ornate teacups.  I quickly stand to take the tray from him.  By the time I have sat the tray on an empty bit of desk, he's closed the door behind himself.

I catch him looking at his daughter, the look on his face bordering on despair, "How has she been?"  He asks softly, not bothering to look my way.

"Unchanged, she had another nightmare a little while ago, but I held her for a little bit and she went back to sleep."

Mr Morichi offers a shallow nod of acknowledgment, then turns his attention to me.

"How about you, how are you holding up?"  He makes his way to the desk, glancing at the open book I had just been trying to decipher, then pouring two cups of tea.  He spoons a single large spoon full of sugar into one and stirs it before handing the cup to me.

"Thank you,"  I say before raising the cup to my lips.  The bitter fluid makes me grimace, but I take a second sip b
passages.content_zh:
一週後

坐在書桌前，我翻開那本舊皮革裝訂典籍的另一頁……這並非我在老教堂地下室找到的那本，但其內容的恐怖程度卻毫不遜色。

幾本相似的書籍堆疊在書桌一側。

我沉重地嘆了口氣，這種疲憊遠非睡眠不足所能解釋。揉著眼睛，我瞥向床上熟睡的Azumi。至少她現在能睡著了，我原以為她的尖叫永遠不會停止。但願噩夢也能隨之消散。

過去一週裡，她在那個異界經歷可怕儀式所造成的身體創傷已逐漸癒合。但她內在的某些部分已然改變。偏執、恐懼與噩夢構成了她的世界，其間更夾雜著一種侵略性的性需求。

她赤裸躺在被單上，部分傷口已結痂，但皮膚上大多清晰呈現著深紅色新鮮疤痕構成的圖案。

門鎖輕微的開啟聲轉移了我的注意力。門扉敞開時，Morichi先生端著托盤步入房間，盤上有一壺茶、一碗糖和兩只精緻的小茶杯。我迅速起身接過托盤。剛將托盤放在書桌空處，他已隨手關上了門。

我瞥見他正注視著女兒，臉上神情近乎絕望。「她狀況如何？」他輕聲問道，並未轉頭看我。

「沒有變化。不久前她又做了噩夢，但我抱著她安撫了一會兒，她就又睡著了。」

Morichi先生微微點頭示意明白，隨即將注意力轉向我。

「那你呢？你還撐得住嗎？」他走向書桌，瞥了一眼我剛才試圖解讀的攤開書籍，然後倒了兩杯茶。他在其中一杯舀了滿滿一大匙糖，攪拌後遞給我。

「謝謝。」我說著將茶杯舉到唇邊。苦澀的液體讓我皺眉，但我還是喝了第二口才放下杯子。

「有任何進展嗎？」他指向攤開的書本。

未及回答，Azumi的哭喊聲從房間另一頭傳來——幾乎是尖叫。我衝到她身邊，Morichi先生僅落後幾步。

她以一種令人心悸的眼神瞪視我片刻，才認出我是誰。「$PlayerName，」她雙臂緊緊環抱住我，力道之大令我的肋骨發疼，「求求你不要讓她帶走我，她來找我了，她需要我，求你不要讓她得到我。」Azumi在我耳邊喃喃低語，話語如無盡溪流般不斷從她唇間湧出。

我開始意識到Morichi先生站得離我們多近。轉頭望向他，他臉上神情掙扎，顯然不確定該加入我們這微小時刻，抑或只是旁觀。「Azumi，我珍貴的女兒，我們都在這裡陪著你。」他的聲音透著悲傷。

Azumi在我懷中微微轉身，仰視她的父親：「爸爸？」她的語氣充滿不確定，彷彿無法確信自己真的見到了他。

Morichi先生靠近一步，伸手輕撫她的臉頰：「是的，Azumi。」他擠出一絲微弱的笑容。

她唇邊初現笑意，隨即神情驟轉驚恐。她以巨大力量推開我，尖叫著將我撞下床鋪。我順著她的目光看向房間裡那片吸引她注意的空無之處，再轉回她身上。

床上，她蜷縮成球狀，雙腿緊貼胸口，渾身顫抖。

當我看見她的疤痕再次發光時，不禁遲疑。「她想出來，她必須出來，她需要利用我。」她的尖叫隨著每個字愈發尖銳，「使者必須降臨！」

我再次回到床邊，雙臂環抱住她，緊緊擁著，同時試圖忽略她疤痕散發的異界光芒。

當她再次開口時，聲音變得異常詭異，令我毛骨悚然：「這全是你的錯，父親。」這句話自一週前我將流血昏迷的她帶回宅邸以來，已聽過不下數十次。

前一秒她還在我懷中緊繃顫抖，下一秒便眼球上翻，癱軟無力。

我極度謹慎地將她安置在床上，為她蓋上毯子——儘管明知幾分鐘後她又會踢開。

確認她已熟睡安穩後，我轉向Morichi先生：「我想現在你該告訴我，為何她始終將這一切歸咎於你。」

我從床邊起身走向窗戶，眺望萬頓灣的景色。並非第一次，我看見有人站在樹林邊緣，從林線處監視著這座宅邸。

我迅速退離窗邊，看向Morichi先生。

passages.name: Cobb Encounter
passages.content:
<<if $CobbIntimidate is "Played">><<if ($PlayerStrength + $PlayerIntimidation) gte 12>><<if ($PlayerStrength + $PlayerBrawl) gte 13>>I quickly step into his swing, catching his wrist with one hand and driving my fist into his belly with the other.  The piece of wood falls from his hands.

Shoving him, I push him solidly against the side of the nearest house, "Want to tell me what that was about?"  I ask, my tone angry.

"Fuck you,"  He hisses, then spits in my face.

The moment I move to wipe the spittle away, Cobb suddenly thrashes wildly, freeing himself from my grasp and starting to run as fast as his feet can carry him.

I take off running after him, but the man is //fast//, quickly out pacing me.

Weaving around buildings, its not long before I loose track of him.

Cursing under my breath, I look around for any further sign of him, but come up empty.

<<elseif ($PlayerStrength + $PlayerBrawl) gte 9>>He swings in a downward arc, but I'm just barely quicker.  The piece of wood snags my jacket but does not hurt me.

Backpeddaling, I quickly move out of his reach, my hand instinctively reaching for my .38 Special.

"You need to leave this place,"  He says, holding the stick out in front of himself as if that somehow makes his point, extending it towards me, "Just leave."

For several seconds, he glares angrily at me, as if prepared to make some further point, then he drops the stick and starts to run away.

//What the hell was that?//  I wonder, for a moment thinking about trying to catch him, but then deciding against it.

<<else>>He swings in a downward arc, the piece of wood catching me in the shoulder with enough force to knock me sprawling to the ground.  

When I look up, its just in time for him to hit me in the ribs with his stick.

After a few seconds of not being hit again, I cautiously glance past my arm.  

He's standing beside the building he had originally rushed me from, "You dont have a fucking clue what you've gotten y
passages.content_zh:
[IF $CobbIntimidate is "Played"][IF ($PlayerStrength + $PlayerIntimidation) gte 12][IF ($PlayerStrength + $PlayerBrawl) gte 13]我迅速踏進他的揮擊範圍，一手抓住他的手腕，另一手猛力將拳頭砸進他的腹部。那截木棍從他手中掉落。

我猛力一推，將他結實地撞在最近一棟房子的側牆上，「想告訴我這是怎麼回事嗎？」我問道，語氣帶著怒意。

「去你的，」他嘶聲道，接著朝我臉上吐了口唾沫。

就在我抬手要抹去唾液的瞬間，Cobb突然猛烈掙扎，掙脫了我的箝制，拔腿狂奔。

我立刻追了上去，但這傢伙速度很快，迅速拉開了與我的距離。

他在建築物間穿梭，沒多久我就失去了他的蹤跡。

我低聲咒罵，環顧四周尋找他的任何跡象，但一無所獲。

[ELSEIF ($PlayerStrength + $PlayerBrawl) gte 9]他揮舞木棍劃出一道下劈弧線，但我勉強更快一步。木棍勾住了我的夾克，但沒有傷到我。

我向後急退，迅速退到他攻擊範圍外，手本能地伸向我的.38特製手槍。

「你需要離開這個地方，」他說，將木棍舉在身前，彷彿這樣就能強調他的話，並將棍子指向我，「快走。」

有好幾秒鐘，他憤怒地瞪著我，像是準備要進一步表明立場，然後他丟下木棍，轉身跑走了。

這他媽到底是怎麼回事？我納悶著，一時考慮是否要追上他，但隨即打消了念頭。

[ELSE]他揮舞木棍劃出一道下劈弧線，那截木棍重重擊中我的肩膀，力道之大將我打倒在地。

當我抬頭時，正好來得及看見他用棍子擊中我的肋骨。

過了幾秒沒有再遭受攻擊，我謹慎地從手臂上方瞥去。

他站在最初衝向我時的那棟建築旁，「你他媽根本不知道你捲進了什麼事，」他憤怒地嘶聲道，然後扔下木棍，消失在建築物後方。

等我站起來，意識到自己沒有受重傷時，他早已不見蹤影。

[/IF][ELSE][IF ($PlayerStrength + $PlayerBrawl) gte 13]我迅速踏進他的揮擊範圍，一手抓住他的手腕，另一手猛力將拳頭砸進他的腹部。那截木棍從他手中掉落。

我猛力一推，將他結實地撞在最近一棟房子的側牆上，「想告訴我這是怎麼回事嗎？」我問道，語氣帶著怒意。

「你惹上大麻煩了，你在插手你不懂的事情，」他嘶聲道。

我稍微鬆開了對他的箝制，就在那一刻，他猛地向一側掙扎，掙脫了我的控制，開始逃跑。

我立刻追了上去，但這傢伙速度很快，迅速拉開了與我的距離。

他在建築物間穿梭，沒多久我就失去了他的蹤跡。

我低聲咒罵，環顧四周尋找他的任何跡象，但一無所獲。

[ELSEIF ($PlayerStrength + $PlayerBrawl) gte 9]他揮舞木棍劃出一道下劈弧線，但我勉強更快一步。木棍勾住了我的夾克，但沒有傷到我。

我向後急退，迅速退到他攻擊範圍外，手本能地伸向我的.38特製手槍。

「我真的不想傷害你，但你真的需要離開這個地方，」他說，將木棍舉在身前，彷彿這樣就能強調他的話，並將棍子指向我，「快走。」

有好幾秒鐘，他憤怒地瞪著我，像是準備要進一步表明立場，然後他丟下木棍，轉身跑走了。

這他媽到底是怎麼回事？我納悶著，一時考慮是否要追上他，但隨即打消了念頭。

[ELSE]他揮舞木棍劃出一道下劈弧線，那截木棍重重擊中我的肩膀，力道之大將我打倒在地。

我盡可能護住自己，看見他站在我上方，木棍仍高舉過頭，彷彿準備將我打到屈服為止。

「喂，我們不能談談嗎？」我謹慎地問道。

「談？像你前幾天在碼頭上那樣談嗎？去你的，」他將木棍朝我扔來，然後轉身走開。

我內心有一部分想衝上去追他，但肩膀的疼痛讓我冷靜下來。等我重新站穩時，Cobb已經不見了。[/IF][/IF][/IF][IF $CobbStreetwise is "Played"]「嘿，」我輕聲打招呼道。

Cobb的眼睛四處掃視，彷彿他認為我們正被監視著，「老兄，你在這兒捅了馬蜂窩了，」這位老水手說道，聲音帶著輕微的顫抖，「你當初就該聽我的，直接上船離開。」

他的頭猛地轉向一側，像是看到了什麼。我順著他的目光望去，那個方向並無任何值得注意的東西，便將注意力轉回他身上。他那佈滿血絲的雙眼已再次看向我，「我只是來找個人。Tom，我一個朋友的兒子。找到他我就走。」

Cobb似乎稍微鬆了口氣，「別說我沒警告過你，」他伸出手，像是要安撫我，但又停了下來，畏縮地避開我，「如果你不聽勸，那我也幫不了你。離我他媽的遠一點，我不想被你掀起的任何風波捲進去。」

他語氣中冰冷的決絕讓我頓住，不知該如何回應。

我看著他轉身，開始在房屋之間走遠。

[/IF][繼續。]

passages.content:
<<if $Depravity lte 5>>My head snaps around when I hear a soft growl just behind me.  What I see is a large dog, a mut, its hair matted to its body from all the rain.  It bares its teeth at me and its growl seems to deepen.

I take a cautious step away from the animal, my hand reaching inside my jacket to find the comforting weight of my .38 Special.

Another step away from the animal, and its grown begins to soften.  I continue to move away from the animal, never letting my eyes leave it. 

Eventually, the animal turns, a glint of red beneath it that can only be its erect psnit, before it runs out of sight between two delapidated buildings.

The heavy rain falling fills the area with a dull roar.  Water flows off of dilapidated rooftops to splash into muddy ditches.  

As I move in the street, the mud clings to my boots making them feel heavy and slow.

Standing in the mud caked road, the sounds of your fight temporarily drown out the sound of the wind and rain.

passages.name: Ask about Pastor Erkon
passages.content:
"Aaron mentioned something about Wanton Cove's former pastor?"  I begin, "I was wondering if you could tell me a little bit about him?"

<<if $Madness gte 3>>//I don't know why I bother asking.  The people here in Wanton Cove, nearly every one of them, have only misled and tried to confuse me.//

I open my mouth to say something, but before I do, she begins to speak.<<elseif $Madness is 2>>

<<elseif $Madness is 1>>//Will it even matter if I can learn anything from Nadia?  With //her// out there, it may be too late to make any difference here in Wanton Cove anyway.<<else>><<endif>>

<<if ($PlayerPerception + $PlayerIntelligence) gte 13>>Fear, like a cornered animal, flickers across Nadia's features before she starts to answer, "Not much to tell really.  I knew him from when he'd come into my store, but we never talked much."  She gives a little shrug, "He,"  She pauses, her expression showing concern for a second before she continues, "Was a good man, and good looking as well."  A slight smile touches her lips, but it does not touch her eyes, "I heard Ol' Jackobson saw him up on the bluff.  He seemed to think he jumped to his death, but if you ask me, muddy as that bluff is, its more likely he slipped."

I cant help but think her comments sounded almost rehersed, "That's all?"  I prod.

"Yeah,"  She says in a small voice, not meeting my gaze.  Then her expression changes into a small smile suddenly, her eyes drifting up my body in a very un-lady like way, "So, you going to buy something, or-?"

"Hold on a second,"  I stop her, my voice as firm as I can make it.<<if ($PlayerIntimidation + $PlayerStrength) gte 9>>

Nadia's expression shifts to just a hint shy of terror, "I,"  She swallows audibly, "//Please,// that's all I know."

I've seen that look a number of times in my life.  Always when sitting across the table from someone who knows more than they are telling, but are far more scared of whoever they are protecting than they are for me.  I figh
passages.content_zh:
「Aaron 提到了萬頓灣前任牧師的一些事？」我開口問道，「我在想，你能不能告訴我一些關於他的事？」

[IF $Madness gte 3]我不知道自己為何還要費心詢問。萬頓灣這裡的人，幾乎每一個，都只會誤導我、試圖混淆我。

我張口想說些什麼，但在我開口之前，她開始說話了。[ELSEIF $Madness is 2]

[ELSEIF $Madness is 1]就算能從 Nadia 那裡問出什麼，還重要嗎？有「她」在外面，萬頓灣這裡可能已經做什麼都來不及了。[ELSE][/IF]

[IF ($PlayerPerception + $PlayerIntimidation) gte 13]恐懼，像隻被困的動物，在 Nadia 臉上閃過，然後她才開始回答：「真的沒什麼好說的。他來我店裡時我認識他的，但我們沒怎麼聊過。」她微微聳肩，「他，」她停頓了一下，表情瞬間顯露出擔憂，然後才繼續說：「是個好人，長得也好看。」一抹淺笑掠過她的嘴唇，但並未觸及她的眼睛，「我聽說老 Jackobson 在懸崖上看到他了。他似乎認為他是跳崖自盡的，但依我看，懸崖那麼泥濘，更可能是他失足滑落。」

我忍不住覺得她的說詞聽起來幾乎像排練過，「就這樣？」我追問。

「是啊，」她小聲地說，沒有正視我的目光。然後她的表情突然轉為一抹淺笑，眼睛以一種非常不淑女的方式上下打量著我的身體，「所以，你要買點東西，還是——？」

「等一下，」我打斷她，盡可能讓聲音聽起來堅定。[IF ($PlayerIntimidation + $PlayerStrength) gte 9]

Nadia 的表情轉變為近乎恐懼，「我，」她明顯地吞了口口水，「拜託，我真的只知道這些了。」

我這輩子見過那種表情很多次。總是當我坐在桌子對面，面對那些知道得比說出來的更多，但他們更害怕他們所保護的人，而不是害怕我。我壓下放棄追問、去別處尋找答案的衝動，選擇繼續施壓：「你真的不能告訴我更多了嗎？」

Nadia 搖搖頭，眼睛瞥向門口，彷彿在尋找出路。她眼眶泛淚，幾乎要哭出來了。

「是有人殺了 Pastor Erkon 嗎？」我輕聲問道，試圖安撫她，「你可以告訴我，我可以保護你。」

「不，」一滴淚水湧出並滑落，沿著她左側鼻樑流下。她迅速抬手擦掉，同時抹乾眼睛，「你可能以為你能，但你做不到。」她聲音裡冰冷的確信感讓我頸後的寒毛直豎。

我深吸一口氣，緩緩吐出，點了點頭，「考慮一下吧。如果你改變主意，可以來找我談，好嗎？」

她點點頭，但我從她眼中那驚魂未定的神情可以看出，我大概無法從她這裡得知更多關於這件事的訊息了。[ELSE]

我這輩子見過那種表情很多次。總是當我坐在桌子對面，面對那些知道得比說出來的更多，但他們更害怕他們所保護的人，而不是害怕我。我輕輕嘆了口氣，意識到我無法從她這裡問出更多了。

她點點頭，但我從她眼中那驚魂未定的神情可以看出，我大概無法從她這裡得知更多關於這件事的訊息了。[/IF]

passages.name: Ask about Mr Morichi's daughter.
passages.content:
"I know this is a delicate subject, but my curiosity is getting the best of me,"  I say, watching Mr Morichi out of the corner of my eye, "Mind, if you dont want to answer, you can tell me 'no.'"

Mr Morichi sits down his cup of tea, looking towards me.  His expression is a mixture of amusement and curiosity, "Makes me want to say 'no' already, just to head off whatever unpleasantness you're prepared to unleash upon me,"  Motioning with his hand to continue, "Please, ask."

Pausing for the briefest of moments, I come straight out with it, "I was wondering about your daughter."

I can see his expression darken almost instantly, though it is difficult to tell if its in anger or sadness.

<<if ($PlayerEttiquette + $PlayerCharisma) gte 12>><<if $MorichiDaughter is 3>>You've met her,"  His voice is little more than a whisper, "And I have nothing to add on the subject."

I can tell from his tone that he will not be saying more on the subject.

"It is,"  Mr Morichi says stiffly, his expression still unhappy.

"You also said that she is a danger to herself."

"True,"  He answers with only the one word.

Taking a deep breath and letting it out slowly, "If you want me to change the subject, I can."

passages.name: Special Thanks
passages.content:
<<back>>

I want to thank all the people who have been such a help and inspiration to me as I've worked on this, and other projects.

Thank you to all my fans.  Without you, I'd quickly lose all motivation and will to work on this kind of project.  

Thank you to everyone over in the twine help thread on TFGamesSite.com  You are all very willing to help, while asking nothing in return.  You guys are absolutely wonderful.

Thank you to AngelofRevelation from TFGamesSite.com, and a personal friend.  While you don't know much more than me when it comes to coding a game like this, you're always willing to take a look over my shoulder and help me to fix problems that crop up, or give me a second opinion on a chunk of code so that I know I'm doing it right.  You played a big part in getting the character creator in place, I doubt I would have done it without you.

A very special thanks to TheMadExile from TFGamesSite.com.  You have always shown an amazing amount of patience in explaining how things work and helping me with the multitude of problems and questions that I've brought to your attention.  When I struggle with something, you're always there to help set me straight, often going so far as to write entire chunks of code for me.  Without you, I'd have likely given up game making within a week of the time I first started.  You have been an incredible help every step of the way.  Thank you!

Thanks to Patchwolf for helping out with testing for a while, and for introducing me to Trello, which has proven to be a very useful tool for brainstorming, planning, storyboarding and tracking progress

Thank you to Sensate and Rfpnj from TFGamesSite (Along with a couple of other people who have come and gone along the way) for grinding through frequent test builds, helping me run down and fix countless bugs, and generally acting as a sounding board on different issues.  Not only have you guys made this game much more playable for those trying to enjoy this game, bu

passages.name: StreetFight
passages.content:
My opponent looks <<if $enemyHP gte 5>>powerful<<elseif $enemyHP gte 4>>tough.  <<elseif $enemyHP gte 3>>healthy.  <<elseif $enemyHP gte 2>>badly hurt.  <<else>>nearly dead.<</if>>

The street looks abandoned, almost as if only my assailant and myself remain in this God forsaken town.  The soft creaks of store signs and the slashing of puddles play like a soft background to the more immediate sounds of our fight.

/%Player's actions%/
<<if $playerAction is "gun">>\s
   <<if $Ammo gte 1>>/%Assuming enemies have an ammunition count as well. If not, "$ammo" would be shorter%/\s
   <<set $Hit = $PlayerFirearms + random(1,20)>>\s
      <<if $Hit gt $enemyDefense>>\s
	  <<set $damage = random(2,4)>>
		You deal <<print $damage>> to the enemy! \s
      <<set $enemyHP = $enemyHP - $damage>>/%You didn't specify the damage formula, so I'm using this. =^-^=%/\s
         <<if $enemyHP gte 1>>\s
		Your assailant is still on their feet. <<set $Ammo -= 1>>
         <<else>>\s
		Your assailant falls to the ground, dead.<<set $Ammo -= 1>>
         <</if>>\s
      <<else>>\s
		Your attack misses.<<set $Ammo -= 1>>
      <</if>>\s
   <<else>>\s
	You have no ammo!
   <</if>>\s
<<elseif $playerAction is "fists">>\s
	You throw a punch at your opponent.
	<<set $Hit = $PlayerBrawling + random(1,20)>>\s
		<<if $Hit gt $enemyDefense>>\s
		<<set $damage = random(1,2)>>
		You deal <<print $damage>> to the enemy! \s
		<<set $enemyHP = $enemyHP - $damage>>\s
			<<if $enemyHP gte 1>>\s
			Your assailant is still on their feet.
			<<else>>\s
			Your assailant falls to the ground, dead.
			<</if>>\s
		<<else>>\s
		Your attack misses.
		<</if>>\s
<<elseif $playerAction is "use">>
Working quickly with your First Aid Kit, you tend the worst of your wounds.
<<elseif $Flee is "true">> You turn to run.
<</if>>\s

/%Enemy's actions%/
<<if $enemyHP gte 3>>
	<<if $enemyWeapon is "gun">>\s
	The enemy fires their gun!

passages.name: Explore the opening.
passages.content:
<<if $Day gte 8 && $Compartment is "Occupied?">>Entering the tunnel beneath the old Church, I listen.   I can hear something down the tunnel, but I am not sure what, the acoustics of the tunnel distorting the sound.

"Cobb?"  I call even though its unlikely the sound will carry to him if he's hiding in the chamber down the tunnel.  

//I wonder if she is down here?  Maybe I can finally learn something I can use?//

Shining my flashlight ahead of myself, I continue to walk until I near my destination.

I can hear noise just a short distance ahead, like a something large moving about accompanied by the sound of splashing water.  A wanton moan carries over all of it.

When the chamber finally comes into view, my flashlight catches the water, reflecting around the room in an effect similar to if I had pointed it a prism.  

In the split second that the room is illuminated, I see that I am far from alone.  Several sets of too large eyes look at me.  Most of them are cold stares, <<if $DeepFight is "Played">>three Deep Ones all grouped together around a fourth figure.  <<else>>three hideous creatures all grouped around a fourth figure.<</if>>

The fourth figure is smaller than the others, female, and still wearing the tattered remains of Cobb's clothes.  //Cobb?//  The creature directly behind her is half in the water, with Cobb bent over the side of the pool, its movements do not end even though it looks directly at you.

The second one stands just outside of the pool, its cock in Cobb's mouth while the final member of the group simply stands nearby, clearly aroused, but otherwise unengaged.

All the while, Cobb looks at me, her eyes accusing me. 

I freeze in place, trying to think my way through this situation.  //I need to get her away from them.//  

My hand slowly reaches beneath my jacket, my fingertips coming into contact with the handle of my .38 Special.  The three //things// surrounding Cobb seem unconcerned.

When my fingers begin to cu

passages.name: HighTaintEndTanyaFinal
passages.content:
One month later.

//The radio plays loudly as its owner navigates the vehicle toward his destination.

//Fear still grips the region as the Patchwork Murderer is still at large,// The voice on the radio speaks quickly, just a little static coming from the speakers, //as a twelfth mass murder was reported.  So far police have been able to identify nearly thirty of the victims from body parts found across eight crime scenes.  The police so far either do now know, or are not reporting on the total number of victims across the crime scenes.  Detective Peter Duntel has been quoted as saying //"We're following every possible lead and working hard to identify the victims.  This has some striking parallels to something that happened a few years back and we have some promising leads in that area."// Police have advised citizens to stay in at night and if you must go out to not do so alone.

In possibly related news, more than one hundred,-//

With a disgusted grunt, he pulls up to the curb, putting the car in park.

"What do we have?"  Peter Duntel asks the nearby officer as he exits his car.

"They've caught him,"  The young officer says, his eyes just a little too wide.

//Kid's rattled,// Peter realizes, //Bet <<print $PlayerName>> would do anything to be here right now.//

"Caught who?"  Peter asks, not quite following the train of thought, "Isn't this another Patchwork Murderer site?"

"It is,"  The young officer begins, but Peter finally realizes what the man is saying, "You're saying they caught the Patchwork Murderer?!?"

The young officer just nods.

Peter almost runs toward where he sees a group of officers flanking the alley to keep people out.  

"Sir,"  A burly cop steps toward Peter as he begins to speak, "You shouldn't be here," Mike says, a good cop that Peter has known for more than a decade.

"What the hell you talking about, I'm the lead on this case, I //belong// here.

Mike swallows visibly, "I can't stop you,"  The thick set
passages.content_zh:
一個月後。

收音機大聲播放著，車主駕車駛向目的地。

恐懼仍籠罩著這個地區，因為「拼布殺人魔」依然逍遙法外。收音機裡的聲音快速播報著，揚聲器傳來些微靜電噪音——第十二起大規模謀殺案已被通報。截至目前，警方已能從八個犯罪現場發現的屍塊中辨識出近三十名受害者。警方目前要麼尚不清楚，要麼尚未公佈所有犯罪現場的受害者總數。偵探彼得·鄧特爾被引述說：「我們正追查所有可能的線索，努力辨識受害者身份。這與幾年前發生的事件有驚人的相似之處，我們在那方面已有一些頗有希望的線索。」警方建議市民夜間留在家中，若必須外出，切勿單獨行動。

在可能相關的新聞中，超過一百——

他厭惡地咕噥一聲，將車停到路邊，換到停車檔。

「我們有什麼發現？」彼得·鄧特爾下車時詢問附近的警官。

「他們抓到他了，」年輕的警官說，眼睛瞪得有點過大。

這小子嚇壞了，彼得意識到，打賭 $PlayerName 現在會不惜一切代價想待在這裡。

「抓到誰？」彼得問，沒完全跟上思路，「這不是另一個拼布殺人魔的現場嗎？」

「是的，」年輕的警官開口，但彼得終於明白這人在說什麼，「你是說他們抓到了拼布殺人魔？！？」

年輕的警官只是點點頭。

彼得幾乎是跑向一群警察把守巷口、阻止旁人進入的地方。

「長官，」一個魁梧的警察在彼得開口時走向他，「你不該來這裡，」麥克說，他是彼得認識十多年的一位好警察。

「你他媽在說什麼，我是這案子的負責人，我屬於這裡。」

麥克明顯吞了口口水，「我阻止不了你，」這個壯實的男人說，眼中帶著擔憂，「但我真的認為你應該迴避這次，讓別人告訴我們在這裡發現了什麼，而不是——」

一定比平常更糟，麥克不是那種會讓事情影響情緒的人。

彼得揮手打斷他，推開人群擠了進去。在巷子的封閉區域裡，濃重的血腥味瀰漫在空氣中。

清晨的陽光角度恰好，照亮了狹窄的巷子，大片用深紅血液塗抹的奇異符號汙染了四周的牆壁。

幾名警察聚集在不遠處，擋住了他看向巷子更深處的視線。

一定是屍體，彼得暗自思忖，一時好奇他們是否已經逮捕了拼布殺人魔，或者他還在這附近某處？

從兩名警察之間擠過去，彼得第一次看到了謀殺現場。

在過去一個月看過這麼多可怕場景後，他能看出幾乎一切都和其他現場如出一轍。

唯一的不同之處卻違反常理。

「$PlayerName，你在這裡做什麼？你為什麼穿著裙子？」他問那個站在屍體旁、腳踝深陷血泊中的男人。話一出口，他就意識到自己所見的景象有極度不對勁的地方，遠超乎老友穿著女裝這件事。

彼得瞪大眼睛，伸手去拔腰間的槍，以誇張的緩慢速度抽出了史密斯威森麥格農手槍。

「彼得！你來幫忙了！」$PlayerName 帶著詭異的微笑說，「我們可以救她！我們可以讓她復活！我一直在練習。」

瘋狂清晰地寫在他的眼中，彼得的前搭檔繼續說道：「我盡了一切努力，但他們……做了……」他呼吸急促，唾沫從唇邊飛濺，「對她做了可怕的事。」

彼得無法強迫自己將武器指向他的前搭檔、他最老的朋友。他張口想說話，想問為什麼，但話語哽住，在舌尖夭折，因為他第一次看清了那具屍體。

這具屍體尚未完成。他們一定是在他展示這具屍體的過程中打斷了他，那傳統的皮膚層才剛剛開始縫合到臉上。彼得幾乎立刻認出了那張臉。比他記憶中更女性化，但無疑是 Tom 的臉。

「她很美，不是嗎？」$PlayerName 站在原處輕聲說道，「她現在叫 Tanya。或者等她復活後，就會是了。」他的聲音帶著一種令人難忘的特質，因興奮而顫抖。

彼得身後，另一名警官發出了聲音。很可能他正努力壓抑嘔吐的衝動。

彼得舉起手槍，瞄準 $PlayerName。一直都是他嗎？從第一起謀殺開始？還是只是最近這一系列？一種奇異的平靜籠罩了彼得。他殺了我的兒子，對他做了這種可怕的事。彼得瞥了一眼地上屍體的臉，那是他唯一能確信屬於他兒子的一部分。

「我們現在必須迅速行動，」$PlayerName 突然說道，將彼得的注意力拉回他身上，「一旦 Tanya 復活，我們就得逃跑，我們得躲起來。先驅者已在我們的世界，她很快就會到來。」

結局？

[新遊戲附加。]

passages.name: I turn on my heel.
passages.content:
The scene before me stops me dead in my tracks.  

//Its happening.//

I can barely make out shapes in the fog, though they seem to slip from sight any time I try to focus on any of them.  Instead I direct my attention to the stone table, the altar laid out before me.

On the altar, I can see a man laying on top of a woman who appears to be chained to its surface.  From the angle I am standing at, I can clearly see him penetrating her, thrusting repeatedly as he fucks her cunt.  

Years of habit take over.  I reach into my jacket for my .38 Special, only to find it missing.  Lacking that, I try to force myself forward, but seem to be rooted to the spot.  

I can only see the back of the man, but he seems somehow familiar, //The bones in his hair,//  The man thrusts forward one last time, I can see his balls pull tight  from where I am standing, his cock throbbing as he cums inside of his victim.  //It's Vick.//

When he climbs off of her, I can see the woman clearly for the first time.  <<if $MorichiDaughter gte 3>>//Azumi!//  I think in alarm. <<else>>//The hermaphrodite from my dream.//  I think in surprise. <</if>>Her expression is glazed, as if she is there in body, but her mind is elsewhere.  From the neck down she is covered in blood from a thousand little cuts across her torso.  My every instinct demands that I help her.

Vick turns toward me, a smile on his thin lips, "You're just in time."  His tone holds a cold satisfaction to it.

My eyes flicker to the altar, then back to Vick.   Suddenly able to move again, I start making my way toward his captive, keeping plenty of distance between Vick and myself.

All the while, shapes move in the fog.  I try to ignore them as best I can, but increasingly I am aware that not all of the shapes are human.  I shake my head, trying to clear my increasingly chaotic thoughts enough to focus on the task at hand.  

"I'm here to help,"  I say, hoping <<if $MorichiDaughter gte 3>>Azumi <<else>>the hermaphr

passages.name: Do you wish to play the game with enhanced stats?
passages.content:
NOTE:  You are now playing New Game +.  Your character is now tremendously overpowered, you have skills at levels that will always generate the highest possible skill check results, you have a mountain of ammo made small only in comparison to your mountain of cash, and on the near impossibility that you find yourself in dire straits you also have a few extra first aid kits to get you through some of the nastier fights in the game.

Now, let our tale begain anew.

[img[images/Title.jpg]]

I spent nearly twenty-five years on the force. 

In that time, I had convinced myself that mankind was at the root of all this world's evil.  The things a person is capable of doing to another human being are truly horrible.  I'd seen the worst this world had to offer, or so I thought.

passages.content:
My opponent looks <<if $enemyHP gte 5>>powerful<<elseif $enemyHP gte 4>>tough.  <<elseif $enemyHP gte 3>>healthy.  <<elseif $enemyHP gte 2>>badly hurt.  <<else>>nearly dead.<</if>>

The howl of wind across the bluff is nearly enough to drown out the sounds of your fighting.  The relentless rain seems intent to drown the entire island before your fight can end.

/%Player's actions%/
<<if $playerAction is "gun">>\s
   <<if $Ammo gte 1>>/%Assuming enemies have an ammunition count as well. If not, "$ammo" would be shorter%/\s
   <<set $Hit = $PlayerFirearms + random(1,20)>>\s
      <<if $Hit gt $enemyDefense>>\s
	  <<set $damage = random(2,4)>>
		You deal <<print $damage>> to the enemy! \s
      <<set $enemyHP = $enemyHP - $damage>>/%You didn't specify the damage formula, so I'm using this. =^-^=%/\s
         <<if $enemyHP gte 1>>\s
		Your assailant is still on their feet. <<set $Ammo -= 1>>
         <<else>>\s
		Your assailant falls to the ground, dead.<<set $Ammo -= 1>>
         <</if>>\s
      <<else>>\s
		Your attack misses.<<set $Ammo -= 1>>
      <</if>>\s
   <<else>>\s
	You have no ammo!
   <</if>>\s
<<elseif $playerAction is "fists">>\s

	<<set $Hit = $PlayerBrawling + random(1,20)>>\s

		<<set $damage = random(1,2)>>
		You deal <<print $damage>> to the enemy! \s

			<<if $enemyHP gte 1>>\s
			Your assailant is still on their feet.
			<<else>>\s

passages.name: Bluff
passages.content:
<<if $BookFight is "Not Played" && $PastorBook is "Taken" && $Day gte 7>> A particularly hard gust of wind staggers me as I crest the top of the bluff.  The cold rain stings as it strikes my skin, like a thousand little spears nettling my face and hands.

I turn slightly, partially to ragain my footing, and partially to avoid the worst of the rain hitting me in the face.  

As I do so, I discover that I am not alone.  A hulking figure so large that I feel like a small child beside it.  Even several paces behind me, it seems to loom over me.  My eyes widen in horror at the sight, rooted to the spot as if my remaining still may somehow avoid provoking the abomonation.  Whatever it is, in spite of its size it still looks //mostly// human, and its state of nakedness leaves little doubt that it's female.

"Boooook,"  It says in a drawn out croak.

//Does this //thing// want the book from the church?//  I wonder, acutely aware of the book's heavy weight inside of my jacket.

Realizing that this //thing// has yet to attack, but sensing that it could happen at any moment, I only have a split second to weigh my options.

[[Threaten it.]]

[[Attack it.|Book Fight]]

[[Try to reason with it.]]<<set $BookFight to "Played">><<else>>From the top of the bluff, I feel like I can see forever into the cloud shrouded distance.

<<if $Day is 5 && $DOneSighting is "Not Played">>When my eyes turn to the waters far below, my attention is quickly drawn to a pale shape among the rocks.

My eyes widen in surprise and I quickly start to pull my jacket off as I prepare to dive into the waters.  Then the reality of the situation starts to sink in, //Jumping from this height would be dangerous, and with those rocks below, it would be suicide.//  I think hopelessly.

All I can do is look down helplessly at the body being tossed about by the breaking waves.
