passages.name: Ask about her family.
passages.content:
<<if ($PlayerEttiquette + $PlayerCharisma) gte 15>>”I tend to be a curious person by nature, and I was wondering if you would be willing to tell me a little bit about your family?  Have you lived in Wanton Cove long?  That kind of thing.”  You ask in a friendly tone.

Her raptor like gaze seems to hold me in place, “I am the daughter of Walter and Ruth Schultz,”  Her tone suggests that the names should mean something to me, though I’m not sure I have ever heard of them before, //Schultz, that’s a pretty common German family name.//  My musings end as she continues, “They owned half of Wanton Cove, and ran all of the fishing industry here in town.  After they died, I took over.”

Leaning forward, her eyes never leave my own.  When she leans forward, I feel an urge to lean back, away from her, and though I try to suppress that urge, after a few seconds, I do lean back from her.

//The woman has a presence like a force of nature.//
“My worthless husband’s name is Toby,”  The smile that suddenly forms on her lips is anything but kind, “But we just call him Sophie.  My son Dewey is made of sterner stuff than his father though.”

I don’t have to wonder if she’s done talking or not, she simply leans back in her chair again and gives me a look that suggests I’m wasting her time.

<<elseif ($PlayerEttiquette + $PlayerCharisma) gte 8>>”I was wondering if you’d be interested in talking for a little while?”  I venture carefully.

“I let you into my house, didn’t I?”  She replies coldly.
“I’d like to know more about you and your family if that’s ok.”

Lady Anne’s gaze holds my own like a physical force until she finally replies, “I am the daughter of Walter and Ruth Schultz,”  Her tone suggests that the names should mean something to me, though I’m not sure I have ever heard of them before, //Schultz, that’s a pretty common German family name.//  My musings end as she continues, “They owned half of Wanton Cove, and ran all of the fishing industry here in town. 眼前的景象讓我猛然止步。

它正在發生。

我勉強能辨識霧中的形體，但每當我試圖聚焦，它們便從視線中溜走。於是我將注意力轉向石桌，那座展現在我面前的祭壇。

祭壇上，我看見一個男人壓在一個女人身上，後者似乎被鎖鏈固定在檯面。從我站立的角度，能清楚看見他正插入她體內，反覆抽送，操弄著她的陰戶。

多年的習慣接管了行動。我伸手探入外套想掏出我的.38特製左輪，卻發現它不見了。沒有槍，我試圖強迫自己前進，但雙腳卻像紮根般動彈不得。

我只能看見男人的背影，但他有種莫名的熟悉感。他髮間的白骨飾物。男人最後一次向前挺進，從我站的位置能看見他的睪丸緊縮，陰莖在他於受害者體內射精時搏動著。是Vick。

當他從她身上爬下時，我第一次清楚看見那女人的臉。[IF $MorichiDaughter gte 3]是Azumi！我驚恐地想。[ELSE]是我夢中的雙性人。我驚訝地想。[/IF]她的表情呆滯，彷彿身體在此，心神卻在別處。從脖子以下，她軀幹上千道細小割傷流出的鮮血覆滿全身。我每一分本能都叫囂著要幫助她。

Vick轉向我，薄唇勾起一抹微笑，「你來得正是時候。」他的語氣帶著冰冷的滿足。

我的視線快速掃向祭壇，又回到Vick身上。突然間我能動了，開始朝他的俘虜走去，同時與Vick保持著足夠距離。

與此同時，形體在霧中移動。我盡力忽視它們，但越來越意識到並非所有形體都是人類。我搖搖頭，試圖釐清越發混亂的思緒，專注於眼前的任務。

「我來幫妳。」我說，希望[IF $MorichiDaughter gte 3]Azumi[ELSE]這雙性人[/IF]對周遭發生的事還有足夠意識，能真正聽見我的話。

當我抵達石桌時，已經開始評估她的傷勢。那些割傷雖然導致大量失血，但單獨看來似乎都不致命。我得找方法蓋住這些傷口，減緩流血，直到能將她帶給能幫忙的人。

在我看不見的後方，虛空更猛烈地拉扯著這個詭異之地，它變得比以往更多，同時又比原本的空無更少。

霧中某處傳來一聲尖叫。短促清晰，驟然中止。是Tanya。我認得出她的聲音。

「你知道接下來會發生什麼。」Vick的聲音從霧中另一處傳來，但我幾乎沒聽進他的話，「你該為自己的墮落感到羞恥。你曾有那麼大的潛力。」最後幾句話在我感知邊緣輕如耳語。

*Thu'ulh, eet chu plech na Zal'chaas.* 這詭異的話語同時從四面八方湧來，在我腦中無盡迴盪，淹沒了所有其他聲音。一股從未經歷過的強烈慾望浪潮襲捲了我，讓我意識到自己正處於興奮狀態。

這感覺如此強烈，我覺得自己該射精了，但無從釋放這股感受，我只能停留在爆發的邊緣。

真美，我突然想著，看向眼前鎖在桌上的女人。她的裸體牢牢吸住我的注意力。「你真是蒙受祝福。」話語不受控制地從你口中滑出。

我內心深處開始產生變化，一股黑暗從潛伏之處升起，一股始終蟄伏在你靈魂中的黑暗。

我對抗這些新思緒、新感受，但就像試圖用桶舀乾海洋。這是一場不可能贏的戰鬥。

我的目光游移過她的身體，欣賞她血跡斑斑的乳房，視線繼續向下。我伸出一隻手，觸碰她的大腿內側。

她以一聲充滿痛苦的呻吟回應。

我的手指輕易滑入她被充分操弄過的穴口。

*你該為自己的墮落感到羞恥。你曾有那麼大的潛力。* Vick這番奇怪的用詞在我心中產生共鳴，儘管我不明白為何會如此。當這念頭閃過腦海時，我開始意識到自己似乎不再掌控自己的身體。當事件開始以我無法阻止的方式展開時，我感覺像個寄居在自己體內的陌生人。

我的雙手開始在她身上游移，揉捏她的乳房，輕撫她的陰莖。幾分鐘後，我將另一隻手也放到她的陰莖上，用雙手撫弄它。緩慢而平穩的套弄，引導她越來越接近高潮。

我手中的陰莖突然搏動，一股濃稠的精液從頂端噴射而出，濺灑在她的腹部，流過我的手指。

[IF $MorichiDaughter gte 3]Azumi[ELSE]這雙性人[/IF]顫抖著，彷彿高潮的餘波。

但接著某件事改變了。

當她的分娩開始時，我笑了。

[繼續。]

passages.name: You witnessed Pastor Erksons death?
passages.content:
"I heard that you saw Pastor Erkon commit suicide?"  I begin.

"You heard right,"  Mr Jackobson replies.

"Can you tell me about it?  Let me know what you saw?"  I continue.

"I could,"  Mr Jackobson says with a hint of smile.

<<if ($PlayerStreetwise + $PlayerCharisma) gte 12>>I wait for him to continue, but realizing that he's trying to get a rise out of me, I chance tact.

"We going to enjoy this comfortable silence a little longer, or you going to tell me what you remember?"  I prompt.

Mr Jackobson lets out an abrupt laugh, a single sound that seems to linger in the air, "I like you." <<if $Mutation gte 5>>He grins, "For a fucking fish man, you're alright."

"Fish man?"  I inquire, thinking about the changes to my body and how what I'm becoming can hardly be considered human anymore.

"Yeah, fish man, you looked in a mirror recently?"  Mr Jackobson sneers, "And before you bother asking, no, I dont know why it happens to some people around here."

<</if>>The seconds start to tick by again, my fristration with the man rapidly growing, though I hide it well. 

After a few more seconds tick by, Mr Jackobson starts to speak, "Pastor Erkon got sick,"  He begins, his voice lacking emotion, "Spent a lot of time alone, in that church of his.  Wasn't acting like his friendly self anymore."

Mr Jackobson's eyes never leave my own as he speaks, their intensity almost enough to unsettle me, "About four years ago now, he just walked out to the bluff.  I could see him from where I was walking out by the treeline up there.  He just stood there for a few seconds, then jumped."

I cant help but feel like the story is rehearsed, realizing that he used the same words as Aaron through some of what he said.

The faintest hint of a smile touches Mr Jackobsons features as he watches my reaction.

<<elseif ($PlayerStreetwise + $PlayerCharisma) gte 8>>I wait for him to continue, the silence becoming uncomfortable as the seconds tick by.

"Well?"  I prompt.\

passages.name: HighTaintEndAzumiFinal
passages.content:
One month later.

//The radio plays loudly as its owner navigates the vehicle toward his destination.

//Fear still grips the region as the Patchwork Murderer is still at large,// The voice on the radio speaks quickly, just a little static coming from the speakers, //as a twelfth mass murder was reported.  So far police have been able to identify nearly thirty of the victims from body parts found across eight crime scenes.  The police so far either do now know, or are not reporting on the total number of victims across the crime scenes.  Detective Peter Duntel has been quoted as saying //"We're following every possible lead and working hard to identify the victims.  This has some striking parallels to something that happened a few years back and we have some promising leads in that area."// Police have advised citizens to stay in at night and if you must go out to not do so alone.

In possibly related news, more than one hundred,-//

With a disgusted grunt, he pulls up to the curb, putting the car in park.

"What do we have?"  Peter Duntel asks the nearby officer as he exits his car.

"They've caught him,"  The young officer says, his eyes just a little too wide.

//Kid's rattled,// Peter realizes, //Bet <<print $PlayerName>> would do anything to be here right now.//

"Caught who?"  Peter asks, not quite following the train of thought, "Isn't this another Patchwork Murderer site?"

"It is,"  The young officer begins, but Peter finally realizes what the man is saying, "You're saying they caught the Patchwork Murderer?!?"

The young officer just nods.

Peter almost runs toward where he sees a group of officers flanking the alley to keep people out.  

"Sir,"  A burly cop steps toward Peter as he begins to speak, "You shouldn't be here," Mike says, a good cop that Peter has known for more than a decade.

"What the hell you talking about, I'm the lead on this case, I //belong// here.

Mike swallows visibly, "I can't stop you,"  The thick set
passages.content_zh:
一個月後。

收音機大聲播放著，車主駕車駛向目的地。

恐懼仍籠罩著這個地區，因為「拼布殺人魔」依然逍遙法外。收音機裡的聲音急促地說著，揚聲器傳來些微靜電雜音，報導著第十二起大規模謀殺案。截至目前，警方已從八個犯罪現場發現的屍塊中辨識出近三十名受害者。警方目前要麼尚未掌握，要麼尚未公佈所有犯罪現場的受害者總數。偵探彼得·鄧特爾被引述說：「我們正追查所有可能的線索，努力辨識受害者身份。這與幾年前發生的事件有驚人的相似之處，我們在那方面已有一些頗有希望的線索。」警方建議市民夜間留在家中，若必須外出，切勿單獨行動。

在可能相關的新聞中，超過一百名——

他厭惡地咕噥一聲，將車停到路邊，換到停車檔。

「我們這裡有什麼情況？」彼得·鄧特爾下車時詢問附近的警官。

「他們抓到他了，」年輕警官說道，眼睛瞪得有點過大。

這小子嚇壞了，彼得意識到，打賭$PlayerName現在會不惜一切代價想待在這裡。

「抓到誰？」彼得問道，沒完全跟上對方的思路，「這不是另一個拼布殺人魔的現場嗎？」

「是的，」年輕警官開口，但彼得終於明白這人在說什麼，「你是說他們抓到了拼布殺人魔？！？」

年輕警官只是點了點頭。

彼得幾乎是跑向一群警察把守巷口、阻止旁人進入的地方。

「長官，」一名魁梧的警察在彼得開口時走向他，「你不該來這裡，」麥克說道，他是彼得認識十多年的好警察。

「你他媽在說什麼，我是這案子的負責人，我本來就該在這裡。」

麥克明顯地吞了吞口水，「我阻止不了你，」這個壯實的男人說道，眼中帶著擔憂，「但我真的認為你應該迴避這次，讓別人告訴我們在這裡發現了什麼，而不是——」

情況一定比平常更糟，麥克不是那種會讓事情影響到自己的人。

彼得揮手打斷他，推開人群前進。在巷子的封閉區域裡，濃重的血腥味瀰漫在空氣中。

清晨的陽光角度恰好，照亮了狹窄的巷子，大片用深紅血液塗抹的怪異符號玷污了他周圍的牆壁。

幾名警察聚集在不遠處，擋住了他看向巷子更深處的視線。

一定是屍體，彼得暗自想著，一時好奇他們是否已經逮捕了拼布殺人魔，或者他還在這附近某處？

從兩名警察之間擠過去，彼得第一次看到了謀殺現場。

在過去一個月見識了這麼多可怕場景後，他能看出幾乎一切都和其他現場如出一轍。

唯一的不同之處卻違反常理。

「$PlayerName，你在這裡做什麼？你為什麼穿著裙子？」他問那個站在屍體旁、腳踝深陷血泊中的男人。話一出口，他就意識到自己所見的景象有極度不對勁的地方，遠超乎老友穿著女裝這件事。

彼得瞪大眼睛，伸手去拔腰間的槍，以誇張的緩慢速度抽出了史密斯威森麥格農手槍。

「彼得！你來幫忙了！」$PlayerName帶著詭異的微笑說道，「我快要救出Azumi了！」

$PlayerName突然看向身旁的空處，「別擔心，他會幫助我們的。我們現在非常接近了！」

當他再次看向彼得時，眼中的瘋狂清晰可見，「我愛她，」他說道，彷彿這就解釋了一切。

彼得無法強迫自己將武器指向他以前的搭檔、他最老的朋友。彼得張口欲言，想問為什麼，但話語哽住，在他第一次看清屍體時便胎死腹中。

這具屍體尚未完成，他們一定是在他展示這具屍體的過程中打斷了他，那層傳統的皮膚才剛剛開始縫合到臉上。彼得幾乎立刻認出了那張臉。比他記憶中更女性化，但無疑是Tom的臉。

「她很美，不是嗎？」$PlayerName站在原處輕聲說道，「我們現在叫她Tanya。她正在幫我從她手中拯救Azumi，」他的聲音帶著一種縈繞不去的特質，「Azumi原本被設定為完美的容器，但現在Tanya可以代替她孕育，」他的聲音因興奮而顫抖，「你不明白嗎，Azumi不必死了！」

開端

[新遊戲+。]

passages.name: Happy End 1
passages.content:
Thursday morning, shortly after 10am.

Morning found me sitting in my recliner, feet kicked up, wearing an old white tank top and a pair of dirty white boxer shorts.  

Idly, I turn the knob on the television again, looking for something other than news reports of the latest heinous act of violence against some little kid, or poorly written shows about a perfect world that doesn't really exist.  I stop on the news channel again, giving the national weather forecast in all its black and white glory. 

Setting the remote down again, I trade it out for the warm beer from last night still sitting on the end table.

The beer almost reaches my lips when I hear a firm knock on my living room door.  //God damn it, if that's Peter wanting a favor again,//  My eyes narrow at the thought, //Again?  I haven't seen him in over a year.//

Sighing, I set the beer back down and try to kick in the foot rest of my recliner.  The foot rest swings right back up.  With an exasperated growl, I kick it down a second time.  This time it catches and stays down.

The knock on my door is firmer this time, more insistent.  "I'm coming!"  I yell towards the door, not caring that my annoyance is clear in my voice.

Wading through the mess towards the front door, I'm only mildly surprised that I don't knock any of the precarious piles of books, papers or mess over before reaching the door.

Opening the door as far as the chain lock will allow, "Whatever you're selling, I'm not interested."  I growl, not even bothering to look who it is at the door.

"<<print $PlayerName>>, it's me, Peter."  Peter Duntell, my old partner says from the other side of the door.  "I tried calling, but you didn't take my call.  I need a favor.  My son has gone missing."  

//Knew he would want a favor, probably going to try sending me off to some God forsaken hell.  Not going to happen.//

I can hear the pain, the worry in his voice.  But to be honest, I couldn't bring myself to care all that much
passages.content_zh:
星期四早上，十點剛過不久。

早晨發現我癱在躺椅上，雙腳翹著，身上穿著一件舊白色背心和一條髒兮兮的白色四角褲。

我漫不經心地再次轉動電視旋鈕，想找點別的節目看——除了那些關於小孩遭受最新駭人暴力的新聞報導，或是描繪那個根本不存在的完美世界的拙劣劇集。我又停在了新聞頻道，上面正用黑白畫面播報著全國天氣預報。

再次放下遙控器，我換成拿起昨晚留在茶几上那罐溫熱的啤酒。

啤酒幾乎要碰到嘴唇時，我聽到客廳門上傳來一陣堅定的敲門聲。該死的，如果是Peter又想來討人情——想到這裡我瞇起眼睛，又來？我已經一年多沒見過他了。

嘆了口氣，我把啤酒放回去，試圖踢下躺椅的腳凳。腳凳卻直接彈了回去。我惱怒地低吼一聲，又踢了第二次。這次它卡住，總算保持放下來了。

門上的敲擊聲這次更用力、更堅持了。「來了啦！」我朝門吼道，毫不掩飾語氣中的煩躁。

我蹚過滿地雜物走向前門，居然沒撞倒任何一堆搖搖欲墜的書本、文件或垃圾，連我自己都有點意外。

我把門打開到鏈條鎖允許的最大縫隙，「不管你賣什麼，我沒興趣。」我低吼道，甚至懶得看門外是誰。

「$PlayerName，是我，Peter。」我的老搭檔Peter Duntell在門外說道。「我打過電話，但你沒接。我需要幫忙。我兒子失蹤了。」

就知道他會來討人情，八成又想把我打發去什麼天殺的鬼地方。門都沒有。

我能聽出他聲音裡的痛苦和擔憂。但老實說，我實在提不起多少關心。我喜歡他兒子，就像喜歡他父親一樣，他是個好孩子，但我現在只想回去喝我的啤酒。「不關我的事，報警啊，去報失蹤人口。我看不出這跟我有什麼關係。」我說道，語氣比我本意更尖刻。

我再次關上門，轉身準備離開。

Peter又敲門了，這次是用拳頭捶。撞擊聲響得讓我腦袋感覺快要炸開，也可能只是我昨晚宿醉太嚴重。

「$PlayerName！」Peter隔門吼道，「我試過了！已經一個多月了！」接著他說了一句我從未聽他承認過的話：「我很害怕。」

聽著，Peter是個硬漢。他五十出頭，大概比我大五歲，一生所有成就都是拚命掙來的。他是那種脾氣穩、行動快、從不退縮、永遠堅持做自己認為對的事的男人。聽到他承認自己害怕，而且我相信他是真心的……嗯，老實說，就算我發現外星人入侵了，可能都沒這麼驚訝。

我猛地停下腳步。甚至在意識到自己至少會聽他把話說完之前，我的肩膀就已經垮了下來，一副認輸的模樣。

「幹。」我喃喃自語。

看來不管我喜不喜歡，都得回去了——這念頭就像陌生人在我耳邊低語著胡話。

我轉身回到門邊，解開鏈條鎖，把門拉開一半。我已經走回躺椅的半路上，他才把門完全推開，自己走了進來。

過了幾次心跳的時間，「你怎麼變成這樣？」Peter問道。我完全明白他的意思。我見識過太多，太多暴行，太多無辜牽連。我被這些事擊垮了，而現在，我正試圖殺死自己，用一罐又一罐的啤酒。

「你他媽清楚我怎麼了。」我拿起那罐溫啤酒，一口氣灌完剩下的，然後把空罐朝一個早已堆滿空瓶的垃圾桶扔去。我扔出的罐子，連同另外三個，哐啷一聲掉在地上，加入了雜物的行列。

「要喝啤酒嗎？」我問著，一邊小心翼翼地走向廚房。

「不，我不想喝，你也不該喝。」Peter堅定地說。

如果要回去，我肯定會想喝啤酒——我環顧四周，希望能找到另一瓶開過但可能還沒空的，失敗之後，嘆了口氣。

我轉頭看向Peter，心中混雜著怒氣與煩躁，「你只有五分鐘。」

「你會給我更多時間。你會聽我說完，而且你會幫忙。」我甚至還沒來得及開口抗議，他就舉起一根手指阻止我，「在你做過那些事之後。你欠我的。」他語氣堅定。

好吧，幹。

[繼續。]

passages.name: Threaten it.
passages.content:
Reaching into my jacket, I draw my .38 Special with a practiced motion, <<if ($PlayerIntimidation + $PlayerStrength) gte 10>>"You're going to back away from me now,"  I put as much heat into my voice as I can.  

I try to not let my surprise show as I see the thing take a slow step back.  There's a mix of madness and terror in its eyes as it stretches one hand towards me.  Its clawed fingers pointed at me like weapons for a moment before it turns his hand, splaying its fingers like it expects me to hand it something, "Booook, neeeeeed iiiiiiiit,"  Its croaking voice whines uncomfortably in my ears.

"No, I need it," I reply.  But before I can say more, it takes a half step towards me.  Instinctively, I raise my gun a little higher, pointing it towards this monstrocity's face.

It stops, its too large eyes shifting between my face and the .38 Special in my hand.  Its expression turns desperate as it takes several small steps back before pivoting and starting to run across the bluff.  

I start to follow, the muddy ground making me feel sluggish as I watch it rapidly increase the distance between us.  But I don't have to go far before I begin to slow as she heads towards the edge of the bluff.  //No where left for her to run.  She has to come back this way if she wants to get off this bluff.//

The thought barely crosses my mind before I realize there is no sign of the creature slowing.  

My pulse quickens as I watch in disbelief.  In stead of slowing, if anything it begins to run faster, then as it fanishes from view, I can hear it make a sound that may have been a scream that drops off into the distance.  By the time I near the bluff's edge, I look down into the water, and the jagged rocks, far below.  I let my eyes sweep the area, but seeing no sign of the thing that had just jumped, I reluctantly step back from the edge.

<<else>>"Stop, stay away,"  You yell, but even you can hear the uncretainty in your own voice.

In a blur of motion, the creatu

passages.name: First Visit to Ruins.
passages.content:
Looking at the ground again, I pay more attention to that I am standing on.  I quickly come to the conclusion that the path, //No,// My eyes take in the width of the area I am walking, //The road,// is actually made of crushed seashells and bits of coral.

I turn my attention back to the buildings around me, taking in their features as well.  The bulk of the buildings I can see are large and round at the bottom, rising up to a narrow point high up.  //Like they were grown,// I think again.  The hairs on the back of my neck begin to stand on end.

Walking slowly, I find myself transfixed by the strange beauty of the place, the way what little light makes it through the trees above reflects off of the buildings, reflecting the light in a host of colors, //It's like being inside of a rainbow.//

I do not know how long I wandered, but I'm eventually pulled from my reprieve by a faint and unfamiliar sound.  

My footsteps draw me in that direction.  As the sound becomes clearer, it only becomes more difficult to understand what I am hearing.  It's like a thousand different voices, speaking as many different languages, all trying to whisper over the top of one another.  

I feel a chill in my bones that has nothing to do with the cold or wet.  Part of me wants to move closer to the sound, yet part of me wants to flee.

My curiosity gets the best of me and I carefully continue forward as stealthily as I am able.  

When I round the building I am closest to, I at first see something I mistake as a snake, but then my eyes follow the tentacle toward its source.  

What I see makes no sense to me.  I can see with my own eyes that what lies before me is real, though the rational part of my mind knows beyond doubt that what I see is an impossibility.  I don't know if I want to laugh, cry, or shriek in terror at the sight.

Before me is a mass, a mass of eyes, mouths and tentacles in fleshy pink colors.  The tentacles appear to range from maybe six to perhaps a

My opponent looks <<if $enemyHP gte 5>>powerful<<elseif $enemyHP gte 4>>tough.  <<elseif $enemyHP gte 3>>healthy.  <<elseif $enemyHP gte 2>>badly hurt.  <<else>>nearly dead.<</if>>

The interior of the Barmaid's Daughter is louder than normal as patrons cheer and shout happily.  All around you, the people seem to back away from you, leaving room for you to fight with your assailant.

   <<if $Ammo gte 1>>/%Assuming enemies have an ammunition count as well. If not, "$ammo" would be shorter%/\s
   <<set $Hit = $PlayerFirearms + random(1,20)>>\s
      <<if $Hit gt $enemyDefense>>\s

      <<set $enemyHP = $enemyHP - $damage>>/%You didn't specify the damage formula, so I'm using this. =^-^=%/\s

		Your assailant is still on their feet. <<set $Ammo -= 1>>

		Your assailant falls to the ground, unconscious.<<set $Ammo -= 1>>
         <</if>>\s

      <</if>>\s

	You have no ammo!

			<<if $enemyHP gte 1>>\s

			<<else>>\s

<<elseif $playerAction is "use">>
Working quickly with your First Aid Kit, you tend the worst of your wounds.

<</if>>\s

	Your opponent slashes at you with their knife.

passages.name: MidTaintEndAfter2
passages.content:
One month later.

//The radio plays loudly as its owner navigates the vehicle toward his destination.

//Fear still grips the region as the Patchwork Murderer is still at large,// The voice on the radio speaks quickly, just a little static coming from the speakers, //as a twelfth mass murder was reported.  So far police have been able to identify nearly thirty of the victims from body parts found across eight crime scenes.  The police so far either do now know, or are not reporting on the total number of victims across the crime scenes.  Detective Peter Duntel has been quoted as saying //"We're following every possible lead and working hard to identify the victims.  This has some striking parallels to something that happened a few years back and we have some promising leads in that area."// Police have advised citizens to stay in at night and if you must go out to not do so alone.

In possibly related news, more than one hundred,-//

With a disgusted grunt, he pulls up to the curb, putting the car in park.

"What do we have?"  Peter Duntel asks the nearby officer as he exits his car.

"They've caught him,"  The young officer says, his eyes just a little too wide.

//Kid's rattled,// Peter realizes, //Bet <<print $PlayerName>> would do anything to be here right now.//

"Caught who?"  Peter asks, not quite following the train of thought, "Isn't this another Patchwork Murderer site?"

"It is,"  The young officer begins, but Peter finally realizes what the man is saying, "You're saying they caught the Patchwork Murderer?!?"

The young officer just nods.

Peter almost runs toward where he sees a group of officers flanking the alley to keep people out.  

"Sir,"  A burly cop steps toward Peter as he begins to speak, "You shouldn't be here," Mike says, a good cop that Peter has known for more than a decade.

"What the hell you talking about, I'm the lead on this case, I //belong// here.

Mike swallows visibly, "I can't stop you,"  The thick set
passages.content_zh:
一個月後。

收音機大聲播放著，車主正駕車駛向目的地。

拼布殺人魔依然逍遙法外，恐懼仍緊抓著這片地區。收音機裡的聲音急促地說著，揚聲器傳來些微靜電雜音——第十二起大規模謀殺案已被通報。截至目前，警方已從八個犯罪現場發現的屍塊中辨識出近三十名受害者。警方目前要麼尚未掌握，要麼尚未公佈所有犯罪現場的受害者總數。偵探彼得·鄧特爾被引述說：「我們正追查所有可能的線索，努力辨識受害者身份。這與幾年前發生的事件有驚人的相似之處，我們在那方面已有一些頗有希望的線索。」警方建議市民夜間留在家中，若必須外出切勿獨行。

可能相關的消息，超過一百——

他厭惡地咕噥一聲，將車停靠路邊，打上停車檔。

「我們有什麼發現？」彼得·鄧特爾下車時間身旁的警官。

「他們抓到他了。」年輕警官說道，眼睛瞪得有點過大。

這小子嚇壞了，彼得意識到，打賭$PlayerName現在會不惜一切代價想待在這裡。

「抓到誰？」彼得問，沒完全跟上思路，「這不是另一個拼布殺人魔的現場嗎？」

「是的，」年輕警官開口，但彼得終於明白這人在說什麼，「你是說他們抓到了拼布殺人魔？！？」

年輕警官只是點頭。

彼得幾乎是跑向那群封鎖巷口、阻止民眾進入的警官方向。

「長官，」一名魁梧警察在彼得開口時走向他，「你不該來這裡。」麥克說道，他是彼得認識十多年的好警察。

「你他媽在說什麼，我是這案子的負責人，我本來就該在這裡。」

麥克明顯吞了口口水，「我阻止不了你，」這壯實的男人說道，眼中帶著擔憂，「但我真的認為你該迴避這次，讓別人告訴我們在這裡發現了什麼，而不是——」

肯定比往常更糟，麥克不是會讓事情影響情緒的人。

彼得揮手打斷他，推開人群前進。在巷子的封閉空間裡，濃重的血腥味瀰漫空氣中。

清晨的陽光角度恰好，照亮了狹窄的巷道，大片以鮮血繪成的怪異符號塗抹在他周圍的牆上。

幾名警官聚集在不遠處，擋住了他看向巷子更深處的視線。

肯定是屍體，彼得暗自想著，瞬間好奇他們是否已逮捕了拼布殺人魔，或者他仍在附近某處？

從兩名警官之間擠過去，彼得首次看見了謀殺現場。

過去一個月見識了這麼多可怕場景後，他能看出幾乎一切都與其他現場如出一轍。

唯一的不同卻違反常理。

「$PlayerName，你在這裡做什麼？」他問那個站在屍體旁、赤裸雙腳浸在血泊中的男人。可怕的疤痕，紅腫猙獰，可見於他的胯下。乍看之下，這男人似乎自行閹割了，那令人作嘔的傷口僅部分癒合。

話一出口，彼得的思緒才趕上他所見的景象，臉色頓時蒼白。

彼得瞪大雙眼，伸手去拔腰間的槍，以誇張的緩慢速度抽出史密斯威森麥格農手槍。

「彼得！我一直在等你！」$PlayerName帶著詭異的微笑說，「我的女主人會非常高興你終於來加入我們了！」

他無法強迫自己將武器指向昔日搭檔、最老的朋友。彼得張口欲言，想問為什麼，但話語哽住，在舌尖夭折——因為他第一次看清了那具屍體。

這具屍體尚未完成，他們肯定是在他展示這具屍體的過程中打斷了他，那傳統的皮膚層才剛開始縫合到臉上。彼得幾乎立刻認出了那張臉。比他記憶中更女性化，但無疑是Tom的臉。

「她很美，不是嗎？」$PlayerName從站立處輕聲說道，「我們現在叫她Tanya。自從我們的女主人——那預兆者——降臨後，她就乞求成為她獻祭的核心。」他的聲音帶著令人難忘的特質，「她是最後的獻祭，現在我們的工作可以開始了。」

開始

passages.name: FinalBook
passages.content:
"It's me, Tanya,"  Comes the voice from the other side of the door.

I nearly collapse with relief before the soft feminine voice echoes in my mind once more, //You will open the door for me, soon.//  A nearly overwhelming sense of despair washes over me as I am gripped by uncertainty.  

Falling to my knees, I drop my pistol at my side, my mind reeling as I momentarily loose the line between reality, . . . and //whatever// else is happening here in Wanton Cove.

"I'm coming in,"  Tanya's voice calls, sounding concerned. 

Putting action to words, the door to my room opens, bathing the area in light.  //The lamp went out,// I think, and I grasp that thought with all my strength, knowing that at least //that// is real.  Even with such a little thing to ground myself, I begin to organize my thoughts again, trying to make sense of the situation.  //Window must not have been properly latched, water or wind put the lamp out.//  I rationalize.  

//Then,// I shiver at the memory of her so close to me, nearly touching my skin, //There has to be a line between fantasy and reality, that chill must have been the cold from outside, mist from the rain, something.//

I turn my head slightly, noticing that Tanya has come to a complete stop just inside the room.  My vision has not yet fully returned, but I can tell where she is looking, and its not at me.

"Where did you find it?"  There's a reverence in her voice, "Where did you find the Al Azif?"  She slowly takes a step toward the book on the table.

"What are you going on about?"  I ask, grasping onto the discussion like someone about to drown clutching a ring buoy.  Focused on Tanya and the book, i struggle to my feet and stagger a step toward her.  My .38 Special long forgotten on the floor behind me.  "Al Sif?"

She looks at me.  My vision has cleared that I can just make out details on her face, but am unable to read her expression, "Al //Azif//,"  The way she emphasizes the second half of the name makes
passages.content_zh:
「是我，Tanya。」門的另一側傳來聲音。

我幾乎因鬆懈而癱軟，但那個柔和的女性嗓音再次在我腦海中迴響：你很快就會為我開門。一股近乎壓倒性的絕望感淹沒了我，不確定性緊緊攫住我的心。

我雙膝跪地，手槍滑落身旁，思緒混亂，瞬間失去了現實與……萬頓灣此地正在發生的某種異狀之間的界線。

「我要進來了。」Tanya的聲音喊道，聽起來帶著擔憂。

話語剛落，我房間的門被打開，光線灑滿整個區域。燈滅了，我想，並用盡全力抓住這個念頭，知道至少那是真實的。即使只是如此微小的事物能讓我立足，我開始重新整理思緒，試圖理解現狀。窗戶一定沒關好，雨水或風把燈弄熄了。我合理化地推測。

接著，我因回憶起她如此靠近我、幾乎觸及我皮膚的畫面而顫抖。幻想與現實之間必須有條界線，那股寒意一定是來自外面的冷空氣、雨中的霧氣，或是什麼別的。

我微微轉頭，注意到Tanya進房後便完全停下腳步。我的視力尚未完全恢復，但我能看出她正注視著什麼——而且不是看著我。

「你在哪裡找到它的？」她的聲音帶著一種崇敬，「你在哪裡找到《Al Azif》？」她緩緩朝桌上的書踏出一步。

「你在說什麼？」我問道，緊抓著這段對話，就像即將溺斃的人抓住救生圈。我專注於Tanya和那本書，掙扎著起身，踉蹌地朝她走了一步。我的.38 Special左輪早已被遺忘在身後的地板上。「Al Sif？」

她看向我。我的視力已清晰到能勉強辨識她臉上的細節，卻無法讀懂她的表情。「Al Azif，」她強調名字後半部分的方式，聽起來像是因我剛才的發音錯誤而感到不悅，「《死者之書》，Necronomicon，」她繼續說著，彷彿這些詞對我理應有意義，「而如果這是我一直在尋找的那個抄本，」當她朝書本又踏近一步時，我內心深處有個強烈的感覺：讓她碰到那本書可能不是個好主意，「那它就不是黑暗時代以來重製的複本之一。這一本……很古老。」

「別碰它！」我對自己聲音中的狠厲感到驚訝，「它是我的。」自從找到它後，我真的讓它離開過我的視線嗎？這個念頭令人不安，尤其是在短短幾分鐘前發生那些事之後。

Tanya舉起雙手示意冷靜，同時從桌邊退了半步，「$PlayerName，沒事的，我不會從你這裡拿走它或做什麼。但我來萬頓灣就是為了尋找這個。」

我一隻手撐著桌子穩住自己，另一隻手闔上書本，然後半轉向Tanya，「告訴你知道的一切？」我的開場比預期更尖銳。

她的目光瞥向書，又回到我身上，「好吧，你想知道什麼？」

我眨了兩次眼，視力仍然模糊但持續好轉，「全部。」

Tanya嘆了口氣，然後點頭，「好，我給你簡短版本。」她停頓片刻，似乎在整理思緒才再次開口，「公元八世紀有個名叫Alhazred的人，被稱為『半瘋的阿拉伯人』，據說是他撰寫了《Al Azif》，並崇拜著被稱為『舊日支配者』的惡魔生物。從那以後，其他人根據他的著作製作了更多抄本。圍繞這些舊日支配者的邪教幾乎自古就存在。」

我皺起眉頭，希望她快點說到重點。為什麼我感到如此煩躁？我瞥向那本書——這本《Al Azif》或Necronomicon——並意識到我的手正放在它的封面上。

「但儘管他在八世紀寫了這本書，我發現的證據顯示這些舊日支配者比已知歷史更古老，就像這座島上的遺跡，可能和吉薩大金字塔一樣古老，甚至更古老。」我不確定她的停頓是為了戲劇效果，還是在思考接下來該說什麼，「總之，我不認為Alhazred撰寫了《Al Azif》，我認為他是從某種更古老的東西翻譯過來的。」

「而你認為這就是他翻譯的原本？」我的語氣更像陳述而非疑問。

Tanya突然用奇怪的眼神打量著你，「你還好嗎？」

話音剛落，我頭暈目眩，視野突然模糊。最後的記憶是——我開始倒下。

[繼續。]

passages.name: Help her inside.
passages.content:
Once inside, I take a seat across from <<if $RoseCrow is "Not Played">>the crying woman.  

She dabs at her eyes with a rag that looks nearly as dirty as she is.

"You're Rose?"  I ask softly.

She nods.

"I'm <<print $PlayerName>> <<print $PlayerName2>>.  I'm not looking to upset you further, but I wanted to ask you about your husband, Wilson?"  

"You're an officer of the law?"  She asks, looking at me with a hint of hope in her eyes.

"I was once,"  I admit, "I'm for someone that went missing, a young man named Tom who came to Wanton Cove a while back."

"So you're like a private investigator?  Like in the films I've heard about?"  She asks, curisosity suddenly overwhelming her grief for a few seconds.  Even before I can answer her excitement begins to fade.

"Something like that, though in this case its just my old partner asking me to come poke around a bit,"  I fight back a faint smile.

"With the unpleasantness on the shoreline,"  I begin, changing the subject, "I heard your husband's name come up, said he walked with a limp like the girl on the shore would have.  Pretty messed up ankle."

Rose nods again, seeming on the verge of tears once more, "Yes, he walked with a limp.  Old fishing accident crushed his ankle.  Something broke on the deck of his boat, swung around and caught him against the deck."

I'm quiet for a few seconds, thinking about what I should do.  Finally I offer, "I can't promise I'll find anything, but if you're willing to tell me about the last time you saw Wilson, and about anything strange in the week or so leading up to his dissappearance, I'll keep my eyes pealed."

"//Thank you,//" She gushes as if I just promised to have her husband home in time for supper.  

She looks arounf frantically, as if trying to figure out what to say, then she begins speaking in a rush, "Last time I saw him, he was headed for the docks, said one of his friends was coming in and he was going to help unload the catch.  I tried to l

passages.name: Your room.
passages.content:
Looking around my room, I see that it's small and simple.  Surprisingly though, it also appears to be passably clean. 

Dominating the space near the small window is a small bed, barely a cot.  A flat pillow and a folded blanket rests on the thin mattress.

At the bed's foot, in place of a dresser is a simple chest.  It looks sturdy, but there's no sign of any way to lock it.

Closer to where I am standing, there is a small table, and smaller stool against the west wall.  On it's surface is a small oil lamp and a half full box of wooden matches.

//In this modern age, it's hard to believe people still live like this.//  The thought fleetingly crosses my mind.

<<if $Settled is "Not Played">>I spend several minutes inspecting the room, checking the view from the window, inspecting the lock on the door, and seeing if there is anything else of note in the room.

Out the window is a nearly two story drop into a mud caked alley.  The only view is of the dark and foreboding wood a short distance beyond the edge of town.

The room's door isn't as sturdy as I would like, but using the key to lock it, I find that at least the lock feels secure.  

Unlocking the door once more, I continue my inspection of the room.

I open the chest, looking at it's empty interior as if seeking some kind of answer.  But if there are answers to be had, I see no sign of them there.

Pressing on the mattress, I find it to be far too soft for my liking, but I suppose it will have to do for my stay in Wanton Cove.

It doesn't take me long to unpack my things.  I place the letter Peter gave me at the bottom of the trunk along with a folder full of my personal notes from the 'Faceless' case, my clothes on top of it all.  

I take a deep breath, patting under my arm to feel the .38 Special I have holstered there under my duster.  I don't honestly expect to need it, but I find it comforting to have all the same.<<set $Letter to "Not Played">><<set $Settled to "Played">><<endif>

passages.content_zh:
一個月後。

收音機大聲播放著，車主駕車駛向目的地。

拼布殺人魔依然逍遙法外，恐懼仍緊攫這片地區。收音機裡的聲音急促地播報著第十二起大規模謀殺案，喇叭僅傳來些微靜電雜音。截至目前，警方已從八個犯罪現場發現的屍塊中辨識出近三十名受害者。關於所有犯罪現場的總受害人數，警方不是尚未掌握，就是選擇不公開。偵探彼得·鄧特爾被引述說：「我們正追查所有可能的線索，努力辨識受害者身份。這與幾年前發生的某起事件有驚人的相似之處，我們在那方面已有一些頗有希望的線索。」警方建議市民夜間留在家中，若必須外出，切勿獨行。

可能相關的消息，超過一百——

他厭惡地咕噥一聲，將車停靠路邊，打上停車檔。

「情況如何？」彼得·鄧特爾下車時詢問附近的警官。

「他們抓到他了。」年輕警官說道，眼睛瞪得有點過大。

這小子嚇壞了，彼得意識到，打賭$PlayerName現在會不惜一切代價想待在這裡。

「抓到誰？」彼得問，沒完全跟上思路，「這不是另一個拼布殺人魔的現場嗎？」

「是現場，」年輕警官開口，但彼得終於明白對方在說什麼，「你是說他們抓到了拼布殺人魔？！？」

年輕警官只是點頭。

彼得幾乎是跑向一群警察把守巷口、阻止旁人進入的位置。

「長官，」一名壯碩警察在彼得開口時走上前，「你不該來這裡。」麥克說道，他是彼得認識十多年的好警察。

「你他媽在說什麼，我是這案子的負責人，我本來就該在這裡。」

麥克明顯吞了口口水，「我攔不住你，」這體格厚實的男人說道，眼中帶著擔憂，「但我真的認為你該迴避這次，讓別人告訴我們發現了什麼，而不是——」

肯定比往常更糟，麥克不是會讓事情影響情緒的人。

彼得揮手打斷他，推開人群前進。在巷子的封閉空間裡，濃重的血腥鐵鏽味瀰漫空氣中。

清晨的陽光角度恰好，照亮了狹窄的巷道，大片以鮮血繪成的怪異符號塗抹在他周圍的牆上。

幾名警察聚集在不遠處，擋住了他望向巷子更深處的視線。

肯定是屍體，彼得暗自想著，瞬間好奇他們是否已逮捕了拼布殺人魔，或者他仍在附近某處？

從兩名警察之間擠過去，彼得首次看見謀殺現場。

過去一個月見識過這麼多可怕場景後，他能看出幾乎一切都與其他現場如出一轍。

唯一的不同卻違反常理。

「$PlayerName，你在這裡做什麼？」他問那個站在屍體旁、腳踝深陷血泊中的男人。話一出口，他便意識到自己所見的景象極度不對勁。

彼得瞪大雙眼，伸手去拔腰間的槍，以誇張的緩慢速度抽出史密斯威森麥格農手槍。

「彼得！我一直在等你！」$PlayerName帶著詭異的微笑說道，「我的女主人會非常高興你終於來加入我們了！」

他無法強迫自己將武器指向昔日搭檔、最老的朋友。彼得張口想說話，想問為什麼，但話語哽住，在舌尖夭折，因為他第一次看清了那具屍體。

這具屍體尚未完成，他們一定是在他展示這具屍體的過程中打斷了他，那層傳統的皮膚才剛開始縫合到臉上。彼得幾乎立刻認出了那張臉。比他記憶中更女性化，但無疑是Tom的臉。

「她很美，不是嗎？」$PlayerName從站立處輕聲說道，「我們現在叫她Tanya。自從我們的女主人——先驅者——降臨後，她就乞求成為她獻禮的核心。」他的聲音帶著令人難忘的特質，「這是最後的獻禮，現在我們的工作可以開始了。」

開始

passages.name: Try to reason with it.
passages.content:
<<if ($PlayerStreetwise + $PlayerCharisma) gte 11>>"Hey there friend,"  I try to make my voice as soothing as I can, "I've got the book right here,"  I pat the front of my jacket to emphasize my point, "And I'll be happy to give it to you, but maybe in exchange, you can answer a couple of questions for me."

I take her wary expression as encouragement, "We can help each other a bit maybe?"

She nods slowly, her too large eyes never leaving my own as she does so, "Neeeeeed booooook."

"And you can have it in just a minute,"  I reassure her, "But first, can you tell me if you want the book for you, or for someone else?"

A hint of anger flashes across her features at the question, or perhaps the phrasing of it, "Meeee, NEEEED booook."

"Ok, you're the one who needs the book, not someone else,"  You force your friendly smile to stay in place on your face, "Just one, maybe two more questions, then you can have it.  Ok?"

I can tell that she's already getting antsy, but she nods.  Her hands flex and all I can think for a second is how sharp those claws look, "Ok, second question, can you tell me what you need the book for?"

The madness in her eyes seems to well up, threatening to reach out and overwhelm me.  <<if $Madness gte 3>>I'm almost certain that she's hearing the same voices in her head that I am.<<elseif $Madness gte 1>>I wonder to myself if the voices whispering in the back of my mind are the same as the ones she is hearing.<</if>>

"Neeeed,"  The raw sound of her voice makes me want to cringe, "Fix meeeeeee."

I reach into my jacket, taking hold of the book.  I silently debate with myself if I should give the book over as I promised, or if I should try to keep it.  Deep down, I know keeping it would ensure a physical confrontation with this creature that I am uncertain I could win.  

Instead, I ask one more question to buy myself a few more precious seconds, "This book can fix you?  Make you human again?"

"Yeeeeeeessssss,"  The creature

Four years earlier.

//Walking towards the alley, I could see pale faced cops standing on the sidewalk, blocking the way into the dimly lit alleyway.

As I approach, another cop rushes out of the alley before bending double and emptying the contents of his stomach onto the street.

I take a step to one side to avoid the worst of the mess, and meet the eye of Captain Steiner.

"Detective <<print $PlayerName2>>,"  Captain Steiner meets my eyes.  He's clearly rattled by whatever is in the alley, "Hope you have a stronger stomach than the rest of us."  His tone is distant, like he's disassociating himself from whatever they found.

"Show me the way,"  I say. I can already smell the coppery scent of blood on the air.  //Something about this feels far too familiar, like this has all happened before in a half remembered nightmare.//

Captain Steiner nods, then waves for me to follow.

The alley is dark, and narrow, not quite wide enough to drive an automobile down.  The only light I can see down the damp corridor between the two warehouses is coming from a couple of flashlights a short distance ahead.

Without really thinking about it, I pull my flashlight and thumb it on, lighting the way.

When I see a hint of blood lazily running from down the alley, pooling in a puddle a couple of paces ahead, I make a point of stepping around it. 

"Must be a lot of blood?"  I make it half question, half statement.  Captain Steiner turns his head to meet my eyes.  I'd swear that he's three shades lighter than when we entered the alley.  He doesn't say anything as he continues towards the crime scene.

passages.content_zh:
四年前。

走向那條小巷時，我能看見臉色蒼白的警察站在人行道上，擋住了通往昏暗巷弄的去路。

當我走近時，另一名警察從巷子裡衝了出來，隨即彎下腰，將胃裡的東西全吐在街上。

我向旁邊踏了一步，避開最糟的嘔吐物，對上了Steiner隊長的目光。

"帶路吧，" 我說。我已經能聞到空氣中那股銅鏽般的血腥味。這一切感覺太過熟悉，彷彿這一切都曾在某個半記半忘的噩夢中發生過。

Steiner隊長點點頭，揮手示意我跟上。

巷子又暗又窄，窄到幾乎無法讓一輛汽車通過。在這條位於兩座倉庫之間、潮濕的走廊深處，唯一的光源來自前方不遠處的幾支手電筒。

我幾乎沒怎麼思考，就抽出自己的手電筒，拇指一按，照亮了前路。

當我看到一絲血跡從巷子深處緩緩流出，在前方幾步遠的地方匯成一灘時，我特意繞了過去。

"血一定很多吧？" 我的話半是疑問，半是陳述。Steiner隊長轉過頭來看著我。我敢發誓，他的臉色比我們進巷子時又蒼白了三分。他什麼也沒說，繼續朝犯罪現場走去。

接近犯罪現場時，地上的血跡已多到無法避免踩到。到底有多少受害者？

我開始放慢腳步，一種不安的感覺籠罩著我，彷彿我的皮膚都不太對勁。我的目光移向其他警官手電筒照著的方向。

牆上有著奇怪的符號，用寬闊的紅色筆觸繪成。我花了幾秒鐘才意識到那是血。

我不知道自己在那裡站了多久，盯著那些符號，但我意識到Steiner隊長正期待地看著我，彷彿在等我說些什麼。

我深吸一口氣，強迫自己將手電筒照向那個似乎所有人都避開視線的地方——那兩幅血紅壁畫之間、巷子的正中央。

有好幾次心跳的時間，我的大腦彷彿無法理解眼前所見。

躺在一片小小血海中央的，是一具瓷器般蒼白的身體，與周圍的深紅色形成了幾乎令人震驚的對比。

注意著腳下，耳中心跳如雷，我緩緩靠近現場。

passages.name: Try to scare some information out of him.
passages.content:
I adjust my jacket, allowing Cobb a brief glimpse at my .38 Special.

"I don't take well to threats,"  I say simply.  

<<if ($PlayerStrength + $PlayerIntimidation) gte 10>>'Shit,"  Cobb hisses, a hint of panic in his eyes.  "it's dangerous round here.  That's all I'm saying."  He says in a rush.

"How about you help me out a little then?"  I ask in my most reasonable voice.

'Sure, whatever."  His eyes flicker to my jacket where my .38 Special is hidden, "What do you want to know?"  

Cobb's eyes dart from one spot to another around the area, as if worried about who might see him speaking with me.  It makes me wonder who's keeping him on such a short leash.

"You said that this town doesn't like outsiders?"  I inquire.

"We're not used to unfamiliar faces, know what I mean?"  He replies.

I can tell that his body language is growing increasingly agitated, "Ok."  I say, "Maybe you can tell me something else.  I'm looking for a guy, mid twenties, short red hair and green eyes.  Athletic.  His name is Tom, and he would have been in Wanton Cove about a month ago." 

I can tell from his expression that he knows exactly who I'm talking about.

"You seen him?"  I make it more of a statement than question.

"No, I haven't seen anyone like that."  Cobb says quickly.

I glower at him, stepping closer.

Cobb chooses this moment to make a break for it, turning quickly on his heel and making a run for it.

<<if ($PlayerStrength + $PlayerBrawl) gte 13>>He barely takes a step before a grab hold of his collar, slamming him against the building's side.

"Lets try this again,"  I say coldly, "Do you know what happened to Tom?"

"He came through here a little over a month ago," Cobb barely puts up a struggle as I keep him pinned to the wall. "He was asking a lot of questions, and some of the locals didn't like it."  I can hear a //lot// of fear in his voice, and it rattles me a little to see someone crack like this so quickly.

passages.name: Travel to the Ruins.
passages.content:
<<if $RuinsFound is "Not Played">>Past Vick's home, I find a trail, right where Vick had said it would be.  I glance over my shoulder toward Vick's cabin one last time, then take my first step down the narrow trail.  //He urged me not to go, that the place is dangerous enough that even he wont go there.//  But in spite of my misgivings, my resolve is set.

The muddy ground squashes and oozes beneath my boot, sucking at my heel and threatening to pull the boot from my foot.  I've traveled for several minutes, my head bowed to avoid the worst of the rain pattering down through the canopy above, when my boot strikes solid ground.  In that moment, I become aware of the fact that I have not heard the sounds of a single animal, or even of a single insect for a while now.

Focusing my eyes, I see a flat but disjointed stone beneath me.  I raise my head, turning my attention to the area around myself.  At first I do not notice anything, but after a few seconds I start to realize that I'm not looking at just trees and plants anymore. I begin to notice rocky pillars and stone work.  Buildings that each appear to have been carved from a single massive stone loom all around me.  

At first I find myself impressed as I take in the dozen or so buildings immediately around myself, but then I start to realize that there are more, far larger buildings just a little bit further along the path, some stretching up into the leafy canopy above, //Who, or what could have built all of this?//  For just a second, everything that has happened since I arrived in Wanton Cove fades away and I simply marvel at the ruins around me, //It's unlike anything I've ever seen, or even heard of before.//

I take a few steps toward the closest building.  What I had first assumed was rock and stonework takes on a different feel when I can see it clearer.  While the material still appears to be stone, it has a smooth, polished appearance, and reflects the light in a faint rainbow much like the inter

Her eyes seem to look through mine into my very soul, the seconds tick on into nearly a minute, making me uncomfortable in the growing silence.

passages.name: Ask Mr Morichi about the history of Wanton Cove.
passages.content:
"You've lived in Wanton Cove your entire life?"  I ask.

"Unfortunately so,"  Mr Morichi replies with a wry grin, "Though not a day goes by that I dont wonder why I stay."

"Having lived here for so long, I'm sure you know things about the island that an outsider wouldnt,"  Even as I ask the question, I wonder what kind of insights I might gain from his knowledge of the island.

"Not really,"  He dashes my hopes, but then I can see the faint smile at the corner of his lips as he watches me out of the corner of his eye, "But I'll see what I can share."<<if $Madness gte 2>>

//Perhaps there are secrets in his words, and all I must do is read the story between the lines to understand?//<<endif>>

He spends nearly a minute seemingly in thought, sipping lightly at his tea, before he finally speaks, "Wanton Cove is a fishing town, without the fishing this town would be empty within a year.  But then, you probably had already figured that much."

"Hmmm,"  Mr Morichi sighs, "Things you may not know though."

He sips at his tea again, "There is a small cave near the woodline up here on the bluff, that cave reaches a long way back and takes you into a huge cave that many of the locals refer to as the cathedral.  I used to play in that cave occasionally as a child, but I have lost track of where exactly the opening is in recent years.  Its nearly impossible to find if you don't know where to look."

Looking thoughtful again, he then adds, "For the last couple of years there has been talk about a man living out in the forest.  I hear he is dangerous and has attacked a couple of people in the past, though its always 'a friend of a friend,' or 'someone I know' instead of first hand accounts, so that may just be hearsay.  The thing that makes him insteresting however is that no one knows who he is, or how he came to be on this island."

A slight enigmatic smile finds its way onto Mr Morichi's lips.

The silenct stretches for a few seconds, punctuated by the sof

passages.name: I feel drawn to investigate the area further.
passages.content:
I walk along the bluff slowly, searching the area for anything that seems out of place, something I have done at a hundred different crime scenes in the past.

I find myself instinctively walking about twenty paces away from the edge, the steep slope leading down only a couple of paces from where I am walking.  Dropping off at about a thirty degree angle, it breaks off sharply where the cliff falls off into the ocean.

Riverlets of water run down the slope, leaving muddy little paths in the ground that all channel off into the ocean below.

<<if ($PlayerInvestigation + $PlayerIntelligence) gte 11>>//So, lets walk through this,// I think to myself.

I glance back at town, following the trail leading up to the bluff with my eyes, //He would have walked up here, likely from the Church,// I reason.  My attention settles on the area around myself, //From here, he would have been able to see everything in the area, anything this side of the treeline, all the way down to the far side of Wanton Cove.//

//It would have been difficult for someone to observe the suicide if Pastor Erkon was paying any attention at all to what was going on around himself.//

I turn towards the cliff, looking at the muddy slope, //How did he 'stand there for a few seconds,' as Mr Jackobson said?  That slope has to be slick all the time from this rain.//

I grimace, looking up and down the slope, trying to see any spot where it would have been safe to stand, //Its possible that the slope has changed over the last few years, but I don't see any sign of major changes to the slope recently though.//

For several seconds, I consider what I'm looking at, then decide on a different tact, walking towards the treeline, //Mr Jackobson mentioned that he watched Pastor Erkon jump while walking along the treeline.//

When I reach the treeline, I look towards the cliff,  //Can't see anything from here, the rise of the bluff, combined with the slope leading to the cliff makes it impossible to

passages.name: I had ever worked
passages.content:
Four years earlier.

//Walking towards the alley, I could see pale faced cops standing on the sidewalk, blocking the way into the dimly lit alleyway.

As I approach, another cop rushes out of the alley before bending double and emptying the contents of his stomach onto the street.

I take a step to one side to avoid the worst of the mess, and meet the eye of Captain Steiner.

"Detective <<print $PlayerName2>>,"  Captain Steiner meets my eyes.  He's clearly rattled by whatever is in the alley, "Hope you have a stronger stomach than the rest of us."  His tone is distant, like he's disassociating himself from whatever they found.

"Show me the way,"  I say. I can already smell the coppery scent of blood on the air.

Captain Steiner nods, then waves for me to follow.

The alley is dark, and narrow, not quite wide enough to drive an automobile down.  The only light I can see down the damp corridor between the two warehouses is coming from a couple of flashlights a short distance ahead.

Without really thinking about it, I pull my flashlight and thumb it on, lighting the way.

When I see a hint of blood lazily running from down the alley, pooling in a puddle a couple of paces ahead, I make a point of stepping around it. 

"Must be a lot of blood?"  I make it half question, half statement.  Captain Steiner turns his head to meet my eyes.  I'd swear that he's three shades lighter than when we entered the alley.  He doesn't say anything as he continues towards the crime scene.

As we near the crime scene, the blood on the ground has become so abundant that it's impossible not to step in it as we approach. //Just how many victims are there?// 

I begin to slow, an uneasy feeling settling over me, as if my skin doesn't fit quite right.  My eyes travel towards where the other officer's flashlights are pointed.

There are strange symbols, painted in broad red swaths of color along the walls.  It takes me a few seconds to realize that it's blood.

I do

passages.name: Anne Interior
passages.content:
The house is sparsely decorated and well cleaned.  Stark white walls show no indication of ever having pictures hung on them with the exception of a large painting of Lady Anne herself resting above the fireplace.  The center of the large room has very little in the way of furnishings.  A table, at about knee height separates an expensive looking chair, plush and shining of well polished leather, and a small couch that appears heavily worn from long use.

The chair’s back faces the simple stone hearth surrounding a crackling fire.  For just a second, I imagine Lady Anne sitting in that chair, cast in silhouette as flames crackle behind her.

As if the thought is an invitation, Lady Anne takes the chair, then motions me towards the couch.

<<set $random to random(1,4)>><<if $random is 4>>As I reluctantly take the offered, there's a sound from the next room over.

//Sounds like someone washing dishes, or putting them away.//  The thought drifts through my mind, to be forgotten almost as quickly as it arrived.

<<elseif $random is 3>>Settling into the chair, I see a man out of the corner of my eye.  I have only a split second to realize that he is naked other than some form of collar around his neck.  

I quickly turn my head to look towards him, but he is already gone.  I can faintly hear the sound of footsteps retreating out of sight.

<<elseif $random is 2>>"Husband!  Refreshments for our guest."  Lady Anne barks the words like an order.  

"Yes dear!"  A meek, feminine voice replies, "Right away dear!"

I'm just settling into the offered chair when a naked man walks into the room carrying a tray with two glasses of what appears to be water on it.  He sits the tray on the table, easily within Lady Anne's reach, but far enough away from me that I'd have to either get up, or lean quite a ways to reach it.

"Will there be anything else you need my dear?"  Lady Anne's husband says softly.  I can see his eyes downcast, his slightly too skinny body hel

passages.name: Ruins24
passages.content:
<<if $GooCounter is 0>>A sound like thunder crossed with a cat trying to eject a hairball pulls my attention down the road I'm about to cross.  

Not thirty paces from me I can see two creatures.  Humanoid, like a frog a fish and a human all got together to have some horrific bastard child.  <<if $Read gte 4>>//Deep Ones.//  The thought settles unpleasantly in my mind.<</if>>  My pace falters and I quickly assess my options.  

Trying not to draw unwanted attention to myself, I step closer to the nearest building in an attempt to conceal myself.  

For a couple of minutes I remain, listening to the //things// make their unnatural guttural sounds at one another.  They seem to be searching for something, though thankfully their search does not draw them any closer to myself.

One of the creatures suddenly draws itself to it's full imposing height, it's head swiveling as it searches for something.  Then it makes a deep huffing sound before both of the creatures quickly move off.

//Somethings got them spooked, and if they're spooked I don't want to stick around to find out what spooked them.//  Choosing a direction that shouldn't intercept their path, I hurry away.

<<set $GooCounter += 1>><<elseif $GooCounter is 1>>When I look down the stone path between two of these strange buildings, I freeze in place, my hand instinctively reaching for my .38 Special, "Shit,"  I mutter to myself.

Looking back at me is <<if $Read gte 4>>a Deep One, <<else>>a massive fish-man, <</if>>it's gray-green scales glistening wetly.    I can see it's body tense, it's too large black eyes blinking one at a time.  

It makes a soft hissing sound that I can hear even from as far away as I am currently standing.
  
Deep in my gut, I know this //thing// is about to attack.  My .38 Special clears it's holster beneath my arm as I prepare myself for what is to come.

But it doesn't attack.  Instead it remains tensed for a couple more seconds before looking to one side.  It's head
passages.content_zh:
[IF $GooCounter is 0]一陣像是雷鳴混合著貓咪試圖咳出毛球的聲音，將我的注意力拉向我正要穿越的那條道路。

離我不到三十步遠的地方，我能看見兩隻生物。人形，就像青蛙、魚和人類全部混在一起，生出了某種可怕的雜種。[IF $Read gte 4]深潛者。這個念頭令人不快地在我腦中浮現。[/IF]我的腳步遲疑了，我迅速評估我的選擇。

我試著不引起不必要的注意，靠近最近的建築物，試圖隱藏自己。

我待了幾分鐘，聽著那些東西彼此發出不自然的喉音。牠們似乎在尋找什麼，不過謝天謝地，牠們的搜索並沒有讓牠們更靠近我。

其中一隻生物突然挺直了牠那令人畏懼的全身高度，頭部轉動著搜尋某物。然後牠發出一聲深沉的噴氣聲，接著兩隻生物都迅速離開了。

有什麼東西嚇到牠們了，而如果連牠們都被嚇到，我可不想留下來搞清楚是什麼嚇到了牠們。我選擇了一個應該不會與牠們路徑交錯的方向，匆匆離去。

[ELSEIF $GooCounter is 1]當我望向兩棟奇特建築之間的石徑時，我僵在原地，手本能地伸向我的.38特製左輪。「該死，」我喃喃自語。

回望著我的是[IF $Read gte 4]一隻深潛者，[ELSE]一個巨大的魚人，[/IF]牠灰綠色的鱗片濕漉漉地閃著光。我能看到牠的身體緊繃，牠那過大的黑色眼睛一次眨動一隻。

牠發出一聲輕柔的嘶嘶聲，即使我站在目前這麼遠的距離也能聽見。

在我內心深處，我知道這東西就要發動攻擊了。我的.38特製左輪從腋下的槍套中抽出，我為即將到來的事情做好了準備。

但牠並沒有攻擊。相反地，牠又保持緊繃了幾秒鐘，然後看向一側。牠的頭轉回來看我，接著又再次轉向一側，隨後牠突然轉身，朝相反方向離開了。

那到底是怎麼回事？我剛有時間思考，就注意到遠處傳來輕微的、喋喋不休的胡言亂語聲。

我極度想避免與那隻觸手怪物再次對峙，於是也趕緊離開這個區域。我已經走了一段不短的距離，腦中才閃過一個念頭：那個[IF $Read gte 4]深潛者[ELSE]東西[/IF]是害怕那隻觸手怪物嗎？還是這裡有更多我沒看出來的內情？

[ELSE]某種大型物體移動的聲音將我的注意力拉向附近的一棟建築——如果這些結構真能稱得上是傳統意義上的建築的話？——我看到裡面有動靜。

我謹慎地靠近，試圖看清裡面是什麼。

在黑暗的內部隱約可見的是[IF $Read gte 4]一隻深潛者。那魚人蹲伏得很低，牠帶刺的鰭威脅性地張開。[ELSE]一隻既非人也非魚的生物，厚實的灰綠色鱗片覆蓋著人形的軀體。牠的指尖和腳趾可見猙獰的利爪。然而，牠身上最不像人類的特徵，是那滿口剃刀般鋒利的牙齒和巨大的黑色眼睛。[/IF]

那生物像現在這樣彎腰低伏，掃視著建築內部，偶爾發出一聲輕微的、帶喘息的嘶嘶聲。

我不想引起牠的注意，於是我從建築物旁慢慢後退。牠在做什麼？我納悶著，不確定牠是在尋找某物、獵捕某物，還是牠的存在有某些我根本無法理解的其它原因。

[/IF][繼續。]

passages.name: Ask Nadia about your transformation.
passages.content:
Unable to think of a better way to approach the conversation, I come straight out an ask, "My chest hurts and my cock is shrinking,"  I nearly choke on the words as I say them.

Nadia maintains her friendly expression for all of about half a second before bursting out laughing, "Ah!"  She starts when she can finally breathe again, "If you aren't a crisp fresh ocean breeze on a muggy day!"  

<<if $Madness gte 3>>//As if your amusement has anything to do with the question.  You're reveling in my circumstances!//  The thought crosses my mind with the subtlty of a clap of thunder.  It takes me only a second to stomp the thought down.<<elseif $Madness is 2>>//What //do// you know about all of this?//  I feel like a dark cloud settles suddenly over my soul, trying to smother me.  //Can I even trust what you say?//<<elseif $Madness is 1>>My eyes flicker to the rafters, searching for any sign of unnatural shadows //SHE may be listening.//<<else>><<endif>>

Her smile seems to light up her face, but over the next few seconds, something sad seems to creep into her eyes, "Not sure exactly what causes it,"  She starts, her tone soft, almost conspiratorial, "But this town, or maybe this island, seems to change people."<<if $Mutation gte 4>>

She fidgets slightly, then reaches for her collar, pulling it back slightly to reveal a path of gray pebbly skin along her collar, "Ran into something in the dark a few months back, and next day I found this,"  She confesses, her eyes seeming to linger on your own mutations, "Guessing you ran into that same something."  She gives me a sympatheitc look, "I do my best to not be judgemental, and I make an effort to avoid drawing attention to things like what's happening to you, especially when,"  She trails off, moving her collar back up to cover the deformity, "When I'm in the same boat."<</if>>

passages.content_zh:
[IF $CharHP lte 1][/IF][IF $DeepFight is "Played" && $random gte 5]附近，我看到那隻巨大的生物——一個灰綠色的魚人——擺出了攻擊姿態。

它的身軀在雨中閃著濕光，令人不安地盯著我，一隻眼睛緩慢地眨動，比另一隻慢了那麼一秒。

一股冰冷的恐懼沉入我的腹部，我那把可靠的.38特製手槍熟悉的重量，對於我毫不懷疑即將到來的事情，幾乎帶不來什麼安慰。

彷彿感知到我的覺悟，它咧開那張長滿剃刀般利齒的嘴。發出最初引起我注意的那種聲音，這生物朝我猛撲過來。

[ELSEIF $random lte 3 && $Day gte 5]動靜將我的注意力引向一個病態蒼白的形體，它正朝我的方向移動。轉身面向它，我看見了某種駭人的東西。

過寬的眼睛瞪視著我，它那巨大的嘴巴張開，露出雙排針狀的牙齒。

在本能地伸手去掏我的.38特製手槍的同時，這怪物發起了衝鋒。

[ELSE][IF $GooCounter is 0]在其中一個倉庫附近，我看到一個人影站在陰影中，注視著我。

我停下腳步，手伸向我的.38特製手槍，它的存在給我一絲安慰。幾乎就在我這麼做的同時，那個人影動了，他的步態和舉止在我腦後敲響了警鐘。

當我的.38特製手槍剛抽出槍套時，那人幾乎已經到了我面前。

[ELSEIF $GooCounter is 1]在碼頭上，我看到一個人影正看著我。我分不清是男是女，但那個人影散發出的怒意幾乎是種有形之物。

我的手伸向我的.38特製手槍，幾乎就在同一瞬間，我看到一抹微笑觸及了那人影的嘴唇。

[ELSEIF $GooCounter gte 2]我聽到一聲靴子踩在木製碼頭上的聲響，就在我視線邊緣之外。

本能啟動，我向側邊踏出半步，同時轉身面向聲音來源，我的手以熟練的輕鬆感找到了我的.38特製手槍。

我的.38特製手槍剛離開槍套，那人影便朝我衝來。

[/IF]

Below, a dozen naked girls lay on the floor in a daisy chain, each one with their tongue lapping eagerly at the pussy of the next.  My breath catches, a bit of arousal leaving me feeling flushed.

A tremor goes through my body, arousal making me momentarily weak in the knees.  

<<endif>>The big man walks around them like this is the most casual thing in the world.  He seems completely oblivious to the carnal moans filling the room.

<<if $Taint lte 64>>//Perhaps it's normal in this house, but I've rarely even heard of this kind of thing before now.//  I think, half in wo
passages.content_zh:
[IF $random is 1]我不禁感到驚訝，當我看見她們——兩個女孩坐在寬敞客廳裡一張華麗的沙發上。她們倆全身赤裸，雙手在彼此身上游移，親吻著對方。[IF $Taint gte 65]

我的思緒轉向自身的變化。我身體日益女性化的曲線，以及生殖器那駭人的轉變。我顫抖著，混合著幾乎壓抑不住的恐懼，而這恐懼卻彷彿即將被高漲的慾望浪潮淹沒。

我花了漫長的好幾秒鐘，才強迫自己將心思拉回當下。[/IF]

那個高大男人清喉嚨的聲音，讓我意識到自己正明目張膽地盯著眼前的景象。「這邊請，」他重複說道。

我終於注意到這間大客廳裝飾得何其奢華，包括一盞大型黃銅吊燈，高懸上方，掛著一排散發柔和光芒的蠟燭。

當那個氣勢逼人的男人打開房間遠側的一扇門時，他示意我進去。

我深吸一口氣，[ELSEIF $random is 2]穿過寬敞的客廳，一縷陽光從面朝海洋的大窗戶濾進來，在上方的黃銅吊燈上閃爍。

下方，十幾個赤裸的女孩在地板上躺成一個雛菊鏈，每個人都急切地用舌頭舔舐著下一個人的陰部。我屏住呼吸，一絲興奮讓我感到臉頰發燙。

[IF $Taint gte 65]有那麼一瞬間，我發現自己陷入遐想，想像自己成為她們中的一員，急切地舔舐一個女人的陰戶，同時另一個人用她的嘴取悅我。

一陣顫慄掠過我的身體，興奮讓我膝蓋一時發軟。

[/IF]那個大個子男人繞過她們，彷彿這是世界上最平常的事。他似乎完全沒注意到充斥房間的肉慾呻吟。

[IF $Taint lte 64]也許在這房子裡這很正常，但在此之前，我甚至很少聽說過這種事。我半是驚奇、半是震驚地想著。

[/IF]我小心翼翼地注意腳步，目光卻幾乎無法離開眼前這情色場景，跟隨著他。

當他打開房間遠側的門時，我只停頓了一秒，然後[ELSEIF $random is 3]我睜大了眼睛，看見兩位赤裸的女士，兩人都手膝著地，像動物一樣被第三個握著皮帶的女人牽著在房間裡繞行。

其中一個女孩朝我看來，我預期會看到恐懼，或是崩潰之人那種空洞的眼神。然而，我所看到的比這兩者都更讓我震撼——她的臉上閃耀著混合了興奮與快樂的神情。

[IF $Taint gte 65]這就是我正在變成的模樣嗎？我的雙膝發軟，興奮與厭惡在我體內交戰。

[/IF]不知該如何是好，我急忙加快腳步追上那個高大男人。

他從鼻尖朝我瞥了一眼，眉頭暫時皺起。然後他轉過身去，打開身旁的門，示意我進去。

[ELSE]當我被領著穿過這棟大宅時，我沒有看到其他人。然而，我知道房子裡並非只有我們。在我周圍，我能聽到輕柔的興奮呻吟，其間還夾雜著遠處釋放的高喊。

隨著想像力佔了上風，我感到自己的慾望也在上升，腦海中充滿了那些在我視線之外必定正在發生的畫面。

我猛地搖了搖頭，意識到自己已經落後那個高大男人一段距離，於是加快腳步趕上。

男人停下腳步，他的目光明顯對你的存在感到不贊同。他甚至沒有看，便伸出手握住門把。門無聲地打開，他示意我進去。
[/IF][我踏進裡頭的小房間。]

passages.name: Ask about the Morichi Family
passages.content:
<<if ($PlayerEttiquette + $PlayerCharisma) gte 11>>"It’s obvious that you know the area, and the people who live in Wanton Cove, fairly well,"  I begin, trying to appeal to her vanity, "I was wondering if you could tell me anything about the Morichi family?"

Lady Anne's expression changes subtly, somehow reminding me of how a cat looks when it catches a mouse.  //Am I the mouse?//

"What would you like to know?"  She asks, then waves her hand dismissively, as if my answer wouldn't matter anyway before continuing, “The Morichi family has been here almost as long as the town.  The way Mr Morichi tells it, he’s a direct descendant of Zodak Alhazred, our town’s founder, but that claim is fragile at best.  Seems some whore got pregnant and claimed that the bastard was Alhazred’s,”  Her lip curls in distaste, “But then, a whore will claim anything if they think it will buy them coin.”

“The daughter, blessedly the last of the Morichi line, is a hideous abomination,”  She shivers visibly, though I have to wonder if the act is theatrics given how reserved much of her behavior usually is, “Barely human if you ask me, and she should have been thrown into the sea the day she was born.”  Her lip curls in disgust, “If I had more sense, I’d have done it myself,”  There a deep seated anger in her tone, a hint of hatred in her eyes.  //There's something more to this.//

Then her expression changes, becoming softer with an almost alarming suddenness, “That //thing// he calls a daughter came to my home one night, broke in and tried to attack me.  When my worthless husband tried to stop her, it turned on him.  The twit fellated her, //willingly// so she would leave.”

The abruptness with which she stops speaking, combined with the intensity of her glare, tells me that she is done speaking on the subject.

passages.name: Ask about HER.
passages.content:
Opening my mouth to speak, I prepare to ask about //her// but the words catch in my throat.

I peer into the shadows of the dimly lit tavern, searching for any sign of //her// and wondering if any of the shadows might reach out at any moment to grasp me.  

The sound of Aaron clearing his throat draws my attention back to him, "You look like you could use a drink,"  He states flatly.

"Probably, but not right now,"  You manage, "I was wondering though, when's the next ferry to the mainland coming?"

"With this storm?"  He chuckles drily, pausing for a second to listen to the heavy rain pounding the roof before continuing, "Big storm like this, you can expect it to be a couple of days, maybe as much as a week after the storm passes before we see the ferry again."

<<if $Madness gte 3>>I turn quickly, seeing a shadow shift at the corner of my vision.  There's a table of sailors sitting at the table where my eyes finally fall.  //She's messing with me.  She's playing with my senses, trying to keep me off balance.//

As quickly as I look away, my head jerks back to Aaron again.

<<elseif $Madness gte 2>>Pausing before I reply, I try to watch the edges of my vision.  Worried that //she// might be listening to our conversation.

After a few seconds, I turn my attention back to Aaron.

<<else>>I glance over my shoulder before replying, making sure that none of the shadows in the room seem out of place.

After a second, my attention shifts back to the bartender.

<<endif>>"Anything else going out before then?  Private boat heading for the mainland or anything like that?"  I ask, a hint of desperation creeping into my voice.

"Nope, guess you're stuck with us until this all blows over,"  Aaron smirks, "But if it helps any, I'll waive the room fee if you're stuck in Wanton Cove a couple of extra days."

passages.name: Pursuit
passages.content:
As I run, I can hear angry shouts coming from behind me.  Far too close for comfort, but after a couple of minutes I begin to realize that they aren't really gaining on me either.  //Small mercies.//

<<if ($PlayerPerception + $PlayerIntelligence) gte 13>>I hear the sound of something move in the underbrush just a short distance to my right.  I instinctively alter my headlong flight to avoid whatever it may be.  Not long after, the same thing happens again, and again.  

I don't know how long run fore before my pace devolves into a brisk walk, but I start to think about my situation while struggling to catch my breath.

//Even now, they aren't gaining on me.  Almost every noise I hear is from the right side.  They're trying to herd me towards something, or someone.  Or since I'm effectively running in a big circle, they might be trying to set up some kind of ambush ahead of me,// I realize.  I also come to the quick realization that there's nothing I can do about it.

A twig snap, closer this time, causes me to quicken my pace once more.

<<elseif ($PlayerPerception + $PlayerIntelligence) gte 8>>I can hear sounds to either side of me from time to time.  Each time I instinctively shy away from the sound, trying to avoid any of the cultists during my headlong flight.

After a while, I begin to become aware of something, //I'm being herded.  They aren't really trying to catch me.//  My pace falters as I struggle to breathe, //But why?  And towards what?//

<<else>>As I run, I can hear sounds all around me, each one seeming closer than the last. 

It feels like I run forever, and no matter how much adrenaline is coursing through my body, my pace eventually starts to falter.

<<endif>>I burst from the treeline into a clearing that I instantly recognize.  Vick's cabin lies just a short distance away, and Vick himself is only about a dozen feet in front of me.

世界彷彿籠罩在永恆的迷霧中，濃密得我只能看見一片凝固的灰。

我抬起手臂，舉到面前，卻依然看不見它。

一時興起，我摸了摸自己的臉，只為確認我的手確實在那兒。嗯，當手指拂過臉頰時，我這麼想著。我的皮膚摸起來很柔軟……帶著女性般的觸感。

有東西掠過我後腰的皮膚，我猛地一顫。在觸碰過後，我能感覺到一種濕黏滑膩的殘留感。

我因恐懼而沉重的呼吸聲，驟然打破了寂靜，讓我的心跳加速。

完全不知身在何處，我開始在崎嶇的地面上狂奔，雙手伸向前方，以免撞上什麼東西。

我跌跌撞撞地跑著，這番慌不擇路的逃竄顯得踉蹌不穩。

我以全身的力量猛撞上某種堅硬、毫不留情的物體，肺裡的空氣被撞得擠了出來。

我向後跌倒，臀部重重著地。

從身後的某處，我聽見有人——或者某種東西——正流暢地在灰色迷霧中移動，離我更近了。

我再次手忙腳亂地爬起來，開始摸索面前的牆壁，觸感幾乎完全光滑。我沿著牆面移動，試圖找到它的邊緣，或是某個開口。

我的指尖在牆上找到一道接縫，便順著它向上摸索，直到頭頂高度，接縫轉而水平延伸了大約三英尺。在那距離的盡頭，它又開始筆直向下。

一扇門？我心想，發狂似地摸索著門把該在的位置。

我找到了。

身後的聲響現在更近了，我能感覺到周圍的空氣在流動，幾乎像漩渦般在我身邊翻攪。

轉動門把，我幾乎跌進門內的空間。

我發現自己置身於一個以紅天鵝絨和金邊裝飾的房間。慵懶盤旋的煙霧飄浮在空中，形成奇異且近乎催眠的圖案。

房間裡堆滿了帶金色流蘇的紅色枕頭。

我回頭瞥了一眼，想確認門是否關上——最好還鎖著——卻只看到光滑的牆壁。我困惑地伸出手觸摸牆壁，但找不到任何我剛剛穿過的門的痕跡。

passages.name: Ask Ms Sherwood what she knows about Azumi.
passages.content:
While sitting across from Ms. Sherwood, I get the urge to ask her about Azumi, //If she was involved in the Church back when Azumi was a baby, she might know something,//  I take a moment to gather my thoughts before speaking.

"My interest has been piqued about something, and I thought you might be able to help.  You know who Azumi Morichi is I assume?"

Ms. Sherwood's eyes flicker to one of the crucifix on the wall, and she suddenly looks uneasy.

"Mr Morichi has told me a bit about his //colorful// family history, he's even told me a little bit about his courtship and some of the events that surrounded Azumi's birth."

Across from me, Ms Sherwood subtly crosses herself before absently touching the crucifix at the nape of her neck.

"I've met the girl, she's", . . . you struggle to find the right words, "She's got her own personal demons, but I think behind her //urges// she is a good person."

She looks ready to bolt, her unease is like a physical thing struggling to get away from anything to do with the subject.

Knowing that backing down now almost ensures she won't speak on the subject with me in the future, I push on even though I know it may still the woman's tongue, "Is there any chance you can tell anything you know about Azumi, especially who her mother might have been?

Ms Sherwood remains silent for several heartbeats, then when I am nearly ready to give up on hearing her say anything, she takes a deep breath and speaks, "Margaret Anne, she calls herself Lady Anne."

passages.name: HighTaintEnd3
passages.content:
The scene before me stops me dead in my tracks.  

//Its happening.//

I can barely make out shapes in the fog, though they seem to slip from sight any time I try to focus on any of them.  Instead I direct my attention to the stone table, the altar laid out before me.

On the altar, I can see a man laying on top of a woman who appears to be chained to its surface.  From the angle I am standing at, I can clearly see him penetrating her, thrusting repeatedly as he fucks her cunt.  

Years of habit take over.  I reach into my jacket for my .38 Special, only to find it missing.  Lacking that, I try to force myself forward, but seem to be rooted to the spot.

When he climbs off of her, I can see the woman clearly for the first time.  <<if $MorichiDaughter gte 3>>//Azumi!//  I think in alarm. <<else>>//The hermaphrodite from my dream.//  I think in surprise.  //Azumi?// <</if>>Her expression is glazed, as if she is there in body, but her mind is elsewhere.  From the neck down she is covered in blood from a thousand little cuts across her torso.  My every instinct demands that I help her.

Vick turns toward me, a smile on his thin lips, "You're just in time."  His tone holds a cold satisfaction to it.

My eyes flicker to the altar, then back to Vick.   Suddenly able to move again, I start making my way toward his captive, keeping plenty of distance between Vick and myself.

All the while, shapes move in the fog.  I try to ignore them as best I can, but increasingly I am aware that not all of the shapes are human.  I shake my head, trying to clear my increasingly chaotic thoughts enough to focus on the task at hand.  

"I'm here to help,"  I say, hoping <<if $MorichiDaughter gte 3>>Azumi <<else>>

祭壇上，我看見一名男子壓在一個女人身上，後者似乎被鎖鏈固定在石面。從我站立的角度，能清楚看見他正插入她體內，反覆抽送，操弄著她的陰戶。

Vick轉向我，薄唇勾起微笑：「你來得正是時候。」語調裡透著冰冷的滿足。

在我未見的身後，虛空正更猛烈地拉扯這詭異之地，它同時變得比以往更多，卻又比原有的空無更少。

霧中某處傳來尖叫。短促尖利，驟然而止。是Tanya。我認得她的聲音。

passages.name: Ask about Tom.
passages.content:
"I'm in Wanton Cove looking for someone."  I say, "Young man, mid twenties, short red hair, green eyes, athletic build.  His name's Tom."

<<if ($PlayerPerception + $PlayerIntelligence) gte 8>>I can tell that the question makes her uneasy.  Her eyes narrow and turn downward.  It's obvious that she is carefully weighing her answer. 

"Yeah, I remember him, came through here about a month ago." Nadia pauses, "Haven't seen him for a long while though."

I feel like she's telling only a partial truth, but I let it slide for now.<<else>>She seems to pause for a second before answering.

"Yeah, I remember him, came through here about a month ago." Nadia pauses, "Haven't seen him for a long while though."<</if>>

"Do you know what he did while he was in Wanton Cove?  Where he might have went?" I ask.

<<if $Taint lte 20>>She pauses longer this time, obviously struggling with how she wants to answer, "You seem like a nice guy."  She begins, "You should really leave."  

Her eyes hold a hint of fear in them. "Just get on the ferry in the morning, go back to the mainland and forget about this place."  She begs.

"I can't leave until I find Tom,"  I reply softly.

Nadia lets out a sigh, "I'm sorry that I can't be of any help."  

I can tell that she doesn't want to talk about this any longer.<<else>>"Guess it's too late for you to turn away now."  She says with a tone of defeat in her voice.

Her eyes meet yours, "I don't know what happened to Tom for sure, thought I have my suspicions,"  She says carefully.

<<if ($PlayerPerception + $PlayerIntelligence) gte 10>>I get the distinct impression that she has some pretty strong suspicions as to what happened to Tom.

I keep my mouth shut though, waiting to see what I can learn from her instead of prying for more information.

<<endif>>"A few years back, the pastor of our little Church started looking into,"  She pauses as if struggling to find the right words, "some things."

passages.name: Sensation returns.
passages.content:
My vision swims as I open my eyes.  The events of the last few minutes coming back to me in a rush.  

I sit up too fast, vertigo washing over me for several seconds before I can steady myself.  Somehow I manage to hold down the contents of my stomach, though the acrid bite of vomit is heavy in the back of my throat.

Kneeling on the ground, I begin to take in my surroundings.  I seem to be on some small island.  The ground is a sickly shade of brown and gray, with little bits of brown grass in places.  Everything seems shrouded in a layer of fog, making it difficult to see very far.  

Looking over my shoulder, I see blackness, a thick //nothing// that is blacker than any night.  Just the simple act of looking at it is enough to make my skin crawl.  I swallow hard, //If that's the way out, maybe I'll stay a minute longer.//

Unable to force myself to look at the unnatural darkness any longer, I look around the rest of the small island.  Almost exactly opposite the darkness I can see two small pillars looming in the fog.  

Taking my time, I stand.  I sway on my feet slightly, but stay upright.  Slowly, I walk toward the pillars.

Within just a couple of steps, I can see the pillars more clearly.  Ropes tied tightly around the top and bottom of each before reaching out past the edge of the little island.  //It's an old rope bridge.//  I realize.

When I near the rope bridge, I look closer at the pillars.  Both appear to be made of dark gray stone and are engraved with strange symbols, a few of which I recognize even if I do not know their meaning.

Reaching out, I touch the rope, giving it a gentle tug.  The rope seems sturdy enough, but the motion of the bridge when I pull causes an ominous creaking from somewhere in the fog.

Glancing over my shoulder, I can see no sign of the black void I had seen before, most likely having been shrouded by the fog.  

//Well, fuck,//  I think grimly, then take my first hesitant step onto the bridge.

passages.name: Inquire further about her relationship with her father.
passages.content:
"You said before that your father hates you,"  I begin softly, "Do you really believe that?"

Azumi fidgets, looking uncertain, "No,"  She says softly, leaning close to you.  Her cheek brushes yours, "But he doesn't treat me like he did when I was little."

"You've grown up, relationships change over time,"  I observe.

"I used to crawl into bed with him at night,"  Her voice is barely a whisper, "He'd hold me, tell me that I'm precious and that he loves me,"  She trails off for a little while before continuing, "Then he wouldn't let me come to bed with him anymore."

I wait, hoping that she will continue.  When she doesn't, I prompt her, "What happened?  He seems to care deeply for you, even if he's not sure how best to express it."

She clings to me, tight enough that it hurts. But with the vulnerable look on her face I don't have the heart to pry her loose.

"I was,"  There's a tremor in her voice, then she tries again with only slightly more success, "I was horny."

The implications of that simple statement leaves me feeling shocked, //She would do something like that with her own father?//

"And he wasn't as strong as I was,"  She murmurs against my neck.

//She did, . . . this explains a lot about how awkward their relationship is.//

"The next day, he wouldn't talk to me.  And that night his bedroom door was locked,"  Her voice is full of emotion and I can tell that she is weeping, "I cried all night at his door that night."

As soon as she stops talking, she starts to cry in earnest, her whole body shaking with the intensity of it.

I hold her tight for a long time until she finally settles back down.

When she finally loosens her grip on me, I give her one last affectionate squeeze before opening my mouth to speak again.

passages.name: Ask about her family business.
passages.content:
"I was wondering if you could tell me a little more about your family business?  You said that you run the fishing industry.  Isn't that an odd job for a woman?"

<<if ($PlayerEttiquette + $PlayerCharisma) gte 14>>Lady Anne's eyebrow raises slightly, but my respectful tone must be enough in spite of my ill chosen words, "Yes, I run the fishing industry here in Wanton Cove.  Nearly everything on, and off, of this island is because I allow it to be so,"  There's no arrogance in her voice, just simple statement of fact.

"Every fishing vessel that docks in our cove belongs to me, as do their crews."

A stray thought, her husband and what she may have made him to do any number of the sailors who work for her, crosses my mind like a lightning bolt.

The little smile that plays at her lips makes me wonder if perhaps she knows exactly what I am thinking.

"I've seen ships in and out of the cove since arriving, I take it your business is lucrative?"  I ask cautiously.

"If you are asking if I am rich?  I do very well, but its really more about power now, isn't it?  After all, what good is money if you can't do as you wish."  Her hard eyes bore into mine, seemingly in search of my thoughts.

"I don't know.  It never seemed like anyone I've known with money is happy because of it."  I venture.

"Then they're using it wrong,"  The way she says it makes me shiver, like she's just shared a profound secret with me that is beyond my understanding.

passages.name: Places of Interest on the Island of Wanton Cove
passages.content:
[[return|$return]]

The Barmaid's Daughter:
The Barmaid's Daughter was once the community house of Wanton Cove where nearly all of the settlers lived while the rest of the town was built around it. 

In the fledgling years of the town, the building served as a meeting place for the town.  However, after Zodak Alhazred's death, the building became the tavern you see today, though it was originally known as the Wanton Cove Tavern.  In the mid 1800's, a barmaid won ownership of the establishment in a card game and named it after her daughter Isabella.  In the coming years, the locals jokingly called the tavern 'The Barmaid's Daughter'.  The name stuck, and the name was officially changed several years later.

To this day, the Barmaid's Daughter is still used as a meeting place for the locals, and the upper floor which served as a brothel in the early years of the town now serves as an inn for travelers visiting Wanton Cove.

The Alhazred Mansion:
Once the site of Zodak Alhazred's home.  The house fell into the sea along with its owner during one of the harsh storms that frequently batter the island.  After the house had fallen into the sea, Zodak Alhazred's bastard son took his father's name and built a grand mansion in his father's name.  

While the house has since fallen into disrepair, the current owner is a descendant of our town's founder, Zodak Alhazred.

The Mysterious Fountain:
The fountain that most people notice in the park upon arriving in Wanton Cove predates the town.  Like the scattered ruins on the island, this fountain appears ancient.  

No one knows who built the fountain, or the ruins for that matter.  And the strange engravings on the fountain have proven indecipherable through the years, though many have tried.

The Cathedral:
The Cathedral is not a manmade feature of the island, instead it is a natural formation, one of the many caves that have been found on the island.  

The Cathedral can be found down one of the forest tr

passages.name: Examine the fountain.
Nice part of town.
passages.content:
Unlike the rest of Wanton Cove, the people who live on the East side of town appear to spend at least a little time maintaining their homes and properties.  

An occasional broken fence post, a bit of pealing paint, or a missing shingle mar what would otherwise be an average neighborhood in need of some yard care.

My eyes drift over the houses along the street, following the string of houses to where the road begins to switchback it's way up the steep bluff to the East.

Perched precariously near the edge of the bluff, facing the ocean, is a dark and foreboding mansion.  Like much of the town, the iron wrought fence surrounding the mansion is rusty and twisted.  The dark brown building itself has an air of antiquity around it.  Just looking at the mansion fills me with a sense that something isn't quite right about the scene before me.<<if $CobbIntimidate is "Played" && $Day is 3>>

As I pass a house, someone charges me from beside it.  I only have a split second to realize it is Cobb, the man I met upon first arriving in Wanton Cove.  

Brandishing a length of wood, [[he swings it at me.|Cobb Encounter]]<<elseif $CobbStreetwise is "Played" && $Day is 3>>

"Hey,"  A voice calls softly from beside a house.  

When I look, I see Cobb standing there, [[motioning me towards him.|Cobb Encounter]]<<else>><<if $MotherTalk is "Played" and $AnneCuck is "Played" and $TobyTalk is "Not Played">>I'm nearly past the Anne house when I see the glow of a cigarette near the back of the house.  

I pause, stopping where I'm standing as I try to get a better look at whoever it is.

There, garbed in a simple blue dress is Toby, or Sophie as Lady Anne calls him.  

The feminine man takes another drag off of his cigarette

[[Go talk with Toby.]]

[[Continue on your way.|Nice part of town.]]

<<elseif $SherwoodHouse is "true">>

passages.name: Sherwood Pastor Talk
passages.content:
Gesturing at the Crucifix on the wall, then the Bible on the small table, "You're Christian right?  I'm guessing you used to attend church?"

"I was raised Catholic,"  She seems self conscious as she says it, "And I used to attend church here in town until, . . ."

"Until the Pastor died?"  I prompt.

She nods, "Pastor Erkon,"  She says softly.<<if $Madness gte 2>>


//Every terrible thing on this island seems to be connected to something, though those connections are proving elusive.  How does the Pastor fit into this picture?//

As if in answer, the nearly constant cacaphony of harsh whispers become louder, as if mocking my train of thought.

I do my best to ignore the whispers, but with every passing moment, the task of doing so seems more difficult than the last.<<elseif $Madness is 1>>

//Could the Pastor fit into what is happening here?//  The surreal fealing associated with the thought troubles me, like I'm trying to create fantasy to fill the gaps in what little I //do// seem to know.

Using the reprieve to focus on the here and now, I turn my eyes to Ms Sherwood's own troubled gaze.<<else>><<endif>>

passages.name: Ask Phil about the creatures you've seen in the water.
passages.content:
"I have a question for you about the sea life in this area,"  I begin.

"You're going to need to be a bit specific friend, there's a lot of things in these waters," <<if ($PlayerPerception + $Intelligence) gte 11>>I can see a slight tightness around his eyes before he responds.

<<elseif ($PlayerPerception + $Intelligence) gte 6>>He pauses briefly, as if considering his next words carefully.

<<else>>There's a brief pause before he continues.

<<endif>>"But I'm going to go out on a limb here and say you're going to ask about one of our more exotic specimens."

"Yeah, I saw a body in the water,-"

He cuts me off before I can finish, "The one on the beach the other day?"

"No, another one,"  I can see his expression darken slightly, "I was up on the bluff, I could see a body in the water.  There were a group of //things// that approached the body then pulled it under.  Any idea what they could have been?"

"Hmmm,"  Phil looks thoughtful, "Dark things, about the size of a person?"

<<if $Madness gte 3>>//They're real!  He's seen them too!//  My thoughts stall, //But does he know too much?  Is  he in league with them?//

<<endif>>"Yeah, that's right,"  I reply, subconsciously leaning forward in anticipation of learning more.

"Probably a group of seals.  Not unusual for them to play with something in the water."

His tone suggests that's all the answer he has, but I'm not willing to give up so easily, "No, they had a big fin along their back, looked like webbed feet and hands."

Phil lets out a bark of laughter, "Have you ever seen seals in the water?  Their flippers can easily look like hands or feet at a glance, especially when they are in the water, and the fin was almost certainly one of them just swimming on its side and letting one of its foreflippers break the surface."<<if $Madness gte 3>>
