passages.name: Step inside the shack.
passages.content:
Looking at the inside of the shipbuilder's shack, I can't make any sense of the mess filling the interior.  Scraps of wood, tools, and even several massive logs are all covered in varying thicknesses of sawdust.  

Several oil lamps light the far side of the interior.  For a moment, over the sound of a saw being used, I see a flicker of movement from the lighted portion of the room.

"Hello?"  I call, not wanting to surprise whoever is there.

"C'mon back!"  A gruff voice calls, "And stay out of my way when you get back here, I'm just finishing up!"  

Making my way towards the light, I round a corner in the stacked building materials to see a shirtless man, sawdust matted in the hair of his sweat matted chest.  He doesn't even acknowledge me as he adjusts his glasses and looks down the length of the long strip of wood he had just finished cutting.

Sitting the board down, he looks at me, his eyes hard as he sizes me up.  Without warning, his expression lightens, the creases around his mouth becoming laugh lines as his face splits in a toothy grin.

He quickly wipes his hand off on his trousers, the thrusts it towards me to shake my hand.

I take the offered hand more hesitantly than I intended, and he proceeds to shake my hand in a crushing grip, "A pleasure to meet you.  We don't get many visitors here in Wanton Cove, to what do I owe the pleasure?"

"I'm doing a little favor for an old friend, his son went missing in this area recently, so I'm seeing if I can learn anything."  I tell him.

"Tom?"  The man seems to think for a few seconds, "Red headed kid?  Green eyes, freckles and a bit on the short side?"

"Yeah, that sounds like him,"  I can't help but feel a touch of excitement that I've finally found someone who seems to know something about Tom, "You can't tell me where he might have gone, can you?"

"Nah, I can't,"  The man says, "Mind, I'd like to, but I haven't heard anything about him in a while now,"  He shrugs, turning his attenti

passages.name: Ask Mr Morichi about the Ruins.
passages.content:
"Your family has a history in this town,"  I begin.

Mr Morichi raises an eyebrow, his expression turning amused.

"So I was wondering if you might know a little bit about the ruins?"

He takes a long sip from his cup of tea, then looks down at the half empty cup, "I don't know a lot, but I'll share what I have heard."

For several long seconds, he looks thoughtful, "I don't know anything for sure, just hearsay and cryptic family diaries,"  He looks about to take another sip, but then puts the cup down, "Bear in mind that I don't know how much truth there is to any of this.  I saw an old family diary when I was a child, I have no idea what became of it, and that diary said this island was chosen for the town of Wanton Cove because of something that was already here.  I do not know if that something was the ruins, or something else.  I have also heard rumor that the ruins are not abandoned, that someone or //something// may still reside there,"  He trails off for a second then looks me in the eye, "But regardless, there have always been people who have taken an interest in the ruins for some reason.  And for many who find them it becomes an obsession, like a rot that takes hold of the soul."

Mr Morichi pauses, refilling his teacup and taking a sip before continuing, "I have never visited the ruins themselves, but I've see evidence of whatever once stood here, all over the island,"  He pauses, looking concerned, "If I thought it would persuade you, I would ask you to forget the ruins and not spare another thought for those cursed ruins.  But since I know better, I will instead ask that you be careful if you seek them out."

He is quiet for a long while, sipping at his tea as I think about what he said, "I have seen bad people go to that place and come back worse than before.  And I have seen good people go to that place and I have not seen them again,"  He takes another small sip from his cup, his gaze shifting to the choppy waves in the distance out the

passages.name: Morichi Book
passages.content:
"You know as much as anyone I've met here on Wanton Cove, and you've been more open as well,"  I pull the bundled book out of my jacket. 

Sensing that this could be something important, Mr Morichi quickly moves his teapot to one side of his little table to make room for the book.

"I found this while looking around the island, and I was wondering if you might be able to tell me anything about it?"  With care, I unwrap the book and lay it on the cleared portion of the table.

Mr Morichi lets out a little whistle that seems to hang in the air for an unusually long amount of time, "It has been years since I last laid eyes on this,"  His tone holds almost a reverence, "Might I ask where you procured this?"

I consider refusing to say, but given how open he has been with me, I feel like I owe him the same courtecy, "I found it in the basement of the old Church."

Nodding slowly, "That makes sense,"  His eyes turn upwards to meet mine, "Pastor Erkon purchased this book from me a few short months before his untimely demise.  It became his obsession if what people have said is true."

Mr Morichi reaches out, almost touching the book, but then wrapping the cloth back around it instead to cover the old tome, "It supposedly belonged to Zodak Alhazred and was one of the few things that survived when part of the bluff collapsed, taking him and his home into the sea's embrace,"<<if $Madness gte 3>>

//So this comes back to Mr Morichi and his family,//  My thoughts trail off into dark places, the voice murmering in my mind sounds suddenly pleased.<<elseif $Madness gte 1>>

//It all returns here.  How could this be related to Mr Morichi, does he have some secret part in all of this?//<<endif>>

His mannerisms become almost dismissive as he nudges the book towards you as if its an unclean thing he does not wish to touch, "It always made me uncomfrotable, so when the pastor showed up at my doorstep asking if he could buy it, I gladly sold it to him.  Though come to
passages.content_zh:
「你對萬頓灣的了解，不亞於我在這裡遇見過的任何人，而且你也更坦率。」我從外套裡取出那本包裹著的書。

Morichi先生察覺這可能是重要物品，迅速將茶壺移到他小桌的一側，為書本騰出空間。

「我在島上四處查看時發現了這個，不知道你是否能告訴我關於它的任何資訊？」我小心翼翼地解開包裹，將書放在桌面上清出的區域。

Morichi先生發出一聲輕微的口哨聲，那聲音彷彿異常持久地懸在空中，「我已經多年沒見過這本書了。」他的語氣幾乎帶著崇敬，「請問你是在哪裡取得這本書的？」

我考慮拒絕回答，但考慮到他對我如此坦誠，我覺得應該以同樣的禮貌回報，「我在舊教堂的地下室找到的。」

他緩緩點頭，「這就說得通了。」他的目光向上抬起，與我對視，「Erkon牧師在他不幸去世前幾個月，從我這裡買走了這本書。如果人們說的是真的，這本書成了他的執念。」

Morichi先生伸出手，幾乎要觸碰書本，卻又用布將這本古老典籍重新包裹起來，「據說這本書原屬於Zodak Alhazred，是懸崖部分坍塌、將他與他的家園吞噬入海時，少數倖存的物品之一。」[IF $Madness gte 3]

所以這又回到了Morichi先生和他的家族。我的思緒飄向黑暗的角落，腦中低語的聲音突然顯得愉悅。[ELSEIF $Madness gte 1]

一切都指向這裡。這怎麼會和Morichi先生有關？難道他在這一切中扮演著某種秘密角色？[/IF]

他的舉止變得近乎輕蔑，將書朝你推了推，彷彿那是他不願觸碰的不潔之物，「這本書總是讓我不舒服，所以當牧師出現在我家門口詢問能否購買時，我很樂意賣給他。不過現在想想，我始終沒弄清楚他最初是如何得知這本書存在的。」

意識到我可能無法得知更多，我試著轉移話題。

[詢問Morichi先生關於他的茶。]
[詢問Morichi先生關於萬頓灣的歷史。][IF $FountainInvestigated is "Played"]
[詢問Morichi先生關於遺跡的事。][/IF]
[詢問Morichi先生關於他的家庭。][IF $MorichiDaughter gte 1]
[詢問關於Morichi先生女兒的事。][/IF][IF $MorichiDaughter gte 3 && $MotherTalk is "Not Played"]
[詢問Morichi先生關於Azumi的母親。][/IF][IF $MotherMaybe is "Anne"]

Sighing, I take a step back from the door, listening to her weeping on the other side of it.

Feeling upset, I walk back to the muddy street.

The expression on her face is one I've seen before.  The look of a loved one who expects to learn their loved one is dead and has already started to mourn. Sleep does not come easily for me.  I lay in bed, tossing and turning for what seems like hours.  Sometimes even getting up out of bed to pace about the small room I have rented.  

Returning to my bed again, I lay down feeling troubled and impossibly aroused.  I consider masturbating, anything to take the edge off, but instead I force my gritty eyes closed.

//The storm finally let up?//  I think in wonder when I realize my room is lit from the light coming through the window.

Sitting up stiffly, I groan, //To be even ten years younger again, . . . and maybe take a little better care of myself,// I muse.  I rub at my eyes, feeling like I have sand packed beneath their lids, then look outside the window of my room.

Turning toward the little table in my room, I take a step toward it to retrieve the old leather bound tome before I stop short, //It's not there,// I realize.

After all the creaks, squeeks, and groans I've heard on the island, the door swinging open silently makes the hair on the back of my neck stand up.

散落在地板上的有零散的紙張、撕碎的衣物碎片，以及其他各式各樣的小物件。靠近房間遠端有個舊的棕色背包，這些東西大概都是從裡面掉出來的。

看起來像是某人——或某種東西——把裡面的東西全翻了出來。這背包是誰的？他們又遭遇了什麼？

這張紙解答了「是誰」的問題，但同時也衍生出無數新疑問，多到我不知從何梳理起。

這是Tom的字跡。看著紙上潦草的速記，我幾乎看不懂內容，一股怒火在體內沸騰。該死的小子，我是來幫你的，你為什麼就不能像正常人一樣用英文寫清楚？

攥著滿手紙張，我沉重地嘆了口氣。這裡到底發生了什麼？或許同樣重要的是——這一切意味著什麼？我一邊陰鬱地思索，一邊朝這棟小建築的出口走去。

最後那個念頭讓我驟然清醒，開始悄悄後退，遠離建築物，以免觸手怪可能嗅到我的氣味……假設它真的是靠氣味偵測獵物的話。

passages.name: Morning.
passages.content:
After last night's dream, I feel unfocused as I prepare for the day to come.  //Something has to change, some clue, some unasked question, something.  I know I'm in the right place, I just need to start connecting the dots to make sense of all of this.//  

I reach up with one hand, my thumb and forefinger rubbing the grit from my eyes.

Feeling discouraged, I make my way out of my room and start walking down the stairs.  I'm nearly at the bottom when I hear a familiar voice.

“I don't care you little //slut,//”  Aaron's voice is clearly angry, “Its past time you leave this whole 'Tom' business behind you and if I so much as catch wind that you've even //thought// about saying something to that detective friend of yours, well, you know what'll happen to you.”  His tone is a clear threat.

I remain standing at the top of the stairs, my boot inches above the first step leading down.  I resign myself to standing here for a long moment so its not obvious that I just overheard what was said.

A feminine huff, something akin to a frustrated scream comes from the area I could hear Aaron's voice from, then rapid footsteps leading in my general direction.  

Taking a quick step back in an effort to remain unseen, I see Tanya storm past the steps, her head half turning towards me as she passes.  I can see her eyes widen when she sees me, but her stride never slows and there is no other indication that she acknowledged me.

<<if $Taint gte 50>>//Could //she// actually be Tom?//  My own recent changes cross my mind in a rush.

A heavy chill settles into the pit of my gut, //Could I have been looking at who I've come here to find this entire time?//

I scour my thoughts, trying to remember every word of what Tanya had said, trying to find anything that may confirm my sudden, and horrible theory.<<else>>//Could Tanya somehow be Tom?//  The thought seems unreal, but at the same time the theory answers so many questions that I have had since coming to Wanton Cove.

I take a deep breath, letting it out slowly as I center my thoughts.  //Just the thought of bringing this up with her is enough to make the hairs on the back of my neck stand on end.//

Lady Anne leans forward, steepling her fingers before herself.  Her gaze is hard, hard enough to sit me back in my seat as she stares me down, "You have something to ask?"  Her voice is smooth like silk, holding a quality that makes me want to flee this place.

//Does she know what I want to ask?//  The thought shakes me to my core, //But how could she?//

"Azumi,"  I begin.  Part of me wants to assert that she's Azumi's mother, but my courage fails me and I ask instead, "Do you know who Azumi's mother is?"  

"Of course,"  Lady Anne's expression becomes one of amusement.

//She's going to make me ask anyway.//  I think, looking into her hard eyes.  I can tell that she's taking great pleasure at making me squirm, "Are you her mother?"  I finally manage to choke out.

A cruel smile forms on her lips, like the death's head grin of a naked skull, "You already know the answer to that if you're asking the question."

I open my mouth to speak, but the sound that comes out can not be consider spoken language, instead it is like a cross between a choking sound and a whine.  Closing my mouth ends the sound, but it does not improve my mental state in the least, nor my racing thoughts.

The silence stretches on for minutes, me struggling to make sense of this new revelation while Lady Anne looks at me like a child with a magnifying glass might look at an ant.  

I shiver.

She speaks, "My dear Detective, for someone who has dedicated their life to seeking answers, I never expected you to be one who would be taken back by one."

No matter how I try, I can't find the words to continue the conversation.

"I can see that you have questions,"  The smile on her face does not quite reach her eyes, "But I do not feel inclined to answer.  Suffice to say, I have my reasons for wha

passages.name: Tavern8
passages.content:
<<if $GooCounter is 0>>The people gathered in the Barmaid's Daughter create a steady babble of noise.  Several conversations all try to talk over one another, punctuated by the clink of glasses, the grind of chairs sliding in or out of places, and everything else that is going on within the establishment.  

Looking around, I noticed that there are more women in the tavern than I would usually expect from this sort of place, and many of the individuals who are not immediately identifiable as female have an androgynous look to them that makes it difficult to tell if they are man or woman.

<<set $GooCounter += 1>><<elseif $GooCounter is 1>>The stench of smoke is thick in the air, enough so that I can feel it in my lungs in my first big breath of air in the Barmaid's Daughter. 

My eyes drift over the interior and quickly find the source, four men smoking a mix of pipes and cigars at a table not half a dozen paces from where I stand.

I'm nearly ready to dismiss them when I notice there is a woman under the table, kneeling between the legs of one man.  

I stop where I'm standing, watching the woman's head bob up and down in the man's lap a few times, then turn around to move to the next man at the table, resuming with him where she left off with the last.

//I've heard of some pretty seedy places, hell I've //been// to some pretty seedy places, but I've never seen a place where things like that happen so casually before coming to Wanton Cove.//

<<set $GooCounter += 1>><<else>>My eyes drift over the interior of the tavern, taking in the hand full of people within.  

Eventually my gaze settles on a middle aged man with a woman a few years younger than himself sitting in his lap.  For a split second I think the scene looks like any of a hundred ladies I've seen sitting in a man's lap in some bar, but then I have a sudden revelation as to why the pair drew my attention.

The man is holding the woman's hips as she rocks her hips gently.  He has a hint

passages.name: AzumiExit2
passages.content:
A few seconds later, Mr Morichi is locking the door behind us.  The melodious sound of Azumi's giggling can be heard through the door.

Starting to walk away from the door, I have little choice but to follow Mr Morichi or be left behind.

"You understand now why I prefer not to speak of my daugher?"  He asks when we are about half way back to the room he likes to sit in.

"Yeah, I think I do."  I reply, realizing that the experience still has me reeling as I try to wrap my head around everything.

We walk the rest of the way back before he speaks again.

"If you wish to see her again, you may do so, though I have no intention of accompanying you a second time."  He picks up his cup of tea, sipping at it before making a face, "Cold,"  He mutters to himself before sitting it down again, "It breaks my heart every time I see her.  She hates me,"  When he looks at me, there's a hint of moisture in his eyes.

Not sure what to say, I remain silent.

When he looks away, his tone is much more restrained, "I will tell you just a little more about her, then I would ask that we speak of more pleasant things,"  Not waiting for my reply, he continues, "The last time she escaped her room, she raped a man here in town.  That was about four years ago."

//Around the time the Pastor committed suicide.//  I realize.

"She has an insatable sex drive.  She was barely through puberty when it begain.  She'd do anything to satisfy her urges, even forcing herself on unwilling partners.  Needless to say, many of the locals frowned upon this.  After all, few men enjoy being buggered, and fewer still enjoy their wives and daughters being raped."

I can see the muscles along his jaw tighten as he grinds his teeth, something I've never seen him do before, "I tried restraining her to the mansion after the first few incidents, but then she turned her attention to myself and those in my employ.  When her attempts were rebuffed, . . . well, there's a reason she's confined to her
passages.content_zh:
幾秒鐘後，Morichi先生正從外頭鎖上房門。Azumi那銀鈴般的咯咯笑聲仍能透過門板隱約傳來。

我開始朝門外走去，除了跟上Morichi先生的腳步，我別無選擇——否則就只能被獨自留在這裡。

「現在你明白，為何我總不願談起女兒了吧？」當我們走回他慣坐的那個房間，約莫半途時，他開口問道。

「嗯，我想我明白了。」我回答，意識到自己仍因方才的經歷而心神恍惚，試圖消化這一切。

我們默默走完剩下的路程，他才再度開口。

「如果你想再見她，我不會阻止，但我不打算陪你第二次了。」他端起茶杯啜了一口，隨即皺起臉，「涼了。」他低聲自語，將杯子放回桌上，「每次見到她，都讓我心碎。她恨我。」當他看向我時，眼中隱約閃著水光。

我不知道該說什麼，只能保持沉默。

當他移開視線，語氣變得克制許多：「我會再多告訴你一點關於她的事，然後，我希望我們能談些更愉快的話題。」不等我回應，他繼續說道：「上次她逃出房間時，在鎮上強暴了一名男子。那大約是四年前的事。」

差不多就是牧師自殺的那段時間。我意識到。

「她有無法滿足的性慾。青春期剛開始就顯露出來。她會不擇手段滿足衝動，甚至強迫不情願的對象。不用說，當地人多半對此皺眉。畢竟，沒幾個男人喜歡被雞姦，更少人樂見自己的妻女遭強暴。」

他咬緊牙關，下顎肌肉緊繃——這是我從未在他身上見過的神態：「頭幾次事件後，我試圖將她禁錮在宅邸內，但她轉而將目標對準我和雇員們。當她的企圖被拒絕……嗯，這就是為何她現在被關在房間裡。」

「我甚至試過為她尋找自願的情人，希望他們能平息她的慾火，但毫無作用。」他看向你，「如我之前所說，我不會阻止你去見她，如果你真想這麼做。但我懷疑她對你或你尋找Tom的任務，提供不了什麼有價值的線索。只是要知道，若你在我未陪同的情況下見她，無論你情願與否，她都將索求你的『溫存』。」

我緩緩點頭表示理解。

當他將注意力轉向窗外的海浪時，我意識到或許該轉換話題了。

[詢問Morichi先生關於他的茶。]

[詢問Morichi先生關於萬頓灣的歷史。]

[詢問Morichi先生關於他的家族。][IF $PastorBook is "Taken"]

[詢問關於教堂地下室那本書的事。][/IF]

[向Morichi先生告辭，離開宅邸。]

passages.name: Park1
passages.content:
<<if $Day gte 5>><<if $Madness gte 3>>I think I hear something as I near the fountain, something deep sounding, like a whisper of somthing truly massive calling up from somewhere deep underground.

Without stopping to think, I quickly stumble back several paces, half falling in the muddy grass.  The heavy smell of rot washes over me, causing me to gag and neary vomit.  My stomach heaves, though I thankfully expell nothing.

Nearly frozen in fear, I stare at the fountain, waiting for //anything// to happen.  Only the heavy rain falling in sheets down onto me is enough to refocus my attention once more.

//The fountain, I wonder what lurks beneath.  Is it one of //them// or perhaps a prisoner to what is happening here?//

<<elseif $Madness is 2>>A sudden sound from the fountain draws my attention.  

As I watch, a blackish sludge gurgles up from the top to quickly disperse in the heavy rain falling from the sky.

For one paniced moment, I think it is a tentacle, reaching through the pipe to grasp me.  A cry catches in my throat as I take a half step back, positioning myself to defend myself.  A thick heavy smell fills the air, like a giant rotting carcas, but I'm able to resist the urge to gag.  

When the seconds stretch into minutes, I finally realize that nothing untoward is about to happen.  

//Probably.//

<<elseif $Madness is 1>>A deep gurgling sound comes from somewhere nearby, like a half drowned voice.

"Hello?"  I call, searching my surroundings.  The sound comes again, as if in response to my call.  Yelling into the rain a second time, I hear no reply to my repeated cries.  After several seconds, I can smell the scent of rot momentarily assails me before beginning to fade again.

Uneasy, I watch the area around me closely as I return to what I was doing.

<<else>>//Grgu-ughu,// A sickly sound, like a sucking chest wound momentarily drowns out the sound of the pouring rain.

I look around, at the same time, the sound comes again.
passages.content_zh:
[IF $Day gte 5][IF $Madness gte 3]當我靠近噴泉時，我覺得自己聽到了什麼聲音，一種深沉的聲響，彷彿某種真正龐然大物的低語，從地底深處呼喚著。

我未加思索，迅速踉蹌後退幾步，半跌進泥濘的草地裡。一股濃烈的腐敗氣味撲鼻而來，讓我作嘔，幾乎吐出來。我的胃劇烈翻攪，幸好什麼也沒吐出來。

我幾乎嚇得僵住，緊盯著噴泉，等待任何事發生。只有如帷幕般傾瀉在我身上的暴雨，足以讓我再次集中注意力。

那座噴泉，我好奇底下潛伏著什麼。是它們其中之一，還是或許是這一切事件的囚徒？

[ELSEIF $Madness is 2]噴泉突然傳來的聲響吸引了我的注意。

我注視著，一股黑濁的汙泥從頂部汩汩湧出，又迅速在天空降下的暴雨中消散。

在驚慌的一瞬間，我以為那是一條觸手，正穿過水管要抓住我。我倒抽半口氣，後退半步，擺出防禦姿態。空氣中瀰漫著一股濃重刺鼻的氣味，像一具巨大腐爛的動物屍體，但我勉強忍住沒有作嘔。

當幾秒鐘延長成數分鐘，我終於意識到，大概不會有什麼異常的事情要發生了。

大概。

[ELSEIF $Madness is 1]附近某處傳來深沉的汩汩聲，像半淹沒的嗓音。

「喂？」我喊道，環顧四周。那聲音又來了，彷彿在回應我的呼喚。我再次朝雨中大喊，卻沒有得到任何回應。幾秒鐘後，我聞到一股腐敗的氣味短暫襲來，然後又開始消散。

我感到不安，一邊繼續手邊的事，一邊密切注意著周圍的動靜。

[ELSE]咕嚕-呃咕，一種令人不適的聲音，像吸吮性胸部傷口的聲響，暫時蓋過了傾盆大雨的聲音。

我環顧四周，與此同時，聲音再次響起。我及時轉頭，正好看到一股灰黑色的汙泥從附近噴泉頂部噴射出來。

腐敗的氣味飄來，我不禁厭惡地撇了撇嘴。

[/IF][ELSE]我停下腳步，感覺有些不對勁，心想但願那隻鳴鳥能安靜下來，好讓我更專注於這突然讓我緊張起來的事物。

過了幾秒鐘，我突然意識到。那隻鳴鳥本身似乎就顯得不合時宜。我四處張望，猛然發現在這場雨的間歇期，空氣中竟沒有一聲烏鴉或海鷗的叫鳴來填補聲響。

彷彿也察覺到同樣的狀況，那隻鳴鳥安靜了下來。我尋找聲音的來源，但沒有了聲音指引，我無法找到那隻鳥。

[/IF][繼續你的路。]

passages.name: Notes about creating your character.
passages.content:
<<back>>

!Abilities
Brawn:  Your physical prowess.  This affects the following Skills:  Brawling, Firearms, and Intimidation.

Intelligence:  Your mental faculties.  This affects the following Skills: Investigation, Occult, Perception, and Security.

Social:  Your natural charisma.  This affects the following Skills: Etiquette, and Streetwise.

!Skills
Brawling: Your aptitude in a hand to hand fight.
Etiquette:  Your ability to efficiently interact with civil individuals.
Firearms:  Your aptitude with a firearm.  This affects your likelihood of hitting your target.
Intimidation:  Your ability to force individuals to talk when they might not otherwise.
Investigation:  Your police training, affects what you notice when viewing a crime scene or looking for clues.
Occult:  Your knowledge and understanding of things otherworldly.  Helps you understand what you are looking at when seeing things of the occult nature, or helps when deciphering arcane texts and languages.
Perception:  Your alertness, helps you notice details you might otherwise miss.
Security:  Your knowledge of how to avoid or circumvent locks or other potential deterrents.
Streetwise:  Your ability to efficiently interact with less savory individuals.

-Just like in any RPG, there is no right or wrong way to build your character, but how you build your character can have a HUGE effect on how the game plays.

-Your core stats can be adjusted as low as 1 or as high as 10.  These numbers are not just for skills.  Sometimes in the game there will be a raw Attribute check that takes into account your Attribute scores only, without skills associted with them.  In other words, you may have more skill points to use if you dump a bunch of points into Intelligence, but you'll suffer in situations that require an unmodified Brawn or Social check.

-Your Attributes are a starting point and can have a huge impact on your skills as they are added to those scores for skill checks in the game.

passages.name: Dont my mutations bother you?
passages.content:
"You haven't reacted to my mutations at all.  Don't they bother you?"  I ask.

Azumi looks at me with an odd expression, "Should it?"

"They aren't normal, and most people would freak out if they saw me like this,"  I reply.

"Father says that I'm not normal either, and people like you are common here.  I sometimes see people with //mutations//," The word sounds awkward in her mouth, "Like yours out my window."

I open my mouth to say something, but I can't think of anything.  Instead, I change course with the conversation, "You say you see people with my kind of changes outside your window?"

She clutches my arm a little tighter as her face draws close to mine, "Yes, sometimes near the cliff, or at the treeline, but mostly just on brighter days in the water, but the ones in the water are hard to see and are too far away to see very clearly,"  I can feel her breath on my cheek as she speaks.

I've only a moment for this information to sink in before I feel her turn towards me, her erection brushing against my leg lightly.  With both of her hands around my arm, she tries to guide my hand to her cock.  I instinctively resist, moving my hand to my own lap even though a large part of me wants to give in to her desires.

「你對我的變異完全沒有反應。難道不會讓你困擾嗎？」我問道。

Azumi 用一種奇怪的表情看著我，「應該要嗎？」

「這不正常，大多數人看到我這樣會嚇壞的。」我回答。

「父親說我也不正常，而且像你這樣的人在這裏很常見。我有時會看到有變異的人，」這個詞從她嘴裏說出來有些彆扭，「像你這樣的，就在我窗外。」

我張嘴想說些什麼，卻想不出任何話。於是我轉移了話題：「你說你在窗外看到過像我這種變化的人？」

她將我的手臂抓得更緊了些，臉湊近我的臉，「是的，有時在懸崖邊，或是樹林邊緣，但大多是在光線較亮的日子裏出現在水中。不過水裏的那些很難看清，距離太遠了，看不清楚。」她說話時，我能感覺到她的氣息拂過我的臉頰。

passages.name: Tell me a little about yourself?
passages.content:
"Maybe you can tell me a little bit about yourself?"  I ask, wanting to learn a little more about the woman.

"Oh, me?"  She replies, a hint of color creeping into her cheeks, "Not much to tell about."

She lets out a little sigh around the small smile touching the corner of her lips, "I used to be involved at the Church, trying to help out wherever I could, but once our pastor,"  A look of sadness crosses her features, "Once he was gone, there wasn't much left to do around Wanton Cove."  She seems to draw up a little, indignation entering her voice as she continues, "I wasn't going to become one of those hussies down at the tavern."

Taking a deep breath, she recomposes herself a little, "So instead, I spend most of my time at home, cooking, cleaning,"  She gestures at the Bible on the lamp stand beside herself, "and reading the Good Book."<<if $Madness gte 2>>


//Is it God, or her faith that has sheltered her from the corruption of this place?//

A soft laugh threatens to sceape me, //No matter, I fear I am beyond redemption.//

Focusing my attention on the present, I meet Ms Sherwood's eyes.<<elseif $Madness is 1>>

//Does the Good Book protect you?  Is God looking over you even now, keeping the horrors of this place away from your door?//

I feel a twinge of something unfamiliar deep inside of myself but I do not have time to analize it.<<else>><<endif>>

I nod in understanding.  "Sound like a real test of your faith."  I add, unsure how else to respond.

<<if $Mutation gte 3>>Her eyes linger on me for several seconds, her expression hard, like she is judging me.  //Guess my changes are getting to her.//  I think darkly.

//Better change the subject before she thinks too much on my changes.  Something tells me she isn't the type to just accept something like this easily.//  <<else>>"Thank you,"  Ms Sherwood says, flushing slightly.  I find myself mildly surprised to smell a hint of arousal in the air.

passages.name: drunk from the Church.
passages.content:
<<set $random to random(1, 4)>><<if $random is 4>>Starting to walk towards the drunk, I see him turn his glassy gaze towards me.  Not quite focusing on me, he raises his glass, completely missing his mouth as he spills the drink down the front of himself.

I’ve nearly reached him when he starts to lean heavily to one side.  I’m not fast enough to catch him before he topples to the floor in a heap.

Several of the locals chuckle at the scene.  Meanwhile, a loud snore starts from behind the table where he fell.

//I won’t be getting anything useful out of him.//  I think sourly before returning my attention to the rest of [[the establishment.|The Barmaids Daughter]]

<<elseif $random is 3>>Nearing the table, the drunk looks at me suddenly, “eeh! Unner uh able en ssssuuuk I ook,”  He takes a long sip from his cup.

//Did he just?//  I’m shocked as I finally start to piece together what he just said through his slur.

After a second, his eyes drift off of me as if he can no longer see me, his attention turning towards the riotous laughter coming from the card game at the next table.  

When he makes the same suggestion to one of the men at the table, one of them looks at the drunk with disgust, then turns to ignore him.

//Even if I could start a conversation, it’s unlikely be able to gain anything useful from him in this state.//

[[Maybe another time.|The Barmaids Daughter]]

<<elseif $random is 2>>When I near his table, I can see that the man’s eyes are rolled back in his head, only a sliver of the whites showing.  A soft, nasal snore emits from his open mouth.

//He’s no use to anyone like that.//  I think.

Shrugging to myself, I turn my attention to [[the rest of the bar.|The Barmaids Daughter]]

<<else>>It comes as little surprise to see the drunk swaying in his chair, barely able to stay seated properly.  I can see a goofy grin on his face as he struggles to focus on anything in his field of vision.  

When I reach his table, I clear
passages.content_zh:
[IF $random is 4]我開始朝那醉漢走去，見他將呆滯的目光轉向我。他眼神渙散地舉起酒杯，卻完全沒對準嘴，酒液全灑在了自己胸前。

我幾乎要走到他面前時，他開始重重朝一側傾斜。我來不及扶住，他已癱倒在地成了一團。

幾個本地人見狀低聲竊笑。與此同時，他倒下的桌後傳來響亮的鼾聲。

從他這兒是問不出什麼有用的東西了。我悻悻地想，隨即將注意力轉回[這間酒館]的其他地方。

[ELSEIF $random is 3]靠近桌邊時，醉漢突然看向我：「呃！泥、泥補能……縮、縮話……」他舉杯長飲了一口。

他剛剛是說？我震驚地試圖從他含糊的語音中拼湊出意思。

片刻後，他的視線從我身上飄開，彷彿再也看不見我，注意力轉向鄰桌牌局傳來的喧鬧笑聲。

當他對牌桌旁一名男子提出同樣的建議時，那人厭惡地瞪了醉漢一眼，隨即扭頭不理會。

即便能開啟對話，以他這種狀態也很難問出什麼有用的訊息。

[也許改天吧。]

[ELSEIF $random is 2]走近他桌邊時，我看見這男人雙眼上翻，只露出眼白的一絲縫隙。他張開的嘴裡發出輕柔的鼻鼾聲。

他這樣子對誰都沒用。我想。

暗自聳了聳肩，我將注意力轉向[酒館的其他地方。]

[ELSE]見到那醉漢在椅上搖晃、幾乎坐不穩的模樣，我毫不意外。他臉上掛著癡傻的笑容，掙扎著想聚焦視野中的任何東西。

當我走到他桌旁，清了清喉嚨：「能談一下嗎？」

他歪了歪頭，像在努力聽什麼，但再無其他跡象顯示他注意到我。

「先生？」我又試了一次。

男人突然轉向我，我幾乎來不及意識他的意圖。迅速閃開的瞬間，他將胃裡的穢物全吐在我半秒前站立的位置。

真噁心。

接著他抬起手臂用袖子抹去下巴的膽汁，又舉起握著酒杯的手湊到唇邊喝了一口。

現在是沒法從他這兒問出什麼有用的情報了。我皺著臉想。

我將注意力轉回[酒保的女兒]的其他地方。

[/IF]

<<set $Bed to "False">><<set $Day += 1>><<set $CharHP = 10>><<set $Research to 0>><<if $Taint gte 100>><<set $Taint += 5>><<elseif $Taint gte 75>><<set $Taint += 4>><<elseif $Taint gte 50>><<set $Taint += 3>><<elseif $Taint gte 25>><<set $Taint += 2>>
<<elseif $Taint gte 1>><<set $Taint += 1>><</if>>//Blinking at the soft morning sun, I reach out and rub my eyes.  //No dream, thank God for that.//

I can hear the sounds of birds chirping somehere outside, and last night's heavy rain was enough to make the air feel cool and crisp, even with the window closed.  //Maybe today wont be so bad.//  I give out a humorless laugh.

Rolling out of bed, I start to get dressed.  I'm just starting to pull my boots on when I hear a knock at at my room's door.

"Who is it, and what the hell do you want?"  My voice is light, the words little more than harmless banter escaping my lips.

"It's Tom,"  The familiar voice comes through the door, "I hear you came to Wanton Cove looking for me.  Can you really take me home?

Goosebumps break out across my skin and my eyes widen.  With only one boot on, I bolt for the door, swinging it open.  My heart thrills when I see Tom, a sheepish grin on his face, standing there.

I throw my arms around him in a sudden hug that seems to surprise both of us, "Yes!  Yes, I'm here to bring you home!"

Tom returns the hug after a moment, holding on to me tightly.  When his body starts to tremble, I'm put off just a little bit, //Men aren't supposed to cry like that, but then, Tom always was the sensitive type,// but I let it slide.  After all, its hard to condemn a man for doing something that you are on the verge of doing yourself.

Releasing Tom from my grip, I step back to take him in.  Mangy red hair sprouts in irregular tufts from his gray scaly scalp.  His too wide mouth spreads into a gaping smile that reveals rows of razor sharp teeth.  Even as I watch, his neck seems to pulsate, gills moving as he breathes  His solid black eyes a
passages.content_zh:
[IF $Taint gte 100][ELSEIF $Taint gte 75][ELSEIF $Taint gte 50][ELSEIF $Taint gte 25]
[ELSEIF $Taint gte 1][/IF]我眨著眼望向柔和的晨光，伸手揉了揉眼睛。沒有做夢，感謝老天。

我能聽見外面某處傳來鳥兒的啁啾聲，昨晚的滂沱大雨足以讓空氣感覺涼爽清新，即使窗戶關著也一樣。也許今天不會太糟。我發出一聲毫無幽默感的乾笑。

翻身下床，我開始穿衣。正當我開始套上靴子時，聽見有人敲我房間的門。

「是誰？你他媽的想幹嘛？」我的語氣輕鬆，脫口而出的話語不過是無惡意的玩笑。

「是Tom，」熟悉的聲音從門外傳來，「我聽說你來萬頓灣找我。你真的能帶我回家嗎？」

我渾身起雞皮疙瘩，雙眼圓睜。只穿了一隻靴子，我猛地衝向門，一把拉開。看到Tom站在那兒，臉上掛著靦腆的笑容，我的心激動不已。

我突然張開雙臂抱住他，這個舉動似乎讓我們兩人都吃了一驚，「對！對，我來這裡就是為了帶你回家！」

片刻後，Tom回抱了我，緊緊抓著我。當他的身體開始顫抖時，我有點不自在——男人不該那樣哭的，但話說回來，Tom一向是敏感型的人，所以我沒計較。畢竟，很難譴責一個即將做出你自己也快要做的事的人。

我鬆開Tom，後退一步打量他。骯髒的紅髮從他灰色鱗片狀的頭皮上不規則地一簇簇冒出。他那過寬的嘴巴咧開成一個空洞的微笑，露出排排剃刀般鋒利的牙齒。就在我注視的同時，他的脖子似乎在搏動，呼吸時鰓部不斷開合。他那純黑的雙眼巨大，以一種令人不安的強烈目光緊盯著我。

我踉蹌後退一步，伸手摸索我的.38特製左輪，卻發現肩帶和槍套還掛在床頭板上。「搞什——」

「搞什麼？你大老遠跑來找我，就只能這樣？」Tom的聲音變得尖銳刺耳。他朝我步步逼近，爪狀的腳刮擦著地板，利爪般的手屈伸著。

我慌亂地向後退，碰到了床。與此同時，這個Tom怪物//繼續逼近。他舉起一隻看起來邪惡的手，爪子染著血紅，正當他看似準備攻擊時，房門外突然傳來霰彈槍的轟然巨響。

passages.name: Ask Mr Morichi about the image of Azumi in the Leatherbound Tome.
passages.content:
“I stumbled across something that I’d like you to take a look at,”  I begin.

Mr Morichi raises an eyebrow, sitting his cup down as I pull the leather bound tome from my jacket and open it to the page I had seen an image of Azumi on. His eyebrows rise in surprise, “Oh my,”  He says in a breathless whisper.  Reaching out, his fingertips touch the image, tracing its outline like a lover’s caress.  With his other hand, he adjusts his glasses, situating them precisely on his nose before leaning close to the book.  I can see his eyes drift over the image, then turn to the text.   He spends several minutes focused on the book before him, seeming to have forgotten me entirely.

Eventually, he sits back, readjusting his glasses to their usual location, then picks up his cup for a small taste of his tea.  When he sits the cup back down, he looks at me, “Quite the striking resemblance to my ever enchanting daughter.  Though having been the proud owner the book previously, I do not recall seeing this image before.”  A troubled expression settles onto Mr Morichi's face.

When he does not continue, I prod, “Do you know anything about this?  Any light you can shed on the presence of her image in the book?”

Mr Morichi shakes his head slowly, “Sorry, while the image is tremendously fascinating to me, I can not see how it is anything but coincidence that the girl in the book so closely resembles my daughter.”  He pauses, as if considering whether or not to add more to what he has already said, “After all, my daughter is a young woman, and this book is terribly old.”

//Coincidence, perhaps.  But it seems to me that everything in Wanton Cove is somehow connected.  I just need to figure out how this fits into the bigger picture.//

“Thank you for taking a look anyway,”  I say, somewhat dejectedly.

With an inward sigh, I decide to change the subject.

passages.name: Ask her about Wanton Cove.
passages.content:
"What can you tell me about Wanton Cove?"  I ask.

Azumi looks up at me, her expression pinched, "The people here don't like me.  A lot of them are scared of me,"  Her voice suddenly becomes very small, "And I don't understand why."

"A lot of people are put off by what they don't understand,"  I point out, "And your father says that you've done things to the people here in town to upset them."

She takes a deep breath then lets it out in something that sounds like a pout, I can feel her breasts press tighter against my side as she does so, "But it feels //so// good, how is it wrong to do it if it makes them feel good too?"  

I'm a little surprised at the sincerity of her question, "Because some people only want to feel good like that with people who are special to them,"  I say cautiously.

For a moment, she continues to pout, her eyes sad and her bottom lip sticking out in a way that comes off as adorable, then a mischevious smile blossoms on her face, "Can I be special to you?"

//Maybe now would be a good time to change the subject.//  The thought crosses my mind, but at the same time my body responds to her question by leaving me flush with arousal.

「關於萬頓灣，你能告訴我什麼？」我問道。

Azumi抬頭看著我，表情緊繃，「這裡的人不喜歡我。很多人怕我。」她的聲音突然變得很微弱，「而我不明白為什麼。」

「很多人會對他們不理解的事物感到排斥，」我指出，「而且你父親說，你對鎮上的人做過一些事，惹惱了他們。」

她深吸一口氣，然後呼出一聲聽起來像鬧彆扭的嘆息。這麼做時，我能感覺她的胸部更緊地壓在我的身側，「但那感覺那麼好，如果也能讓他們感覺舒服，做這件事有什麼錯呢？」

她問題中的真誠讓我有些驚訝，「因為有些人只想和對他們來說特別的人一起感受那種愉悅，」我謹慎地說。

有一會兒，她繼續噘著嘴，眼神悲傷，下唇微微噘起的模樣顯得可愛。接著，一抹淘氣的笑容在她臉上綻開，「我可以成為你特別的人嗎？」

<<if $SailorGoo is "Played">><<if $GooCounter is 0>>Pausing, I can see something white, glowing faintly, dripping slowly from a broad leaf of a bush at about hip level.  

Leaning in, I see that its the same kind of White Goo I have seen before. 

Watching a thick dollop of the fluid drip slowly towards the ground, its thick consistancy allowing it to streatch several inches before a bit breaks off and falls to the ground to join a growing puddle of it beside the trail.  //Whatever left this must have done so recently.//

or
[[Investicate Closer.|TG1]]

<<set $GooCounter += 1>><<elseif $GooCounter is 1>><<set $GooCounter += 1>>I step in something slick, half skidding, half falling before I can catch myself.  

My hand and part of my leg smeared in a fresh coating of mud, I get back to my feet with an unhappy growl under my breath.

Turning my attention to what I just stepped in, I can see another of those puddles of White Goo.

Silently cursing myself for not seeing it before I stepped in it, I lean closer for a better look.  

Other than the smear of it stretching across the ground where my stepping in it spread it across the trail, I see nothing about this puddle that seems any different than what I have encountered before.

[[Continue on my way.|Trail]]
or

[IF $SailorGoo is "Played"][IF $GooCounter is 0]我停下腳步，看到某種白色的東西，微微發光，從一株約臀部高度的灌木寬大葉片上緩緩滴落。

湊近一看，發現那和我之前見過的白色黏液是同一種。

看著一團濃稠的液體緩緩滴向地面，其黏稠的質地讓它在斷裂前能拉伸出好幾英寸，然後才有一小塊脫落，掉到地上，加入小徑旁不斷擴大的黏液水窪。無論是什麼留下這東西，肯定是不久前才發生的。

[繼續趕路。]
或
[更仔細調查。]

[ELSEIF $GooCounter is 1]我踩到了某種滑溜的東西，半滑半摔，才勉強穩住自己。

我的手和部分腿上沾滿了新鮮的泥濘，我一邊低聲不悅地咕噥，一邊站起身。

將注意力轉向我剛剛踩到的東西，我看到另一灘那種白色黏液。

暗自咒罵自己沒在踩到前發現它，我靠得更近以便看清楚。

除了我踩到它時在地上拖出的那道長長污跡，這灘黏液看起來和我之前遇到的沒什麼不同。

[繼續趕路。]
或
[更仔細調查。]

[ELSE]在我行走的、由岩石和泥濘構成的小徑上，我看到一片微微發光的地面。

靠近時，我看清那是一灘同樣散發奇異光芒的白色黏液，和我之前見過的一樣。

[繼續趕路。]
或
[更仔細調查。]

[/IF][ELSE]我看到附近一個水窪表面有層光澤，雖然這景象並未立刻引起我的注意。過了一會兒我才意識到，水裡的東西是濃稠、白色，並且散發著微光的。

我的好奇心突然被勾起，我靠了過去。無論那是什麼，它漂浮在水面上，而我有一種揮之不去的感覺，覺得我正在看的東西有種莫名的熟悉感。

我伸出手，好奇地觸碰那物質，它似乎黏附在我的指尖上。「這到底是什麼東西？」我出聲自問。

我將指尖舉到臉前嗅聞，但只聞到一絲微甜的鹹味。

盯著這液體看了幾秒後，我發現指尖上的量比最初少了很多。我迅速將剩下的抹在褲腿上，然後繞過那水窪。

[繼續趕路。]
[/IF]

passages.name: You seemed a little intimidated by Aaron?
passages.content:
"When I saw you on the waterfront, you seemed uncomfortable around Aaron."  I ask carefully.

"Um,"  Ms Sherwood looks uncomfortable, almost scared, "Its nothing, don't worry yourself over it."<<if $Madness gte 3>>

Part of me wants to rage at her, frighten her and demand answers, but I force back the rising tide of irrational thought that threatens to overwhelm me.  

As it subsides, a sound, not quite a laugh, bubbles up from my throat.  Me Sherwood looks at me, clearly concerned, though she does not comment on it.<<elseif $Madness is 2>>

Looking across at Ms Sherwood, it feels as if a woman of her nature in a place like this should posess a halo, or some other form of divine touch that would be visible to anyone looking at her.

//Or has my presence ended such a thing?  My merely being her acting to corrupt what little is pure about Wanton Cove?//

The absurdity of my thoughts threaten to make me laugh, pushing them to the side as I focus back on the task at hand.<<elseif $Madness is 1>>

I look at Ms Sherwood for several seconds,  //Can she see that I have changed?  That the realization that at least some of what I have seen threatens to consume me with nearly overwhelming dispair?//

Shaking my head physically in an attempt to clear it, I focus my thoughts once more.<<else>><<endif>>

<<if ($PlayerEttiquette + $PlayerCharisma) gte 10>>"What you say is just between the two of us,"  I push, "I'm not going to tell him, or anyone else, what you say."

Ms Sherwood's gaze meets mine for several long seconds.  I start to think I'm not going to get any more out of her, but then she answers, "Aaron isn't the nice man he pretends to be,"  She says in almost a whisper, her eyes downcast as she speaks.  

I wait, expecting her to say more, but she doesn't continue.

Sighing inwardly, I decide its best to change the subject for now.  <<else>>I consider pushing for more, but I'm afraid I'll rattle her bad enough that I can't learn anything more from

passages.name: I head below deck to gather my meager belongings.
passages.content:
The sailors are still working to moor the ferry to the dock when I hop down onto the old but solid wooden planks.

I stumble slightly as I land on the dock, taking a couple of staggering steps before I regain my footing.  I cast a sheepish look over my shoulder at the ferry, but if anyone saw my little display, they show no signs of it.

Adjusting the rucksack over my shoulder, I take a few seconds to take in my surroundings.

[img[images/Allure docks.png]]

I find myself standing on the East most of three heavily weathered docks jutting out into the cove.  At a glance, I can see a large fishing boat tied off at the next dock over.  Otherwise, the cove appears to be relatively empty.

Right at the end of the dock I am standing on, I can see a large wooden arch, a sign hanging from the top of the arch.  Engraved in the simple wooden sign is "Welcome to Wanton Cove".  My eyes linger on the weathered sign for only a moment before looking away.

I can see a hint of fog clinging to the rocks along the shore, swirling as gentle waves break along the jagged stones.  

For each of the three docks, there appears to be a dedicated warehouse, each as battered and worn as the dock I'm standing on.

At the end of the dock, I can see a man standing beside the warehouse, casually looking in my direction.  He's an old sailor, a brown cap pulled down over his ears, a red and black plaid jacket pulled tightly around himself.  His harsh gaze is in stark contrast with his surprisingly soft features.

From where I'm standing, I can see right into the heart of Wanton Cove.  Down the street, I can see a small assortment of businesses, wooden signs in various states of disrepair hanging above their doors.  A couple of blocks down the street, the road splits.  

To the left is the bulk of the small town, an assortment of small houses, many with small tendrils of smoke drifting lazily from their chimneys.

To the right, the road appears to wind into an area with maybe h

passages.name: Ask Azumi about her image being in the Leatherbound Tome.
passages.content:
//It’s worth a try, right?//  I think to myself, reaching for the leather bound tome beneath my jacket.  Her expression turns serious, like a shadow falling over her soul when the book comes into view.  Watching her reaction, I open the book to the place I saw her likeness before.  Her eyes widen at the sight, and her cock begins to swell.  “A picture of me?”  The awe in her voice is enough to make my heart race with joy.  

I somehow pull my gaze away from her cock and look her in the eyes, “I don’t know.  It’s something I found, and I wanted to see if it meant anything to you.”

“Am I really that pretty?”  Her voice is filled with innocence and wonder.

“Yes,”  I manage to say softly, “You really are that pretty,”  //You're as beautiful as a goddess.//

She bites her lip, a smile barely touching her eyes, “I’d fuck me.”

The simple statement seems so absurd that it snaps me back to the present.  Closing the book, she makes a slight pout.  Once the book is out of sight, she crawls back up onto the bed again, moving to my side and pressing herself against me.

passages.name: Cave9
passages.content:
Deep within the cavernous cave, I look upwards, taking in the enormity of the space.  //It kind of feels like a Cathedral, a dark and evil one.//  I grimace.

<<if $Madness gte 3>>The darkness overhead shifts subtly, and I freeze in place.

//She's here.//  I think with a sickening finality.  Part of me wants to scream, to run away.  But another part of me wants nothing more than to open my arms and be accepted into //her// embrace.

When the seconds tick past, and nothing happens, I finally take a deep breath to center myself before looking closer at what is overhead.  

There among the rocky nooks and crannies at the top of the cave, I see the movement again, only this time, instead of seeing tentacles reaching out to me, I see smaller movements instead.  I experience a moment of panic as I try to imagine what new horror awaits me, but when one of the small dark shapes falls loose and flies to another spot on the celing, I realize it is only a bad.

I laugh, the sound part relief, and even I can't ignore that its part madness.  The sound startles some of the bats, several more of them falling free of the ceiling and moving to another place.  It takes a minute or more before the bats settle back down.

<<elseif $Madness is 2>>The darkness overhead seems to swell, shifting in the light of my flashlight.  

In the blink of an eye, my heart is in my throat as I try to fight back a rising wave of panic.

//She's here!//

A sound, like a captured animal escapes my lips.  The sound echoes loudly and is returned by a thousand screeches.

All at once, the entire ceiling seems to move, surging first towards me, then in every direction at once, a thunderous roar filling the air.

I duck, fighting down blind panic. 

When the sound finally fades, I cautiously open my eyes again, lookig around.  After a few seconds, I come to the realization that what I had seen was not shadow given form, but instead was a host of bats living on the ceiling.

<<elseif

passages.content:
<<if $CharHP lte 1>><<set $CharHP = 1>><</if>><<if $GooCounter is 0>>Crossing the bar, you're stopped by someone grabbing your arm.

Turning, you come face to face with a gruff looking sailor, "You owe me an apology,"  He growls, "You made me spill my drink."

I look at the empty glass in the man's hand, then at the floor near his feet, seeing no sign of the fluid.

<<if $PlayerEttiquette gte 12>>"Sorry friend,"  I say in an effort to defuse the situation, "Maybe I can buy you another to make up for it?"  

"How about you let me shove this glass up your ass instead,"  He growls.  

I barely have time to react before the man throws a punch at me.  <<elseif $PlayerEttiguette gte 7>>"Sorry about that,"  I say, taking half a step away from him.

"You don't sound sorry,"  He growls.

As he reaches for me with one hand, I see his other hand balling into a fist.<</if>>

<<set $GooCounter += 1>><<elseif $GooCounter is 1>>Someone lashes out with their foot, sending me staggering.

As I catch my balance again, I turn.  A man, sitting at a table with several other people, chuckles.

"What was that about?"  I demand, my tone more angry than I had intended.

"Maybe I just don't like you?"  The sailor says with a growl.

I bristle at the man's tone, biting back a retort.

"No bright comeback?"  He laughs again, coming to his feet.

I fight the urge to reach for my .38 Special, I ball my hands into fists instead.  

The man steps closer, his own hands balling into fists.

<<set $GooCounter += 1>><<elseif $GooCounter gte 2>>Beside me, I can see a woman bouncing up and down in one man's lap, her long skirt hiked up around her hips.  I can see another woman under the table, her face near where the two people's groins touch as her tongue lapping at the man's balls.

When the man sees me glance his way, he becomes visibly annoyed, "Wait your fucking turn,"  He growls.

[IF $CharHP lte 1][/IF][IF $GooCounter is 0]穿越酒吧時，有人抓住你的手臂攔住了你。

轉身，你迎面看到一個面相粗獷的水手，「你欠我一個道歉，」他低吼道，「你害我打翻了酒。」

我看了看男人手中的空酒杯，又看了看他腳邊的地板，沒看到任何液體的痕跡。

[IF $PlayerEttiquette gte 12]「抱歉了朋友，」我試圖緩和局面，「要不我再請你一杯作為補償？」

「不如讓我把這杯子塞進你屁眼裡，」他咆哮道。

我幾乎來不及反應，男人便朝我揮拳襲來。[ELSEIF $PlayerEttiguette gte 7]「真是抱歉，」我說著，朝他退開半步。

「聽起來可不怎麼抱歉，」他低吼道。

他一手伸向我時，我看見他另一隻手已握成拳頭。[/IF]

[ELSEIF $GooCounter is 1]有人猛地踢出一腳，讓我踉蹌不穩。

我重新站穩後轉過身。一個與幾人同桌而坐的男人正咯咯笑著。

「你這是幹什麼？」我質問，語氣比預期更憤怒。

「可能我就是看你不順眼？」水手低吼道。

我被他的語氣激怒，強忍著回嘴的衝動。

我壓下伸手掏點三八特製手槍的衝動，改將雙手握成拳頭。

男人逼近一步，他自己的拳頭也握緊了。

[ELSEIF $GooCounter gte 2]在我身旁，我看見一個女人在一個男人的大腿上上下晃動，她的長裙被撩到臀部周圍。桌下還有另一個女人，她的臉貼近兩人胯部相接處，正用舌頭舔舐男人的睪丸。

當男人瞥見我看向他那邊，他明顯惱怒起來，「他媽的排隊等著，」他低吼道。

我轉開視線，準備從那男人與兩位女士交纏的地方走開。

一聲驚叫將我的注意力拉回男人身上。他正在繫皮帶，而剛才還在他腿上的女人正從地上爬起來。

「我跟你說話時別想走開！」男人踉蹌著朝我撲來。

[/IF][Fight]

passages.name: MidTaintEnd4
passages.content:
One part of my mind breaks, unable to accept what I see unfolding before me.  What small scrap of sanity that I still possess withering and dying in a single moment of acceptance.

The other part of my mind rejoices, looking on with an almost child like wonder.

Just like in my dream the night before last, I see movement beneath the bloody skin, like slugs moving beneath the flesh.  Then the movement reaches one of the cuts on <<if $MorichiDaughter gte 3>> Azumi's <<else>>the hermaphrodite's <</if>>chest, just under her right breast.

I lean back, breathless as I watch.  Two fingers slide through the cut, stretching toward me.  The blood on the fingers beads, running from the skin like a bead of water on oil.

The fingers withdraw, and for a single horrified second I think that //she// is leaving, that //she// has decided that now is //not// the time of her birth.  But before I can react, the body beneath me heaves violently enough to knock me from the table.  I can hear ribs breaking and the sickening wet sound of flesh being torn.  

While the sacrifice is surely dead, she makes a sound as air is forced from her lifeless lungs.  I can also see the body moving, <<if $MorichiDaughter gte 3>>Azumi's <<else>>the hermaphrodite's <</if>>impressive cock jerking as it seems to harden.  

I quickly loose interest in <<if $MorichiDaughter gte 3>>Azumi's <<else>>the hermaphrodite's <</if>>member however as I see a perfect porcelain arm thrust forward.  Almost immediately, a head and shoulder follow.

Before the second arm begins to emerge, //she// pauses, tilting her head unnaturally one direction, then the other.  

My racing heart skips a beat when her head turns toward me and stops.  

I am uncertain if she intends me as a tribute, or if she had other plans for me, but I rejoice in knowing that //I have been chosen.  Even useless as I am, I have been chosen.//

For one fleeting second, panic causes me to scramble backward, putting a few paces distance
passages.content_zh:
我一部分心智崩潰了，無法接受眼前展開的景象。僅存的那一絲理智在認清現實的瞬間枯萎消亡。

另一部分心智卻歡欣鼓舞，以近乎孩童般的好奇注視著這一切。

就像前天夜裡的夢境，我看到血淋淋的皮膚下有東西在蠕動，宛如蛞蝓在血肉下穿行。接著，蠕動抵達了[IF $MorichiDaughter gte 3] Azumi [ELSE]那位陰陽人[/IF]胸口的一道切口，就在她右乳下方。

我向後傾身，屏息觀看。兩根手指從切口中滑出，朝我伸展。手指上的血珠凝聚，像油面上的水珠般從皮膚滑落。

手指縮了回去，在驚恐的一瞬間，我以為她要離開了，以為她判定此刻並非她誕生的時機。但未及反應，我身下的軀體劇烈抽搐，猛力將我從桌上震落。我聽見肋骨斷裂的聲響，以及血肉被撕裂的噁心濕黏聲。

儘管祭品確已死亡，當空氣被擠出她無生命的肺部時，她仍發出一聲悶響。我還看見軀體在動，[IF $MorichiDaughter gte 3]Azumi [ELSE]那位陰陽人[/IF]那驚人的陽具抽搐著，彷彿正在硬挺。

但我很快對[IF $MorichiDaughter gte 3]Azumi [ELSE]那位陰陽人[/IF]的性器失去興趣，因為我看見一隻完美無瑕的瓷白手臂猛然伸出。幾乎同時，頭顱與肩膀跟著鑽出。

在第二隻手臂開始顯現前，她停頓下來，頭顱以不自然的姿勢歪向一邊，又歪向另一邊。

當她的頭轉向我並停住時，我狂跳的心臟漏了一拍。

我不確定她是將我視為祭品，抑或對我另有安排，但我因自己被選中而欣喜。即便我如此無用，我仍被選中了。

在短暫的一瞬間，恐慌使我向後爬退，在她從祭壇末端踏上地面時，拉開了幾步距離。

passages.name: WomanSpitMain
passages.content:
<<if $Stranger is "Played">><<set $random to random (1,2)>><<if $random is 1>>She rounds the corner of a shop on the main street just before I reach it.  

The woman in the heavy raincoat stops, glaring at me, "//Filth,//"  She hisses, then spits at my feet before pushing past me and continuing on her way.

//What a bitch,//  I think, watching her go for a moment before turning my attention back to what I was doing before the unpleasant encounter.

<<else>>The moment I see the woman in the raincoat, I tense up.  My hand aches with the desire to reach into my jacket and take hold of my .38 Special.  //I'm not sure if she's just mean, or unhinged.  Either way, the look in her eyes, she's dangerous.//

I give her plenty of room as we pass one another even though it means I have to muck my way through mud and filth to do so.  She spits at the ground at my feet and mumbles something unsavory under her breath as she passes.

<</if>><<else>>I see a woman walking down the main street.  She has a heavy raincoat on and a hood that pulls up around her head.  You can still see her pale face and just a hint of black hair framing her head around the hood.

As she walks, she does so with a careful step and a steady pace, her movements taking on a surprising grace.

When you draw closer to her, her cold angry eyes turn toward you.  You've just started to open your mouth to call a soft greeting when she speaks first, "Go fuck yourself stranger, your kind isn't welcome in Wanton Cove."

I'm so shocked by the unexpected hostility that I stop right where I'm standing.  Turning on my heel, I prepare to confront her, but I discover her standing only half a dozen paces behind me, facing toward me.  I can't help but notice the way her hand is inside her raincoat.  The front of the coat is open just enough to reveal a bit of her expensive looking dress, and the positioning of her hand leading me to believe her hand is resting on the handle of a sidearm concealed beneath her

[IF $Stranger is "Played"][IF $random is 1]就在我抵達前，她從大街上一間店鋪的轉角處拐了出來。

穿著厚重雨衣的女人停下腳步，瞪視著我，「髒東西。」她嘶聲道，朝我腳邊啐了一口，接著推開我繼續走她的路。

真是個賤人，我心想，目送她離開片刻後，才將注意力轉回遭遇這不愉快插曲前我正在做的事情上。

[ELSE]一看到那個穿雨衣的女人，我立刻繃緊了神經。我的手因渴望伸進夾克握住那把.38特製手槍而隱隱作痛。我不確定她只是刻薄，還是精神失常。無論如何，她眼中的神態說明她很危險。

我們擦身而過時，我給了她足夠的空間，即使這意味著我得踩過泥濘和污穢。她經過時，朝我腳邊的地面吐了口唾沫，並低聲嘟囔了些不堪入耳的話。

她行走時步伐謹慎而穩健，動作帶著一種驚人的優雅。

當你靠近她時，她那冰冷憤怒的雙眼轉向你。你剛張開嘴想輕聲打個招呼，她卻先開口了：「去死吧，陌生人，你這種人在萬頓灣不受歡迎。」

我緩緩吐氣，再次開始行走。走了至少十幾步，我才敢冒險回頭瞥一眼。那女人已不見蹤影。[/IF]

passages.name: Aaron
passages.content:
I decide to have a talk with the bartender.  Carefully, I pick my way across the tavern towards him.

<<if $Aaron is "Played">>When I arrive at the bar, I catch Aaron's eye.  

He doesn't look happy about it, but he reluctantly makes his way over to you.  "What can I get you?"

"[[I've got some questions]]."  I reply. <<else>>When I reach the bar, I sit down, waiting for the bartender to take notice of me.  

For several minutes, I sit patiently at the bar, waiting for him to turn towards me.  Meanwhile, he makes a conscious effort to avoid acknowledging me.  

I start to think I'm going to have to do something to force his attention on me, but I manage to catch his eyes when he glances at me through the mirror behind the bar.  

I see his jaw clench when I hold his gaze.

When he turns towards me, his face is plastered with a too-happy grin, and he extends a hand towards me, "I'm Aaron, what can I get you?"

I take his hand, smiling back as if he hadn't just spent over five minutes trying to ignore me until I go away, "Name's <<print $PlayerName>>, and I heard you might have a room for rent.

Aaron crosses his arms over his chest, giving you a defiant stare, his smile faltering, "Fifty bucks a night."  He says flatly.

<</if>><<if $Room is "Not Played">><<if ($PlayerCharisma + $PlayerStreetwise) gte 11>>"Tell you what, make it thirty a night and I'll pay you for a week up front."

Aaron seems to consider for a moment, "Alright."  He finally says.  

Reaching under the bar, he produces a key which he sets on the bar before me.

I pull my billfold, rolling off a pile of twenties and tens to cover the fee.  When I set the money on the counter, I pick up the key, I see the number two on the little piece of plastic dangling from it.  

"[[Thanks.|Aaron][$Money -= 210]]"  I say.<<set $Aaron to "Played">><<else>>"Guess I don't have much choice, do I?"

"Not really,"  He admits, a hint of a smile playing at his lips.

Not seeing another opti
passages.content_zh:
我決定和酒保談談。小心翼翼地穿過酒館，朝他走去。

[IF $Aaron is "Played"]當我走到吧檯時，我捕捉到Aaron的目光。

他看起來不太樂意，但還是勉強朝你走來。「需要什麼？」

「[我有幾個問題]。]」我回答。[ELSE]當我抵達吧檯，我坐下等待酒保注意到我。

我在吧檯耐心坐了好幾分鐘，等著他轉向我。與此同時，他刻意迴避與我眼神接觸。

我開始覺得必須做些什麼來吸引他的注意，但當他透過吧檯後的鏡子瞥向我時，我成功對上了他的視線。

當我緊盯他的眼睛時，我看見他的下巴繃緊了。

他轉向我時，臉上掛著過度開心的笑容，並朝我伸出手：「我是Aaron，需要什麼？」

我握住他的手，回以微笑，彷彿他剛才沒有花超過五分鐘試圖無視我直到我離開。「我叫$PlayerName，聽說你可能有房間出租。」

Aaron雙臂交叉抱胸，帶著挑釁的眼神瞪著你，笑容減弱：「一晚五十塊。」他平淡地說。

[/IF][IF $Room is "Not Played"][IF ($PlayerCharisma + $PlayerStreetwise) gte 11]「這樣吧，算三十塊一晚，我預付一週的錢。」

Aaron似乎考慮了一下，「好吧。」他終於說道。

他伸手到吧檯下，拿出一把鑰匙放在我面前的吧檯上。

我掏出皮夾，數出一疊二十元和十元鈔票支付費用。當我把錢放在櫃檯上並拿起鑰匙時，我看見鑰匙上掛著的小塑膠牌寫著數字二。

「[[謝了。|Aaron][$Money -= 210]]」我說。[ELSE]「看來我沒什麼選擇，對吧？」

「確實沒有，」他承認，嘴角隱約帶著一絲笑意。

沒有其他選擇，我伸手去拿皮夾。

對面的Aaron伸手到吧檯下，拿出一把鑰匙放在我面前。

我開始數出一疊二十元和十元鈔票支付一週的房租。再次迎上他的目光，我把錢放在吧檯上，同時拿走了鑰匙。鑰匙上掛著一個小塑膠牌，上面寫著數字二。

「[[謝了。|Aaron][$Money -= 350]]」我說。[/IF][/IF]

[IF $Room is "Played"][離開酒保。][/IF]

passages.name: Ask Aaron about the Ruins.
passages.content:
"I read briefly about the ruins on this island in the local history book at the general store,"  I begin, "From what I've been able to learn so far, it looks like the fountain in the park might be part of all that."

"Figures,"  Aaron mumbles, then clearer, "I wouldn't know for sure.  But I think the fountain is part of all that.

He doesn't offer anything further, so I pry, "Any chance you can point me in the right direction, I'd like a chance to take a look at them while I'm here in Wanton Cove."

Aaron's face kind of pinches, an expression I associate with someone biting back some retort, "It's easy to get lost out in the forest, but its supposed to be out there somewhere,"  He pauses, wiping a spot on the bar that doesn't appear any more dirty than any other part, "I wouldn't go looking for it though, even if you could find it without getting lost, its just a bunch of old stone buildings so overgrown you'd walk right past them if you weren't looking for them.  Besides, it would be unfortunate if you went poking around those old buildings and you got hurt.  Places like that tend to be dangerous."

<<if $RuinAaron is "true">>"I heard from someone else that you used to frequent the place as a kid.  I'd think you'd be able to give me some decent directions to find it."  I reply.

He scowls, wiping at the counter, then replies without quite looking at me, "Its out past the Pa-"  He cuts himself off quickly, "Just follow the main trail leading out into the forest.  Its just a little ways past a big clearing in the trees."

"See, that wasn't so hard,"  I can't help but retort.

<</if>><<if $RuinsOpen is "Played">><<else>>//Does he not want me to go there for some reason?// I wonder.  Instead, I answer, "Thanks for the warning, I'll keep it in mind.<</if>>

Aaron grimaces, but doesn't comment further.

passages.content_zh:
由於我的衣服，尤其是褲子，緊繃地裹在身上，我脫下了褲子和內褲，然後解開不久前尚未撕開的襯衫鈕扣。

只穿著一件敞開的襯衫和我的長外套，我低頭看著自己硬挺的陰莖，驚嘆於那鼓脹的陽具隨著我的心跳微微彈動。

感受到慾望的脹痛，我用一隻大手握住自己的長度，開始緩緩擼動。

我才剛開始，就注意到一個聲音。某種介於低吟與誦唱之間的聲響，夾雜著情慾的輕柔呻吟，斷斷續續地出現在我聽力所能捕捉的邊緣。

有了可以專注的事物，我將思緒從當下的慾望狀態中抽離。

我開始緩緩轉圈，試圖判斷聲音來自哪個方向，但這很困難。就在我快要轉完一圈時，我注意到一片比其他區域更暗的霧氣。並非因為霧氣稀薄，而是彷彿有什麼東西正從霧中汲取深度與實質。

好奇心戰勝了謹慎，我朝那片黑暗走了幾步。當我意識到自己看到的只是先前那片虛無的漆黑時，我迅速止步。

我轉身朝我放置褲子的方向望去，卻驚恐地踉蹌後退，發現那片虛無離我的臉只有幾英寸遠。

在我跌倒之前，我聽見靴子撞擊木頭的聲音。我迅速伸手，抓住了一截粗厚的繩索。我慌亂地環顧四周，試圖重新辨識方位，卻發現自己不知何故正站在先前那座繩橋上，或者可能是另一座類似的橋。

雖然只退了一步就踏上了橋，但我卻花了十幾步，雙腳才再次觸及那片棕灰色的土地。

我左右張望，試圖再次找到那片黑暗。當我試圖理解自身處境時，感覺頭腦天旋地轉。我質疑自己的理智，質疑這是否是一場夢，而最深切的感受是，我此刻未能領悟某種深奧的事物——彷彿我是一個異類，身處一個從未打算容納我這般理解力有限的存在的地方。而最讓我感到震撼的是，我知道這裡發生的一切遠遠超出了我的理解，我將再也無法以同樣的方式看待這個世界。這還是我的世界嗎？抑或這裡已是別處？

[繼續。]

passages.name: MidTaintEndAfter1
passages.content:
One month Later

//I huddle in the shadows, careful not to be seen.  //They will see me if I'm not careful,// I think, fighting back a mad giggle.  

The sound of a vehicle driving past the building I am hiding beside is enough to make me press hard against the wall, trying to make myself as hard to see as humanly possible.

I blanch as the driver looks my way, the tentacles that make up the bottom half of his face twist and curl, but his black eyes are indifferent as he passes.

//If he saw me, he'll send others,// I think, making my way deeper into the alley.  

Realizing that its no longer safe to stay here, I reach the other end of the alley and look out across the street.

The two little Deep Ones, both dressed in pretty little pink dresses are playing hopscotch across the street.  They've used a stone to mark the sidewalk for their game, in the place of numbers, strange, but all too familiar symbols dominate the center of each space.

Reaching under my tattered coat, I close my fist around my only weapon, a long piece of glass with one end wrapped in a bit of fabric to minimize the risk of cutting myself.  

My raw and bloody fingers are testament to its success.

Grinning to myself, //I should go for the little ones.  They're the easiest to kill.//  I think back on the hand full of Deep Ones I've managed to kill since returning to the mainland.

//SHE is looking for me, its the only reason that there would be so many of these vile creatures here.//  Breaking into a sudden sprint, I'm nearly half way across the street before the horn of a vehicle blares from nearby.  

The little Deep Ones scream when they see me running at them, bloody length of glass in my hand, the sound is remarkably human,  //Tricks, so they can hide among humans,// I rationalize, though I don't recall seeing a single human since leaving Wanton Cove.

The driver of the truck in the road appears to be little more than a mass of tentacles, eyes, and razor sharp teeth
passages.content_zh:
一個月後

我蜷縮在陰影中，小心翼翼地不讓自己被看見。如果不夠謹慎，他們就會發現我——我壓抑著一陣瘋狂的竊笑，如此想著。

一輛車駛過我藏身的建築旁，光是那聲音就足以讓我緊貼牆面，試圖讓自己盡可能地難以被察覺。

當駕駛朝我這邊看時，我臉色發白。他下半張臉由觸手構成，那些觸手扭動蜷曲著，但他黑色的眼睛在我經過時卻顯得漠不關心。

如果他看見了我，他會派其他人來——我一邊想著，一邊往巷子更深處移動。

意識到這裡不再安全，我走到巷子另一端，望向對街。

那兩個小深潛者，都穿著漂亮的粉紅色小洋裝，正在對街玩跳房子遊戲。他們用石頭在人行道上標記遊戲格子，每個格子中央不是數字，而是佔據主導的、奇怪卻又過分熟悉的符號。

我伸手探進破爛的外套下，握緊我唯一的武器——一片長玻璃，一端用布條纏繞以降低割傷自己的風險。

我紅腫流血的手指就是它成效的證明。

我對自己咧嘴一笑。我應該先對付小的。他們最容易殺死。我回想著自從回到大陸後，我成功殺掉的那幾個深潛者。

「她」正在找我，這是這裡會有這麼多可憎生物的唯一原因。我突然拔腿狂奔，在附近車輛喇叭響起時，我已經跑到街道中央了。

當小深潛者看到我手持血淋淋的玻璃朝他們衝去時，他們尖叫起來——那聲音驚人地像人類。詭計，這樣他們才能隱藏在人類之中——我如此合理化，儘管我不記得離開萬頓灣後還見過任何人類。

路上那輛卡車的駕駛，看起來就像一團觸手、眼睛和利齒的聚合體。它持續按著喇叭，發出難以理解、讓我頭痛的囈語，好幾條觸手在車窗外瘋狂揮舞。

我經過時對那醜惡的東西尖聲咒罵。等沒有這麼多大型怪物在附近保護他們時，我會回來收拾這些小傢伙。

我知道待在空曠處只會讓他們更容易找到我，於是改變方向，衝向最近巷子的陰影中。

AHAGL AH MG ILLL AHEE N'GHFT。

[新遊戲+。]

passages.name: BridgeWalk
passages.content:
Each careful step causes a new creak or groan from the old rope bridge.  Occasionally I freeze up as I hear a board start to splinter beneath my boot.  

I'm nearly half way across the bridge when I dare to look down again.  The sag in the middle of the bridge makes it easier to see what is below.

Through the fog I can see something white, and fluid.  It moves gently like something is moving just beneath the surface.  <<if $SailorGoo1 is "Played">>The faint glow the substance puts off, accompanied by the smell in the air, I have little doubt that what lies below is a small river of White Goo.  

<<if $Taint lte 25>>Feeling more uneasy than I had before, I wonder at the implications.

<<elseif $Taint gte 95>>I feel a sudden ache in my pussy as a rising tide of arousal leaves me breathless.  Tightening my grip on the rope bridge, I spend several seconds fighting back the urge to simply dive over the side of the bridge into the stuff.

I take a deep breath in an attempt to calm myself, but it only serves to make things worse as I fill my nostrils with the heady scent of the White Goo.  For several minutes I stand there, focusing my thoughts once more.

<<elseif $Taint lte 45>>My heart suddenly races at the implications.  My eyes widening in horror as I wonder what so much of the stuff would do to me.

<</if>><<else>>Looking down, I can see that the stuff puts off a faint glow, and part of me wonders if the stuff accounts for the salty sweet smell in the air.

Though I find myself curious about what the stuff might be, the rational part of my mind suggests that it may be better if I don't find out.

<</if>>I don't know if its because I stood in place for too long, or if there was some other reason, but with a sudden cracking sound that echoes loudly in the fog, the board beneath my feet splinters and gives way.

<<if $GooCounter is 0>>A happy looking serving girl with a bosom threatening to spill from her low cut top meets my eyes for only a second as she weaves between the tables of the Barmaid's Daughter on her way to a table in the middle of the room.  

Somehow, even through her frequent pivots and giggles she manages to make it to the table without spilling any of the drinks she's holding in her hands.

<<set $GooCounter += 1>><<elseif $GooCounter is 1>>I notice a girl standing near one of the tables.  From where I'm standing, she looks to be one of the serving girls, likely at the table to take an order.

However I can also see that she is holding the front of her skirts up above her waist, the hand of the man closest to her seems to be somewhere in front of her. After a particularly powerful shudder, the woman steps back half a step suddenly, letting her skirts fall back down around her ankles.  The man grins, raising his wetly glistening fingers to his lips where he licks the woman's juices from his finger tips.

<<set $GooCounter += 1>><<else>>Two androgynous looking people, my best guess is that they are female from the swelling of their chests, sit together near one wall.  

Their body language suggests to me that they are uneasy and self conscious.  While they seem to be trying to enjoy their drinks, I catch both of them casting nervous glances toward the men near them.

<<if  $Taint lte 50>>My gaze lingers on the pair with a growing sense that there's more to what I am seeing than I understand.  Unfortunately, being unsure about what I am seeing, I don't know how to broach the subject.

<<elseif $Taint lte 94>>I can feel something twist deep in my gut, //Is that what I'm becoming?//  The thought drifts through my mind like
passages.content_zh:
[IF $GooCounter is 0]一名笑容滿面的女侍，低胸上衣幾乎兜不住她豐滿的胸脯，當她在「酒保的女兒」酒館的桌間穿梭、走向房間中央的桌子時，目光與我僅交會了一秒。

儘管她頻頻轉身、不時輕笑，卻奇蹟般地穩住手中所有飲料，一滴未灑地抵達了那張桌子。

[ELSEIF $GooCounter is 1]我注意到一名女孩站在某張桌子旁。從我的位置看去，她似乎是女侍之一，大概正在桌邊為客人點餐。

但我同時也看見她將裙襬提到了腰際，而最靠近她的那名男子，手似乎正放在她身前。從我位於女子後方的視角來看，無需太多想像便能明白——那男人的手正覆在她私處或附近，尤其當我瞥見她如何傾身靠向男子、身體不時因快感而顫慄時，更確信了這點。

一陣格外劇烈的顫抖後，女子突然向後退了半步，讓裙襬重新垂落至腳踝。男子咧嘴一笑，將濕潤發亮的手指舉到唇邊，舔去了指尖上屬於女子的蜜液。

[ELSE]兩名外貌中性的身影——從她們胸前的隆起判斷，我猜應是女性——靠牆坐在一起。

她們的肢體語言透露出不安與自覺。雖然她們試圖享受飲品，我卻瞥見兩人都不時緊張地瞟向身旁的男人們。

[IF $Taint lte 50]我的目光駐留在這對人影上，愈發強烈地感覺到眼前景象隱含著某種我無法理解的深意。可惜，由於無法確定自己究竟看見了什麼，我不知該如何開啟這個話題。

[ELSEIF $Taint lte 94]我感到某種東西在腸胃深處糾結——那難道就是我正在變成的模樣嗎？這念頭如幽暗魅影般掠過腦海，無論我多努力試圖忽略，它仍持續侵擾著我的思緒。

[ELSE]一陣微小的戰慄竄過我全身，因為我知道，那些正注視著那兩位女士的男人們，也正以同樣飢渴的眼神盯著我。

情慾的潮紅湧上，我加快腳步，試圖將注意力——哪怕只是暫時——轉移到其他事物上。

[/IF][/IF][繼續前行。]

passages.name: Have a bath drawn.
passages.content:
It takes a little while to get everything arranged, but eventually, you have a hot bath drawn for you. 

The big metal tub dominating the middle of your room is a little more than half full of steaming water, filling the entire room with a slight haze.

<<if $Taint lte 39>>Stepping into the tub, I have a sharp intake of breath when the hot water touches my heavy balls.

Letting the breath out slowly, I ease myself into the water.  After a few seconds, I sigh, enjoying the feel of the heat seeping into my body after a long cold day.

[[Enjoy the bath.]]
[[masturbate in the bath.|MalemasturbateBath]]

<<elseif $Taint lte 54>>I step into the tub, easing into the hot water.  I make a hissing noise as my breath catches in my throat the moment the water hits my genitalia.

My breath escapes slowly, almost like a sigh as I start to slip deeper in the water.

//This is nice,//  I think as the heat starts to seep into my body.

My breath comes in short breaths as I slide beneath the water, the heat just shy of too hot.

A hint of thrill goes through me, and I raise a single perfect leg and ease it beneath the waters, causing barely a ripple as I begin to slip beneath the hot water.

I'm intimately aware of how different the water feels on my body than it did before my body started to change.  The heat is hot, but I no longer feel the same knee jerk need to shy away from the heat.  When the water reaches my crotch, it burns, but not the same as when the hot water had touched my cock and balls in the past.  Instead the hot water seems to trace along my slit, like something alive trying to slip between my folds.  

I shudder at the though
passages.content_zh:
安排一切花了點時間，但最終，你還是為自己準備好了一缸熱水浴。

占據房間中央的大型金屬浴缸已注滿過半的蒸騰熱水，整個房間瀰漫著一層薄薄的水霧。

[IF $Taint lte 39]踏進浴缸時，熱水觸及我沉甸甸的睪丸，讓我倒抽一口氣。

緩緩吐息，我讓身體逐漸沉入水中。幾秒後，我滿足地嘆了口氣，享受在漫長寒冷的一天後，熱度滲透進身體的感覺。

[享受沐浴。]
[在浴缸中自慰。]

[ELSEIF $Taint lte 54]我踏進浴缸，緩緩浸入熱水。當水流沖擊到我的生殖器時，喉頭一緊，發出了嘶嘶的吸氣聲。

隨著身體更深地滑入水中，氣息緩緩逸出，幾乎像一聲嘆息。

熱度開始滲入身體，我想，這感覺真不錯。

[享受沐浴。]
[在浴缸中自慰。]

[ELSEIF $Taint lte 94]當我開始將身體浸入熱水時，那溫度讓我頓了一下。

我滑入水面下，熱度幾乎燙得令人難以承受，呼吸隨之變得短促。

[享受沐浴。]
[在浴缸中自慰。]

[ELSE]我走近浴缸，望著靜止水面上蒸騰的熱氣。

一絲顫慄般的興奮掠過，我抬起一條完美無瑕的腿，輕輕探入水中，當我開始滑進熱水時，水面幾乎未起漣漪。

我清晰地意識到，水接觸身體的感覺已與肉體開始變化前截然不同。水很燙，但我不再有那種本能退縮的反應。當熱水淹至我的胯下時，帶來一陣灼熱感，卻不同於過去熱水觸及陰莖與睪丸時的刺激。相反地，熱水彷彿沿著我的縫隙流動，像某種活物試圖鑽入我的褶皺之間。

這念頭讓我渾身一顫，有那麼一瞬間，我彷彿看見某種滑膩、活生生的東西正試圖擠進我體內。

心跳失控地狂飆了好幾秒，我才重新穩住呼吸。而在內心深處，我並不確定那陣心悸究竟是源於恐懼，還是興奮。

暫且擱下這思緒，我讓自己完全沉入浴缸。

[享受沐浴。]
[在浴缸中自慰。]
[/IF]

passages.name: Church1
passages.content:
<<if $Madness gte 3>>Just before I step over the threshold, //her// voice becomes agitated as it echoes inside of my skull.  The sound turning to something eerily similar to laughter before suddenly stopping.

The sudden loss of her whispers stops me dead in my tracks.  Part of me wants to step back outside, to hear the comforting, //arousing// sound of her voice again, while the rest of me rejoices at the sudden freedom from her grasp.

I make it only a couple of steps into the old abandoned church before I sag to my knees, laughing and crying both at the relief I feel.

Hearing only the sound of my own thoughts in my head, I blink through the tears, looking at the church and wondering if this holy place is somehow able to keep //her// at bay.

My whole life, I had struggled with the concept of God, never being a real believer, but for a few seconds I allowed myself to believe that there might just be something bigger than myself out there that could possibly protect me from this ever present corruption I find myself surrounded by.

I nearly open my mouth to utter a prayer, but quickly dismiss the notion.  //If there is a God out there, he doesn't give a damn about me, or this place.//

<<elseif $Madness is 2>>When I enter the old abandoned church, the hairs on the back of my neck stand on end.  

It only takes me a second to realize that the constant soft whispers in the back of my mind have silenced.

A tremor goes through my body, though I am not enirely sure if its because of relief that the whispers have stopped, or because the sound of her voice has been so suddenly ripped away from me.

//This place, it must somehow hold her at bay.//

I start to laugh, unshed tears welling up in my eyes as a sense of relief washes over me.  

After a while, I regain my composure.  My temporary relief dashed as I realize I have to leave this church eventually.

<<elseif $Madness is 1>>A sense of unease washes over me as I step over the threshold into
passages.content_zh:
[IF $Madness gte 3]就在我跨過門檻前，她在我腦海中迴盪的聲音變得激動起來。那聲音轉變成某種詭異得近乎笑聲的聲響，然後突然停止。

她低語的驟然消失讓我當場僵住。一部分的我想退回到門外，再次聆聽她那令人安慰又興奮的聲音，而另一部分的我則為突然從她掌控中解脫而欣喜。

我剛踏入這座廢棄老教堂沒幾步，便雙腿一軟跪倒在地，因這份解脫感又哭又笑。

聽著腦中僅存的自己的思緒，我眨去淚水，環視教堂，懷疑這神聖之地是否真能將她阻擋在外。

我一生都在與上帝的概念搏鬥，從未成為真正的信徒，但此刻有那麼幾秒鐘，我容許自己相信，或許真存在某種比我更強大的力量，能保護我免於周遭這無所不在的腐化。

我幾乎要開口禱告，但隨即打消了念頭。如果真有上帝存在，祂才不會在乎我，或這個地方。

[ELSEIF $Madness is 2]當我走進這座廢棄老教堂時，頸後的寒毛直豎。

我立刻察覺到，腦海中持續不斷的輕柔低語已然沉寂。

一陣顫慄掠過全身，我不太確定這是因為低語停止而鬆了口氣，還是因為她的聲音如此突然地被從我身上剝離。

這個地方，必定有某種力量能暫時阻擋她。

我開始大笑，未落的淚水在眼眶中打轉，一股解脫感淹沒了我。

過了一會兒，我恢復了鎮定。當我意識到自己終究得離開這座教堂時，這短暫的解脫感便煙消雲散。

[ELSEIF $Madness is 1]當我跨過門檻踏入這座廢棄老教堂時，一股不安感襲來。與此同時，那些我甚至未曾察覺存在的輕柔低語，似乎在腦海深處安靜了下來。

這突如其來的寂靜幾乎震耳欲聾。

[ELSE]走進教堂，這地方的死寂讓我感到些許不安。簡直像座墳墓。我陰鬱地想著。

甩開這個念頭，我將注意力拉回到此行的目的上。

[/IF][繼續。]

<<if $TomTanya is "Revealed">><<else>>Arrive in Wanton Cove and begin the search for my former partner's son, Tom.

<<endif>><<if $MotherofLust is "Played">>!!Murder from Four Years Earlier:

I've found reference to a mythological creature; Zal'chaas.  Learn more about her, and how she may tie into this town, and the murder case I was working before I left the police force.<<if $Madness gte 1>>

Whatever she is, I am beginning to believe that this Zal'chaas may in fact be real, and I'm not too proud to admit that the prospect of her being real is enough to fill me with an irrational fear that threatens to overwhelm me.<<endif>>


<<endif>><<if $Pastor is "Played">>!!Pastor's Death:

The Pastor in Wanton Cove committed suicide.  I find it strange that a Pastor would commit suicide in the first place, and the statues in front of the Church seem to suggest something much darker at play.<<if $PastorBook is "Taken">>

I later found a book in the basement of the Church.  Someone, presumably the Pastor, was trying to translate it.  I haven't found anything to tie it to the Pastor's death, but, call it a hunch, I think its connected somehow.<<endif>> 

<<if $JI is "Played">>After talking to both Aaron at the Barmaid's Daughter, and Mr Jackobson in the old part of town, I feel no better about my first impressions.  There's more afoot here than meets the eye.

I need to head up to the bluff, take a look around and see if I can learn more.<<if $JI is "Duncel">>Both Aaron at the Barmaid's Daughter, and Mr Jackobson in the old part of town gave surprisingly similar statements.  

I followed up on their statements by heading up to the Bluff, but that only created more questions than answer.  I'm not likely to get anything more out of Aaron, but Mr Jackobson claims to have witnessed the suicide, so I should speak to him again to clear up some details.<<endif>><<endif>><<if $Compartment is "Played">>

When I returned to the lower room in t

無論她是什麼，我開始相信這個Zal'chaas可能真實存在，而我不得不承認，光是想到她可能真實存在，就足以讓我充滿一種非理性的恐懼，幾乎要將我淹沒。[/IF]

萬頓灣的牧師自殺了。我覺得牧師會自殺本身就很不尋常，而教堂前的雕像似乎暗示著更黑暗的內情。[IF $PastorBook is "Taken"]

後來我在教堂地下室找到一本書。有人，大概是牧師，曾試圖翻譯它。我還沒找到任何直接連結到牧師死亡的證據，但就說是直覺吧，我認為這其中必有關聯。[/IF]

我依據他們的說詞前往懸崖調查，但這只帶來了更多疑問而非答案。我大概無法從Aaron那裡問出更多了，但Jackobson先生聲稱目擊了自殺事件，我應該再找他談談，釐清一些細節。[/IF][/IF][IF $Compartment is "Played"]

當我回到教堂下層房間時，發現有人曾來過，撞倒了房間裡一個巨大的書架。書架後方是一條手工開鑿的隧道，通往一個天然洞室，中央是一個深不見底的深水潭。從水流的動態和空氣中的鹹味判斷，我認為這個水潭應該以某種方式與海洋相連。[/IF][/IF]

passages.name: Dream4
passages.content:
<<set $Bed to "False">><<set $Day += 1>><<set $CharHP = 10>><<set $Research to 0>><<if $Taint gte 100>><<set $Taint += 5>><<elseif $Taint gte 75>><<set $Taint += 4>><<elseif $Taint gte 50>><<set $Taint += 3>><<elseif $Taint gte 25>><<set $Taint += 2>>
<<elseif $Taint gte 1>><<set $Taint += 1>><</if>>//It's too late now,"  The words echo softly in the deep fog that shrouds the world around me.

There's a giggle from somewhere nearby, followed by a sudden pale flash of movement out of the corner of my eye from the opposite direction.

Behind me, the world seems to darken.  An involuntary shiver goes through me, and I start to briskly walk towards the melodious sound of giggling.

I stumble across the uneven ground, not even seeing the details as I follow the retreating sound.

Meanwhile, the darkness behind me seems to loom closer.

"Cha'la'na, thr uch na," //Her// voice whispers behind me.  

"I know!"  I half turn, yelling back at her.

I break into a run, though the faster I move, the more prevaling the darkness becomes as it seems to stretch around me as if prepared to swallow me.

Tripping, I slam hard into the cobblestone road beneath me.  I look back in a panic, expecting //her// to catch me at any moment, but all I can see is the narrow alley behind me, several police officers standing at its end, looking anywhere but at me.

Getting to my feet again, I look in the other direction.  

I blanch at the carnage before me, feeling bile rise up in the back of my throat.

Among all the red is a pale femenine shape laying in a pool of blood, not a drop marring its skin.  

But my attention is instead drawn to what is behind it.  A woman, dressed as I am, is looking straight back at me.  Its the same female version of myself that I had seen the night before in my dreams.//

[img[images/Dream4.jpg]]

A dream!  This is a dream!  //I try to grasp that thought, to hold onto it even as it slips away from me.

Her eyes are filled with sadness
passages.content_zh:
[IF $Taint gte 100][ELSEIF $Taint gte 75][ELSEIF $Taint gte 50][ELSEIF $Taint gte 25]
[ELSEIF $Taint gte 1][/IF]「現在已經太遲了。」這句話在籠罩著我周遭世界的濃霧中輕柔迴盪。

附近某處傳來一陣輕笑，緊接著眼角餘光瞥見對面方向有蒼白的動作一閃而過。

我身後的世界似乎暗了下來。一陣不由自主的顫慄掠過全身，我開始朝著那旋律般的笑聲快步走去。

我在不平的地面上跌跌撞撞，追隨著那逐漸遠去的聲音，甚至看不清周圍的細節。

與此同時，身後的黑暗彷彿越逼越近。

「查拉納，斯烏奇納。」她的聲音在我身後低語。

「我知道！」我半轉過身，朝她大喊。

我拔腿狂奔，然而我跑得越快，黑暗就越是瀰漫，彷彿在我周圍伸展，準備將我吞噬。

一個踉蹌，我重重摔在腳下的鵝卵石路上。我驚慌地回頭，預期她隨時會抓住我，但只看見身後狹窄的巷子，幾名警察站在巷口，目光游移，唯獨不看向我。

我再次站起身，望向另一個方向。

眼前的慘狀讓我臉色煞白，喉頭湧起一股噁心感。

在一片猩紅之中，一具蒼白的女性軀體躺在血泊裡，肌膚上卻未沾染一滴血漬。

但我的注意力卻被它後方的東西吸引。一個女人，穿著與我相同，正直直地回望著我。正是我前一夜夢中見過的那個女性版本的自己。

夢！這是一場夢！我試圖抓住這個念頭，緊緊握著，即使它正從我指間溜走。

她凝視著我，眼中充滿悲傷。血珠從她垂在身側的指尖滴落。「你真的不明白，對吧？」她的聲音帶著一絲驚訝，幾近崇敬。她走近一步，腳觸及她原本站立邊緣的血泊，卻未破開水面。「不過，你很快就會明白了。」

[醒來]

passages.name: nextRuinsFemale
passages.content:
<<if $DeepCombat is "true">>Chest heaving, my breath comes in ragged gasps as I look down on the disgusting thing laying dead at my feet.

Cautiously, I nudge it once with my dirty boot, but elict no response from the thing.  Emboldened, I give it a little kick, resulting in the same lack of response.

After a couple of seconds, I give it a push with my foot again, this time moving it onto its back so I can get a better look at it.  

The crest along the back of its head causes its entire head to list to one side.  A trickle of blackish blood leaking from the corner of its mouth where it siwrls in the water pooled on the strange stone path in the Ruins.  Like its back, its arms, legs, and sides are covered in thick scaly skin.  Not quite true scales, and not quite leather.  Along its belly, the coloration lightens slightly and the scales seem smaller, though after more than one encounter with the creatures, I figure its safe to say that they offer no less protection for it.  A pair of large breasts are on it's chest.  If not for the color, they'd look almost human.  That anything about the creature could seem //human// seems almost absurd.

Finally, my eyes move lower, between its legs where I can see a hint of an opening.  There's a hint of pink inside, and it doesn't take a great thinker to realize that I'm looking at it's vagina. 

With my boot, I step near it's crotch, applying pressure in a way that spreads it open just a little bit for a better look before stepping back.

I take a few moments to consider what I have seen, then away from the body.

[IF $DeepCombat is "true"]胸膛劇烈起伏，我低頭看著腳下那具令人作嘔的屍體，呼吸粗重而破碎。

我謹慎地用髒污的靴子輕推了它一下，但那東西毫無反應。膽子大了些，我輕輕踢了它一腳，結果依舊沒有動靜。

過了幾秒，我又用腳推了它一次，這次將它翻成仰躺，以便看得更清楚。

它後腦的脊冠讓整個頭歪向一側。一股暗黑色的血從嘴角緩緩流出，在遺跡中奇異石徑上的積水中暈開。如同它的背部，它的手臂、雙腿和側腹都覆蓋著厚實的鱗狀皮膚。不完全是真鱗片，也不完全是皮革。腹部顏色稍淺，鱗片似乎較小，但與這些生物交手多次後，我猜這並未減弱其防護力。它胸前有一對巨大的乳房。若不是膚色詭異，它們看起來幾乎像人類的。這生物有任何部分看似人類，都顯得荒謬至極。

最後，我的目光向下移動，看向它雙腿之間，那裡隱約可見一道開口。內部透出一抹粉色，不需多麼聰明也能意識到，我正看著它的陰道。

我用靴子靠近它的胯部，施加壓力使其微微張開，以便看得更清楚，隨後退開。

我花了一點時間思索所見，然後轉身離開屍體。

[繼續。][ELSE]我的目光迅速掃視周圍環境，搜尋附近樹叢，看是否有進一步的威脅跡象。
