passages.name: Tell me more about the pastor.
passages.content:
"Pastor Erkon?"  He asks.

"Yeah, you mentioned that he killed himself.  What happened?"  I ask.

"He got sick.  Started spending a lot of time alone.  Old Jackobson saw him walk up to the bluff and jump."  He looks like he's going to say more, but then stops himself.

<<if ($PlayerCharisma + $PlayerStreetwise) gte 14>>"Anything more you can tell me about him?"  I inquire.

"Some things are best left alone.  And he just couldn't leave things be."  His mouth snaps shut after saying that last bit, his brown eyes glaring at me accusingly.

I know from that look that he isn't going to say anything more on the subject.<</if>>

passages.name: What can you tell me about Wanton Cove?
passages.content:
"What can you tell me about Wanton Cove?"  I ask, my eyes flickering to the door as a way to reassure myself of where the exit is.

"Wannan Cove once be a fine ittle ishing toon,"  Vick nervously looks out one of the cabin's small windows as he continues, "Ah dat changed bout' foh yeahs ago."

Stepping away from the window, his expression grim and serious as he meets my gaze, "Da Passor chang'd fuhst.  Coup'l mons fore he meet hes fate."

Vick clenches, then unclenches his fists, a look of rage passing over his features so quickly I'd have missed it if I had blinked.

"Den da founan quit worken.  Dos gross statoos fron oh da Church peered one evenin.  Da //thedral,//" Vick visibly shudders<<if  ($PlayerPerception + $PlayerIntelligence) gte 11>> and I'm not entirely sure if it in revulsion or extacy<</if>>, "Den sum uh da folks in toon star'ed ah chang."

With a suddenness that startles me, his rancid breath enough to make me gag when his face comes mere inches from my own, "//She// gunna ere //us.//"  His eyes are wide with fear.

//Might be best to leave this alone, at least for now.//  I think as Vick leans away from me in a eerily slow movement.

passages.content:
I glance down at a large rock embedded in the trail, coming to a halt when I notice something.  

Within the stone's surface, its immediately obvious that there are shapes and patterns etched into the rock, though those shapes and patterns are far too faded from the passage of time to make out the entire series of images.  

Most of what I can see are just strange patterns that <<if $Read gte 1>> remind me of the ones I have seen in the old book I found beneath the Church in Wanton Cove<<if $Fountain is "Played">> or even the engravings etched upon the fountain in the little park along main street<</if>>, but its difficult to say for sure.<<else>> I am unfamiliar with the markings, though I do have the nagging feeling that they are somehow familiar.<</if>>

Kneeling down, I pull a bit of the moss away from the stone, revealing a significantly less faded piece of the stone even if it only contains a partial image.  My eyes trace the partial outline of something large, possibly the outline would have once encompassed the entirety of the engravings on the stone.  Part of what looks like it could be a bat's wing is partially visible, I pull at a bit more of the moss, revealing a large eye.  <<if $Madness gte 2>>//It may be watching me,//  I think as I stare at the eye for several long heartbeats. <<else>>As the seconds tick forward, I begin to feel uneasy looking at the eye.  <</if>>Releasing the moss in my hand, it falls to obscure the eye once more.

[[Continue on my way.|Trail]]
passages.content_zh:
我低頭瞥見小徑上嵌著的一塊大石頭，當我注意到某些東西時，停下了腳步。

在石頭表面，可以立刻看出刻有一些形狀和圖案，但那些形狀和圖案因歲月流逝而過於模糊，無法辨識出完整的系列圖像。

我能看到的大部分只是奇怪的圖案，[IF $Read gte 1]讓我想起我在萬頓灣教堂底下找到的那本舊書裡看到的那些[IF $Fountain is "Played"]，甚至是主街小公園裡噴泉上的雕刻[/IF]，但很難確定。[ELSE]我對這些標記並不熟悉，雖然我總有種惱人的感覺，覺得它們似曾相識。[/IF]

我跪下來，從石頭上撥開一些苔蘚，露出一塊褪色程度輕得多的石面，即使上面只包含了一部分圖像。我的目光描摹著某個巨大物體的部分輪廓，或許這個輪廓曾經涵蓋了石頭上所有的雕刻。一部分看起來像是蝙蝠翅膀的東西隱約可見，我又撥開一些苔蘚，露出一隻巨大的眼睛。[IF $Madness gte 2]它可能在注視著我，當我盯著那隻眼睛看了好幾個漫長的心跳時，我這麼想著。[ELSE]隨著時間一秒秒過去，我開始感到注視那隻眼睛的不安。[/IF]我鬆開手中的苔蘚，它落下再次遮住了那隻眼睛。

[繼續上路。]

Aaron looks honestly confused, "I don't know anything about inscriptions on the fountain."

<<if $Madness gte 2>>//Liar!!!  You know!  You're //her// creature!//  A voice in the back of my mind screams madly.  I try to silence it, but it only seems to scream louder.  When my eyes turn to the shadows in one corner of the room, the voice suddenly silences with a whimper.  

It takes me a few seconds, but I'm eventually able to focus my attention back on what Aaron just said.<<endif>>

Sighing inwardly, "Do you know of anyone around Wanton Cove that might know something about it?"  I ask hopefully.

"I'd tell you to talk to the Reverend, but he killed himself about four years ago."

"Name's <<print $PlayerName>>, <<print $PlayerName>> <<print $PlayerName2>>"  I offer the old sailor my hand.

He looks at it, but doesn't take it, "Cobb,"  He replies, his eyes momentarily flickering to our surroundings.

"Mind if I ask you a few questions?"  I ask.

<<if ($PlayerInvestigation + $PlayerIntelligence) gte 10 or ($PlayerPerception + $PlayerIntelligence) gte 7>>My eyes flicker towards his feet. My eyes settle back on his face before he looks back at me again.<<set $SailorGoo1 to "Played">>

passages.name: Ask Phil about the Ruins.
passages.content:
"You're a local, so you might know something,"  I begin.

Phil blows sawdust off of the board he is working on, his eyes flickering to you before returning to his work, "Can't say if I know anything or not until come out with what you want to know."

"Can you tell me about the old ruins here on the island?"  I ask.

He pauses, lowering the piece of lumber in his hands before replying, "Can't say that I know much, unlike some others around here, I tend to stay in town, and even that time is spent mostly in my shop,"  He gestures at the area around himself, "I always used to hear that Aaron would hang out around those old ruins when he was a kid, you might want to ask him about it."

Without further comment, his attention shifts back to the piece of lumber he had been working on, "Sorry I can't be of more help then that."

//Seems like I need to have a good talk with Aaron.// I think darkly.<<set $RuinAaron to "true">>

passages.name: You said your brother was leaving on the fishing boat?
passages.content:
"You mentioned that your brother left on a fishing boat when we talked on the waterfront,"  I say.

A half smile touches her lips, "Since our parents passed a few years back, Felix has been taking care of me.  He gives me whatever he makes fishing, God bless his soul, and I care for the house and do the cooking and cleaning."  Just a hint of color creeps into her cheeks.

//You'd think they were married the way she is behaving.//  I think.

"I think he's been seeing a girl here in town, but he wont tell me anything about her,"  She continues.

//Or maybe not.//  I mentally add to my earlier thought.

"Not much else to tell, he's always been a good brother and a decent, God fearing man.  But I don't see him often because he's gone on one of the fishing boats for days, sometimes weeks, at a time."

Sensing that she does not have much more to add, I change the subject.

passages.name: Happy End 7
passages.content:
As the boat bumps against the dock, I speak up, "That the hell are you doing here?  I thought you'd be hard to find!"  I say loudly to Tom.

Tom tosses his heavy pack to me, then reaches up so I can pull him up onto the ferry's deck, "I'll tell you all about it on our way home.  Right now, I just want to get away from this place, its creepy as hell."

I shake my head slightly, fighting back a laugh.

For a second, I wonder if I should grab my things and hop off the boat, after all, I still have a mystery to solve.

//Take the happy ending for what it is,// I think to myself, though the voice is still like someone else talking to me rather than my own thoughts, //Hear the kid out on the way back to the mainland, maybe I can learn something from him, and if not I can always come back later.//

Reluctantly, I let your gaze drift over the little town again, then turn back to Tom, "Let's get you home, your dad has been worried."  I clap Tom on the shoulder.

"Its good to see you again,"  Tom says with a little smile, and a nervous look toward Wanton Cove.

"You ok?"  

Tom looks a little surprised, "Yeah, I just want to go home to my family, I've seen some weird shit."

"Haven't we all?"  I retort, dark thoughts and half memories stewing in the back of my mind.

//I'll be waiting when next you choose to return to Wanton Cove,// A feminine voice whispers enticingly in the back of my mind.

HAPPY END

passages.name: Go talk to Aaron.
passages.content:
"If it isn't my favorite tennant.'  Aaron says, his tone holds little warmth, "What can I do for you this morning?"

"A couple of sailors,"  I gesture towards the two men I had overheard a little earlier, "were talking about a body on the waterfront yesterday?"

Aaron makes a sour face, takes a deep breath, then starts to talk, "Yeah, happens sometimes, something washes up on shore."

"I'm sure you're right, but it sounds like this one might have been human."  I reply evenly.

He finally stops wiping the counter, "Yeah, it was,"  Aaron's tone suddenly subdued, "Never seen her before. Pale little thing, body mangled on the rocks.  She was crated up and shipped out last night."

Aaron pauses, as if considering what to say.  Finally he reaches down behind the counter, producing a crumpled bit of paper.  He quickly writes something on it, then starts to talk again, "I was out on the waterfront," He pauses almost imperceptively, "When I saw the body on the rocks.  By the time I got down there, Ms Sherwood had spotted it on her morning walk as well."  

He shrugs, "Can't tell you more than that, but Ms Sherwood might be able to tell you more,"  He pushes the paper towards me, I can see that it has an address written on it, "You can find her in the east end of town, she doesn't go out for much but her morning walks.  You should have no trouble finding her."

[[Step away from Aaron.|The Barmaids Daughter]]

passages.name: commonTownEvents
Founder, Zodak Alhazred
passages.content:
[[return|$return]]

Zodak Alhazred was something of an enigma.  Nothing is known about our town's founder before he came to this place.

Of middle eastern descent, he was by historical accounts a tall and slim man, bordering on skeletal.  Rumor has it that he sired half the children in Wanton Cove in its first years.

No one knows what his selection process was for the original settlers of this island, but it is said that the original settlers were paid in gold to come here and start a life in this place.  Some of the descendants of the original settlers still own bits of gold that they claim were part of that payment.

Zodak Alhazred was known to have a brooding, and intense disposition, prone to bouts of anger that quickly subsided.  

He built his own home on the bluff outside the town of Wanton Cove.  A decade later, part of the bluff collapsed, sliding into the sea.  Zodak Alhazred, nor his home, was ever seen again.  

His only known son, a bastard sired with a young girl who lived on the island at that time, spent his life following in his father's footsteps, orchestrating the growth of the fledgling town before he was fully a man.  Towards the middle of his life, he commissioned the construction of a grand mansion in his father's memory on the bluff.  The mansion still stands to this day.

[[Return to Table of Contents.|Wanton Cove History Book]]

[[return|$return]]

passages.name: Do you long for a Happy Ending?
passages.content:
At 48 years of age, I've served my country through the end of the great war.  Afterwards, I joined the force, spent almost twenty-five years trying to make the streets a safer place.  Two of that was spent trying to solve a case that couldn't be solved.  I turned in my badge when they told me to file the case away and move on.

I've lived the two years since at the bottom of a bottle.

I was never a particularly large man.  Standing at 5' 10" and 180lbs. 

My square jaw perpetually sports about three days' worth of stubble, just a hint of gray near the temples.  My brown hair is thick, but cut short.

Above my beak like nose, my grey eyes are sharp and alert. 

I'm not fat, but I'd be lying if I said there wasn't a hint of belly on me, a bit of fat over what was once a well toned body back in my prime.

//Guess all that running in terror I just did wasn't enough to firm it back up again,//  The thought seems strange, making me feel as if something bad just happened, and that I may be stepping into the same all over again.

Slipping out of bed, I make my way across my filthy living room toward the kitchen, pull a bottle of beer from the fridge, then open the bottle on my way to the bathroom to take my morning piss.

I toss the bottle in the trash can on my way out of the bathroom.

[[Continue.|Happy End 1]]<<set $CharacterConfirmed to "Not anymore">><<set $Day = 1>>

passages.name: Ruins15
passages.content:
I can smell rot before I even see the bridge, the smell so heavy in the air it makes me want to gag. 

When I step around a large tree growing out of the middle of the path, I can see the the path rise up like it is going over a hill.  A few steps closer and I realize that its not actually a hill, instead its a gently arched bridge.

The stone work is clearly impressive, even if centuries of exposure to the elements have dulled the craftsmanship.

I find myself moving just a little quicker as I near the bridge, looking it over and deciding it looks sound before I start to walk across it. 

About half way across, I stop to look around, first at my surroundings for any signs of danger, then to the bridge itself

The bridge is perhaps forty paces across with stone rails along the sides that only reach up just a little past my knees.  When I step toward the edge of the bridge, I look down into the channel the bridge stretches above, seeing old stagnant water that //must// account for the smell.

I'm almost ready to turn away when I see the water ripple.  My interest piqued, I look closer at the water, but my eyes are unable to penetrate the filthy black water to see whatever resides within.

After a couple of minutes, and no further sign of whatever is in the water, I finally step away from the edge and finish crossing the bridge.

[[Continue.|Continue into the ruins.]]
passages.content_zh:
還沒看見橋，我已聞到腐敗的氣味，空氣中濃重的臭味讓我幾欲作嘔。

當我繞過從路中央長出的一棵大樹時，看見小徑向上隆起，彷彿要越過一座山丘。再走近幾步，才意識到那並非山丘，而是一座弧度平緩的拱橋。

石造工藝顯然令人驚嘆，即使數世紀的風吹雨打已磨蝕了匠心的痕跡。

接近橋時，我發現自己腳步加快了些，仔細打量後判定橋體穩固，這才開始過橋。

走到約一半，我停步環顧四周——先觀察環境有無危險跡象，再審視橋身本身。

橋寬約四十步，兩側石製欄杆僅略高過我的膝蓋。當我走向橋緣向下望，看見橋身橫跨的溝渠裡積著陳年死水，那氣味想必源自於此。

我幾乎要轉身離開時，卻看見水面泛起漣漪。好奇心被勾起，我更仔細地察看水面，但渾濁的黑水遮蔽了視線，無法看清水底究竟藏著什麼。

幾分鐘過去，再無任何水中動靜，我終於從橋緣退開，走完剩下的橋程。

[繼續。]

passages.name: TomPark
passages.content:
Entering the small, overgrown park, I see someone standing on the other side of the park.

When the figure moves, I see a flicker of red hair under their wide brimmed hat.  //Tom?//

Before I consciously make a decision, I'm already in motion, my footsteps taking me rapidly across the small park.  As I'm passing the fountain, I see the figure begin to move.  For a split second, I get a better look at the figure.  The red hair is about shoulder length, and the person's features are much thinner and feminine than Tom's.

My pace slows as I continue across the park, the momentary excitement of finding Tom quickly replaced by disappointment as I realize the figure isn't him.

By the time I reach the place the figure had been standing, they are long gone.  I take a second to search my surroundings, glancing at the muddy holes that the figure's boots had made.

I start to turn back the way I came when I see part of a hand rolled cigarette in a clump of grass nearby.  Kneeling, I pick it up.  The smell of smoke is still strong from the cigarette, and unlike everything else around me, its still dry to the touch.

//So, our redhead is a smoker.//  I file the little bit of information away in my head in case it ever becomes useful.

Flicking the cigarette into the mud at my feet, I drift back into the park.

[[Return my attention to the park.|overgrown park]]
passages.content_zh:
踏入這座荒蕪蔓生的小公園，我看見對面站著一個人影。

當那人影移動時，寬簷帽下閃現一抹紅髮。是Tom嗎？

還未意識到自己做了決定，我已然動身，腳步急促地穿越小公園。經過噴泉時，我看見人影開始移動。那一瞬間，我將對方看得更清楚了些：紅髮長及肩部，臉部輪廓比Tom更纖細、更顯陰柔。

我放慢步伐繼續穿過公園，發現Tom的短暫興奮迅速被失望取代——我明白那人影不是他。

當我抵達那人影原先站立之處，對方早已離去。我花了一秒掃視周遭，瞥見泥地上留有對方靴子踩出的凹痕。

正當我準備原路折返時，卻看見附近草叢裡有半截手捲香菸。我蹲身拾起它。菸捲上仍散發濃烈煙味，且與周圍一切不同，觸感依然乾燥。

所以，那位紅髮者是位吸菸者。我將這點資訊記在腦中，以備不時之需。

將菸蒂彈進腳邊泥濘，我再度漫步入公園深處。

[將注意力放回公園。]

passages.name: DeepEnd2
passages.content:
As we swim, I can make out shapes in the gloom, like buildings, long lost to the sea. As things become clearer, I find the architecture to be strangely beautiful, like organic things as much grown as built to create a sprawling city at the bottom of the ocean. 

The scent of something in the water makes me pause, my head tilting towards the source.  Through the water, I can hear faint sounds from somewhere nearby.  My companion pauses, looking towards me.  Something in its expression implies amusement.

I decide to follow the scent, it doesn't take me long to find a gap in the rocks that leads to a series of chambers.  Within, I see others, similar to myself.  

The few males I see are powerfully built and in most cases, I can see massive erections jutting out in front of them as they fornicate with the considerably more numerous females. 

I feel a growing arousal within me, like a need with no outlet.  Its only at this moment that I come to a horrible realization.  Unlike the Deep Ones around me, I'm sexless.

One of the Deep Ones swims towards me, its a male.  It looks at me, and I can sense a hint of amusement coming from it at my predicament.  

[img[images/DeepEnd3.png]]

The despearate sound I make is faint in the water, but it seems to carry all around us.

//What kind of hell is this.//  I want to weep, but I am unable to do so.   

END
passages.content_zh:
當我們游動時，我能辨識出幽暗中的形體，像是早已沉沒海底的建築。隨著景象逐漸清晰，我發現這建築結構有種詭異的美感，彷彿有機物般既是生長而成也是建造而成，在海底形成一座蔓延的城市。

水中的某種氣味讓我停下動作，我將頭轉向氣味來源。透過水流，我能聽見附近某處傳來微弱的聲響。我的同伴也停了下來，望向我。牠的神情中透著一絲玩味。

我決定追隨那股氣味，沒多久便在岩石間找到一道裂隙，通往一連串的腔室。裡面有許多與我相似的生物。

我看到的少數雄性體格強壯，在大多數情況下，當牠們與數量更多的雌性交配時，我能看見巨大的勃起器官挺立在牠們身前。

我感到體內逐漸升起的慾望，像是一種無處宣洩的需求。直到此刻，我才驚恐地意識到一件事：與我周圍的深潛者不同，我沒有性別。

其中一隻深潛者朝我游來，是隻雄性。牠看著我，我能感覺到牠對我的困境流露出一絲嘲弄。

我在水中發出的絕望聲音很微弱，卻彷彿在我們周圍迴盪不散。

這到底是什麼地獄。我想哭泣，卻做不到。

END

passages.name: Question the woman.
passages.content:
<<if $Sherwood is "Not Played">>"I don't think we've been properly introduced,"  I begin, extending a hand in greeting, I'm <<print $PlayerName>>, <<print $PlayerName>> <<print $PlayerName2>>.

"Ms Sherwood,"  She replies hesitently, her eyes flickering to Aaron so quickly that I'm not sure I really saw it. Her hand shake is limp, like she's scared to touch my hand.

When I release her hand, it goes to her red skirt, smoothing it as she nervously fidgets.<<set $Sherwood to "Played">><<else>>"Mind if I ask a few more questions?"  I ask Ms Sherwood, hoping to coax a little more information out of her.

She opens her mouth as if she's going to say something, then she simply nods.  <</if>>

Ask about:
[[You have any idea who she was?|B1A]]
[[What are you doing out here on the beach today?|B1B]]
<<if $B1Limp is "Played">>
[[Anyone go missing who had a limp?|B1C]]
<</if>><<if $B1Goo is "Played">>
[[Any idea why there would be White Goo on the body?|B1D]]
<</if>><<if $B1Sex is "Played">>
[[Is there anyone in town who would sexually assault someone?|B1E]]
<</if>><<if $B1Shoulder is "Played">>
[[Can you think of anyone who might try hurting someone like that?|B1F]]
<</if>>
[[If I have any further questions, where can I find you?]]

or

[[I'm done talking to Ms Sherwood.|Investigate the pale shape amongst the rocks.]]<<set $Body1S to "Played">>
passages.content_zh:
[IF $Sherwood is "Not Played"]「我想我們還沒正式認識過，」我開口說道，伸手致意，「我是$PlayerName，$PlayerName $PlayerName2。」

「Sherwood女士，」她遲疑地回答，目光極快地瞥向Aaron，快得讓我懷疑是否真的看見了。她的握手軟弱無力，彷彿害怕觸碰我的手。

當我放開她的手時，她將手移到紅裙上，緊張地撫平裙襬。[ELSE]「介意我再問幾個問題嗎？」我詢問Sherwood女士，希望能從她口中套出更多訊息。

她張開嘴像是要說什麼，卻只是點了點頭。[/IF]

詢問：
[你知道她是誰嗎？]
[你今天為什麼來海灘？]
[IF $B1Limp is "Played"]
[有跛腳的人失蹤嗎？]
[/IF][IF $B1Goo is "Played"]
[知道屍體上為什麼有白色黏液嗎？]
[/IF][IF $B1Sex is "Played"]
[鎮上有誰會性侵他人嗎？]
[/IF][IF $B1Shoulder is "Played"]
[你能想到有誰可能那樣傷害人嗎？]
[/IF]
[如果我還有其他問題，去哪兒能找到你？]

或

[我問完Sherwood女士了。]

passages.name: Ruins18
passages.content:
A building, larger than most, looms before me.  I take a few hesitant steps toward it.  An oval portal acts as a door, whether that was its original purpose or not, and I move steadily toward it.

Stepping inside, my attention is drawn to the light emanating from within.

My eyes fall on a monolithic statue, perhaps twenty feet high and suspended from the ceiling to dangle several feet off the ground.

//It looks like the thing I encountered when I first arrived at the ruins, but somehow it feels much larger.//  My eyes drift over the smooth stone contours, taking in the long thin tentacles, the all too frequent eyes, and the countless mouths filled with all manner of teeth.  If that was all, then it would be an almost perfect match for the tentacled thing, but that isn't all.  Among the mass are orbs, each orb somehow part of the thing though I can not see how they are attached and glowing an eerie yellow green that makes my skin crawl.

I have no idea how long I stood there, staring at it, horrified and mesmerized at the same time.  

It's not until I hear a chittering babble from somewhere inside of the building that I return to my senses.

Not wanting to encounter the tentacled thing if I can help it, I quickly vacate the building.

Even as I move away, the terrible visage plagues my mind. 

[[Continue.|Continue into the ruins.]]
passages.content_zh:
一棟比多數建築更龐大的樓宇聳立在我面前。我遲疑地朝它走了幾步。一個橢圓形洞口充當門戶——無論那是否為它最初的用途——我穩步朝它走去。

踏入內部，我的注意力被裡面散發的光芒吸引。

我的目光落在一座巨大的雕像上，約二十英尺高，從天花板懸吊下來，離地數英尺。

它看起來像是我剛抵達遺址時遭遇的那東西，但感覺上龐大了許多。我的視線掃過光滑的石雕輪廓，細看那細長的觸手、過於密集的眼睛，以及無數長滿各式牙齒的嘴。如果僅止於此，它幾乎完全符合那觸手怪物的形象——但不止如此。在那團形體中嵌著許多球體，每個球體都以某種方式屬於這尊雕像（儘管我看不出連接處），並散發著令人皮膚發麻的詭異黃綠色幽光。

我不知道自己站在那兒瞪視了多久，既恐懼又著迷。

直到建築深處傳來一陣嘰喳的囈語聲，我才猛然回神。

既然能避免，我可不想再遭遇那觸手怪物，於是迅速撤離了這棟建築。

即使我已遠離，那可怕的形貌仍纏繞在我的腦海中。

[繼續。]

passages.name: Church
passages.content:
"What's the story on the Church?"  I ask.

He sighs heavily, stepping back from the counter, he crosses his arms over his chest, "Our Reverend committed suicide, oh, about four years ago now."

"We never got a replacement for him, and the Church has been empty ever since."

He leans forward again, uncrossing his arms and starting to wipe down the counter down again.

<<if ($PlayerIntelligence + $PlayerPerception) gte 13>>I get the impression that there's a lot more to the story than what he's saying, but I need to learn more before I can pry much deeper.

<</if>><<set $Pastor to "Played">>Ask about:
<<if $TomTanya is "Not Played">>[[I'm looking for a young man named Tom who might have passed through.]]
<</if>>[[Wanton Cove]]
<<if $FirstStatue is "Played">>[[Statues]]
<</if>><<if $Pastor is "Played">>[[Tell me more about the pastor.]]
<</if>><<if $MotherTalk is "Played">>[[Ask Aaron about the Morichi Family.]]
<</if>><<if $Fountain is "Played">>[[Engravings on the fountain.]]
<</if>><<if $SailorGoo is "Played">>[[White Goo.]]<</if>><<if $Body1 is "Played">>[[Body on the shore.]]
<</if>><<if $JackobsonBluff is 3>>[[Do you know where Mr Jackobson might have gotten off to?]]<</if>><<if $FountainInvestigated is "Played">>
[[Ask Aaron about the Ruins.]]<</if>>

[[Step away from the Bartender.|The Barmaids Daughter]]
passages.content_zh:
「教堂有什麼故事嗎？」我問道。

他重重嘆了口氣，從櫃檯後退開，雙臂交叉抱在胸前，「我們的牧師自殺了，噢，大概是四年前的事了。」

「我們一直沒找到人接替他的位置，教堂從那以後就一直空著。」

他又向前傾身，解開交叉的手臂，開始再次擦拭櫃檯。

[IF ($PlayerIntelligence + $PlayerPerception) gte 13]我感覺他說的只是故事的一小部分，但在深入追問之前，我需要了解更多資訊。

[/IF]詢問關於：
[IF $TomTanya is "Not Played"][我在找一個可能經過這裡的年輕人，名叫Tom。]
[/IF][萬頓灣]
[IF $FirstStatue is "Played"][雕像]
[/IF][IF $Pastor is "Played"][告訴我更多關於牧師的事。]
[/IF][IF $MotherTalk is "Played"][向Aaron詢問Morichi家族的事。]
[/IF][IF $Fountain is "Played"][噴泉上的刻文。]
[/IF][IF $SailorGoo is "Played"][白色黏液。][/IF][IF $Body1 is "Played"][岸邊的屍體。]
[/IF][IF $JackobsonBluff is 3][你知道Jackobson先生可能去哪了嗎？][/IF][IF $FountainInvestigated is "Played"]
[向Aaron詢問遺跡的事。][/IF]

[離開酒保身邊。]

passages.name: Rape Search
passages.content:
Turning my eyes to the place I first saw //her// along the waterfront, I start to make my way that direction on unsteady legs.

After a couple of minutes, I reach the rock outcropping.  My attention goes to the water, searching for any sign of the woman I saw being raped just a few short minutes earlier.  Unfortunately, there is no sign of her among the large waves breaking against the shore.

Sighing heavily, and feeling hopeless about the situation, I decide I had better investigate the scene properly anyway.  

It only takes a few moments to realize that the rock the deed took place upon is not what I first thought.  Its smooth, too smooth, along one side, with a sharp ninety degree angle leading to another perfectly smooth side.  Only the broken edge I had first laid eyes on appears to be natural.  

Reaching out, I let my fingers brush across the old stone, tracing around clusters of barnacles as I look at the stone.  I begin to become aware of engravings along the stone, much like other similar engravings I have seen previously around the island.

//They have to mean something,// I think to myself, //But what?//

Lacking anything else I can do, I turn back toward the town, looking at Phil's shack nearby.  I start to walk in that general direction.

[[Continue.|Go check out the shipbuilder's shack.]]
passages.content_zh:
我將目光轉向最初在水邊看見她的位置，雙腿不穩地朝那個方向走去。

幾分鐘後，我抵達了那處突出的岩石。我的注意力投向水面，搜尋著短短幾分鐘前目睹被強暴的那名女子的任何蹤跡。可惜，在拍擊岸邊的巨浪中，完全沒有她的身影。

我沉重地嘆了口氣，對這情況感到絕望，但決定無論如何還是該好好調查現場。

沒過多久，我就意識到那樁罪行發生的岩石並非我最初所想的那樣。它的一面異常光滑，過於光滑了，形成一個尖銳的九十度角，連接到另一面同樣完美平滑的表面。只有我最初瞥見的那斷裂邊緣看起來是天然的。

我伸出手，讓指尖拂過古老的石面，繞著一簇簇藤壺移動，同時仔細端詳石頭。我開始注意到石頭上的刻文，與我在島上其他地方見過的類似刻文十分相像。

這些肯定有什麼含義，我心想，但究竟是什麼呢？

在別無他法的情況下，我轉身朝城鎮方向望去，看著附近Phil的小屋。我開始朝那個大致方向走去。

[繼續。]

passages.name: Talk with him for a few minutes.
passages.content:
"Why would I want to do that?"  I ask softly, watchful for any open threat from Cobb.

He must have noticed me tense up, because the next thing he tires to do is put me at ease again, "Don't mean it as a threat."  He says defensively, "But others in these parts might."  

He makes a show of looking around, as if watching for any potential voyeurs.

"Thanks for the warning,"  I say half heartedly, my own eyes scanning my surroundings and seeing nothing of concern.

"I'm serious,"  He says, looking me straight in the eye.

Without another word, he pushes off of the wall, turns on his heel, and walks away from me.
<<if $SailorGoo1 is "Played">>
I stand where I was until Cobb is out of sight.  Once I'm convinced that he's gone, I kneel down for a better look at whatever he felt the need to hide from me.

On the ground is a small puddle of some kind of White Goo, Cobb's boot-print still partially visible in it.  It takes me a moment to realize that the White Goo is putting off a faint glow.

Inspecting further, it's easy to see that the White Goo appears thick, but fluid.

Sighing, I look in the direction that Cobb went one last time, then turn my attention towards the main street through town.
<<set $SailorGoo to "Played">><<endif>>
Stepping away from the building, I [[Head into the center of town.]]
passages.content_zh:
「我為什麼要那樣做？」我輕聲問道，同時警惕著Cobb任何明顯的威脅。

他一定注意到我繃緊了身子，因為他接下來試圖再次讓我放鬆下來，「這不是威脅。」他防衛性地說，「但這地方其他人可能會。」

他刻意環顧四周，彷彿在提防任何可能的窺視者。

「謝謝你的警告。」我半心半意地說，自己的眼睛也掃視著周圍，沒發現什麼值得擔心的。

「我是認真的。」他說，直視著我的眼睛。

他沒再多說一個字，從牆邊撐起身子，腳跟一轉，便從我面前走開了。
[IF $SailorGoo1 is "Played"]
我站在原地，直到Cobb消失在視線外。一旦確信他已經離開，我跪下來，仔細查看他覺得有必要對我隱藏的東西。

地上有一小灘白色黏液，Cobb的靴印還部分殘留在上面。過了一會兒，我才意識到那白色黏液正散發著微光。

進一步觀察，很容易看出這白色黏液看起來濃稠，卻又具有流動性。

我嘆了口氣，最後一次望向Cobb離開的方向，然後將注意力轉向穿過城鎮的主要街道。
[/IF]
我從建築物旁走開，[往城鎮中心走去。]

passages.name: Nadia
passages.content:
<<if $Nadia is "Not Played">>"Hi, I'm <<print $PlayerName>> <<print $PlayerName2>>."  I offer my hand in greeting.

Her smile turns to a toothy grin as she extends her own hand to shake mine, "You can call me Nadia."  She says pleasantly.

I can't help but notice the coarse, almost scaly texture of her palm.

"it's a pleasure to meet you."  I reply. <<set $Nadia to "Played">><<else>>"<<print $PlayerName>>, what can I get you today?" Nadia purrs. <</if>>

"Mind if I ask you a few questions?"  I ask.

"Not at all,"  Nadia starts to twirl a long strand of hair around her finger, biting her bottom lip in a way that I find terribly distracting.

<<if $Taint gte 40>>//Maybe she knows something about what could be doing this to me,//  I think, looking down at body, //She had to have noticed I look a little different by now.//

[[Ask Nadia about your transformation.]]

<</if>>[[Visit with Nadia for a minute.]]

<<if $TomTanya is "Not Played">>[[Ask about Tom.]]<</if>><<if $Jackobson is "Played">>

[[Ask about Pastor Erkon]]<</if>><<if $JackobsonHouse is 3>>

[[You dont know where Mr Jackobson got off to, do you?]]<</if>><<if $FountainInvestigated is "Played">>

[[Ask Nadia about the Ruins.]]<</if>>

[[Buy something.|See her wares.]]

or 

[[Return to looking around the store.|General Store]]
passages.content_zh:
[IF $Nadia is "Not Played"]「嗨，我是 $PlayerName $PlayerName2。」我伸出手打招呼。

她露出燦爛的笑容，也伸出手與我相握，「你可以叫我 Nadia。」她愉快地說。

我忍不住注意到她手掌粗糙、幾乎像鱗片般的質感。

「很高興認識你。」我回答。[ELSE]「$PlayerName，今天想來點什麼？」Nadia 低聲輕語。[/IF]

「介意我問幾個問題嗎？」我問道。

「一點也不介意，」Nadia 開始用手指繞著一縷長髮打轉，並咬著下唇，那模樣讓我感到極度分心。

[IF $Taint gte 40]也許她知道是什麼東西讓我變成這樣，我低頭看著身體想著。她現在一定注意到我看起來有點不一樣了。

[向 Nadia 詢問你的轉變。]

[/IF][和 Nadia 聊一會兒。]

[IF $TomTanya is "Not Played"][詢問關於 Tom 的事。][/IF][IF $Jackobson is "Played"]

[詢問關於 Erkon 牧師的事。][/IF][IF $JackobsonHouse is 3]

[你不知道 Jackobson 先生去哪了，對吧？][/IF][IF $FountainInvestigated is "Played"]

[買點東西。]

或

[返回店內逛逛。]

passages.name: Night8
passages.content:
<<set $Bed to "False">><<set $Day += 1>><<set $CharHP = 10>><<set $Research to 0>><<if $Taint gte 100>><<set $Taint += 5>><<elseif $Taint gte 75>><<set $Taint += 4>><<elseif $Taint gte 50>><<set $Taint += 3>><<elseif $Taint gte 25>><<set $Taint += 2>>
<<elseif $Taint gte 1>><<set $Taint += 1>><</if>>Laying in bed, I close my eyes. 

//If this storm would just let up.  I could take Tom, //Tanya//, and get away from this place.//  Even as I think it, I doubt my resolve, //I still need to figure out how this all fits together.  How //exactly// that murder is tied to Wanton Cove.// I argue to myself.

Seemingly in response to my thoughts about the storm, the building rattles as the wind buffets it.  I can hear the wind whistling around the window in my room.

I open my eyes just a little bit, staring at the blackness outside the window, I watch at the roiling black clouds are illuminated in a series of flashes from within.   I can almost fool myself into thinking I see something massive, something alive, moving in the distance.  Its hulking form casting an inhuman shadow across the sky.

Closing my eyes tightly, a dank chill seeps into my bones as I pull the covers tightly around myself as if that will somehow ward me against thatever lurks just beyond my sight.

[[Fall into a fitful sleep.|Dream8]]
passages.content_zh:
[IF $Taint gte 100][ELSEIF $Taint gte 75][ELSEIF $Taint gte 50][ELSEIF $Taint gte 25]
[ELSEIF $Taint gte 1][/IF]躺在床上，我閉上眼睛。

要是這場暴風雨能停就好了。我就能帶著 Tom 和 Tanya 離開這個地方。即使這麼想著，我也懷疑自己的決心，我仍然需要弄清楚這一切是如何串聯起來的。那樁謀殺案究竟如何與萬頓灣扯上關係。我內心爭辯著。

彷彿是回應我對暴風雨的思緒，建築物在狂風吹襲下嘎吱作響。我能聽見風在我房間窗戶周圍呼嘯的聲音。

我微微睜開眼睛，凝視著窗外的黑暗，看著翻滾的烏雲內部被一連串閃電照亮。我幾乎能騙自己以為看到了某種龐然大物，某種活物，在遠處移動。它巨大的形體在天空投下非人的陰影。

緊緊閉上眼睛，一股陰濕的寒意滲入骨髓，我將被子緊緊裹在身上，彷彿這樣就能抵禦潛伏在我視線之外的那個東西。

[陷入斷斷續續的睡眠。]

passages.name: Slum2
passages.content:
I can see a luminescent white smear along the side of a nearby house.  My footsteps draw me towards it, almost of their own accord.  When I near the smear, I frown at what I see.

<<if $SailorGoo is "Played">>While the White Goo is familiar to me, this time it looks as if someone smeared a liberal amount across the side of the house.  The end of the smear even ends in a glistening white handprint.

Breathing deeply of the humid air, the heady aroma of the goo makes me feel light headed as I flush with arousal.  
[[Leave.|Run down part of town.]]
Or
[[Investigate Closer.|GooWall]]
<<else>>Some kind of milky white substance, thick enough to be called slime, clings to the wall in a long smear.  At the end of the smear is a near perfect handprint in the White Goo.

I lean close, the substance putting off a musky, salty smell that makes my head swim.  I can feel myself flush with arousal as I quickly step away from the wall.

For several seconds, I continue to look at the stuff, going so far as to pick up a small stock and poke at it.  It seems to jiggle at the stick’s touch, and clings to it when I pull the stick away.

//Strange,//  I think, dropping the stick back to the ground.

[[Continue on my way.|Run down part of town.]]<<set $SailorGoo to "Played">><<endif>>
passages.content_zh:
我看到附近一棟房子的側面有一道發光的白色污痕。我的腳步幾乎不由自主地將我引向它。當我靠近污痕時，眼前的景象讓我皺起眉頭。

[IF $SailorGoo is "Played"]雖然這白色黏液對我來說很熟悉，但這次看起來像是有人將大量黏液塗抹在房子的側面。污痕的末端甚至留下了一個閃閃發光的白色手印。

深深吸了一口潮濕的空氣，黏液中濃烈的氣味讓我感到頭暈目眩，同時一股興奮感湧上全身。
[離開。]
或
[更仔細調查。]
[ELSE]某種乳白色的物質，濃稠到足以被稱為黏液，以一道長長的污痕附著在牆上。污痕的末端是一個近乎完美的白色黏液手印。

我湊近一看，這種物質散發出一種麝香、鹹腥的氣味，讓我頭暈目眩。我能感覺到自己因興奮而臉紅，趕緊從牆邊退開。

有好幾秒鐘，我繼續盯著這東西看，甚至撿起一根小樹枝戳了戳它。它在樹枝觸碰時微微晃動，當我把樹枝抽開時，黏液還黏在上面。

真奇怪，我心想，把樹枝扔回地上。

[繼續前進。][/IF]

passages.name: commonDocksEvents
Lebron Home.
passages.content:
Knocking on the door, two siren voices coo, "Please, come in."  I have no way of knowing if one of them spoke, or of they broke their sentence.  

I hesitate for only a second before opening the door and entering.

Inside of the Lebron home, I pry my eyes away from the two attractive young ladies long enough to take in the rest of the room.

What would be a living room in any other home looks like a bedroom in this house. A single large bed sits a short distance from a wood fireplace.  The soft crackle of wood burning can be heard.  The bed is covered with a tangle of blankets along with a couple of extra pillows.

On the ground around the room are several pieces of discarded clothing, not all of which appear to have come from a woman.

The twins grin in unison, "It's good,"  One begins, "To see you again," The second finishes.

Something becomes predatory about the two teens. Their movements reminding me of a pair of wolves preparing to pounce on a trapped animal.

I find myself rooted to the spot, the two girls disrobing in a clearly practiced dance.  

Once naked, they move toward me, "You're going to play,"  The first one says, her expression seductive, "with us, right?"

[[Make an exit while I still can.|Lebron Exit.]]

[[Stay.|Lebron Visit.]]
passages.content_zh:
敲了敲門，兩個如海妖般的聲音輕柔地說：「請進。」我無法分辨是她們其中一人開口，還是兩人分著說完這句話。

我只猶豫了一秒，便開門進入。

在 Lebron 的家裡，我勉強將目光從兩位迷人的年輕女子身上移開，好打量房間的其他部分。

在其他人家裡應該是客廳的地方，在這房子裡看起來卻像臥室。一張大床離木製壁爐不遠，可以聽到木材燃燒的輕微噼啪聲。床上堆滿了糾纏的毯子和幾個額外的枕頭。

房間周圍的地板上散落著幾件丟棄的衣物，其中並非所有都像是女性衣物。

雙胞胎同步露出笑容，「很高興，」一個開口，「再次見到你，」第二個接完。

這對青少女身上散發出某種掠食者的氣息。她們的動作讓我想起一對準備撲向被困獵物的狼。

我發現自己僵在原地，兩個女孩以一種顯然經過練習的舞姿褪去衣物。

一絲不掛後，她們朝我走來，「你會和我們，」第一個女孩說，表情充滿誘惑，「一起玩，對吧？」

[趁我還可以的時候離開。]

[留下。]

passages.name: Ask Phil about the Pastor.
passages.content:
"Did you happen to know Pastor Erkon?"  I ask, watching his reaction.

Phil lets out a little sigh, "Not well, and the few times I met him, we really didn't get along well."

Phil smirks and lets out a bitter little laugh, "He was always going on about 'Save your soul!' and all I ever wanted was my next beer, and a wench I could shove my cock into."  

His eyes settle on my own, "Its a shame that he killed himself, but I can't really say that I miss him."<<if $Madness gte 2>>

I can't help but consider for a few seconds, whether or not Phil could potentially have a motive to lie to me, but the thought is slippery, like a bar of soap in the shower, not quite forming into something coherent.<<endif>>

Ask about:
<<if $TomTanya is "Not Played">>[[Ask Phil about Tom.]]
<</if>>[[Ask Phil about Wanton Cove.]]<<if $SailorGoo is "Played">>
[[Ask Phil about the White Goo.]]<</if>><<if $Taint gte 40>>
[[Ask Phil about your physical changes.|Ask Phil about your transformation.]]<<endif>><<if $DOneSighting is "Played">>
[[Ask Phil about the creatures you've seen in the water.]]<<endif>>
<<if $FountainInvestigated is "Played">>[[Ask Phil about the Ruins.]]

<</if>>
or
[[That is all for now.  Exit the Shipbuilder's Shack.|Go check out the shipbuilder's shack.]]
passages.content_zh:
「你認識Erkon牧師嗎？」我問道，同時觀察他的反應。

Phil輕嘆一口氣：「不算熟，而且僅有的幾次見面，我們都處得不太愉快。」

Phil露出苦笑，帶著一絲譏諷：「他總是在那邊嚷嚷『拯救你的靈魂！』，而我想要的永遠只是下一杯啤酒，還有個能讓我插進去的娘們。」

他的目光定定看著我：「他自殺是件憾事，但我實在說不上想念他。」[IF $Madness gte 2]

我忍不住思索了幾秒——Phil是否有對我說謊的動機？但這念頭滑溜得像淋浴間的肥皂，始終無法凝聚成清晰的思緒。[/IF]

詢問：
[IF $TomTanya is "Not Played"][向Phil詢問Tom的事。]
[/IF][向Phil詢問萬頓灣的事。][IF $SailorGoo is "Played"]
[向Phil詢問白色黏液的事。][/IF][IF $Taint gte 40]
[向Phil詢問你身體的變化。][/IF][IF $DOneSighting is "Played"]
[向Phil詢問你在水中見過的生物。][/IF]
[IF $FountainInvestigated is "Played"][向Phil詢問遺跡的事。]

[/IF]
或
[目前就這樣吧。離開造船工的小屋。]

passages.name: Try to persuade him to talk to you.
passages.content:
"And why do you say that, friend?"  I ask pleasantly.

<<if ($PlayerStreetwise + $PlayerCharisma) gte 10>>"Just leave,"  His eyes meet mine, "While you still can."  He says ominously.

Not bothering to wait until I reply, Cobb pushes off the wall and starts to walk away without another word.

<<else>>Cobb shakes his head, almost sadly before pushing off of the wall behind him and starting to walk away.

<<endif>>I start to open my mouth to call after him, but decide against it.  //Plenty of time to find him and continue the conversation later.  Besides, maybe someone else will be more forthcoming.//

<<if $SailorGoo1 is "Played">>
I stand where I was until Cobb is out of sight.  Once I'm convinced that he's gone, I kneel down for a better look at whatever he felt the need to hide from me.

On the ground is a small puddle of some kind of White Goo, Cobb's boot-print still partially visible in it.  As I look, I can see that the White Goo is putting off a faint glow.

Inspecting further, it's easy to see that the White Goo appears thick, but fluid.

Sighing, I look in the direction that Cobb went one last time, then turn my attention towards the main street through town.<<set $SailorGoo to "Played">><<endif>>

[[Head into the center of town.]]
passages.content_zh:
「朋友，為什麼這麼說呢？」我友善地問道。

[IF ($PlayerStreetwise + $PlayerCharisma) gte 10]「快離開吧，」他的目光與我相接，「趁你還能夠的時候。」他不祥地說道。

不等我回應，Cobb便從牆邊起身，一言不發地邁步離開。

[ELSE]Cobb近乎悲傷地搖搖頭，從倚靠的牆邊起身，開始走遠。

[/IF]我張口想叫住他，但最終作罷。反正還有時間，之後再找他繼續談話。再說，或許其他人會更願意開口。

[IF $SailorGoo1 is "Played"]
我站在原地，直到Cobb消失在視線外。確定他離開後，我蹲下身仔細查看他剛才急著隱藏的東西。

地上有一小灘白色黏液，還留著半個Cobb的靴印。仔細看，能發現黏液正散發微弱的螢光。

再靠近觀察，這白色黏液看似濃稠，卻又具有流動性。

我嘆了口氣，最後望了一眼Cobb離去的方向，轉身朝鎮上的主街走去。[/IF]

[前往鎮中心。]

passages.name: Anne Husband Reject
passages.content:
I sputter, trying to force words past my lips.  The implications of what she is offering sinking in with an unsettling certainty. "No,"  I croak out, my own voice sounding small.

When her expression turns mildly dissapproving, I hastily add before I fully register what I am doing, "No Ma'am."

Her husband has a strange look on his face, almost like he's both thankful for my rejection and thankful for it at the same time.

What I said must have been the right thing, there's a slight upward twinge at the corner of her mouth that I'd call the start of a smile on almost anyone else.

Unsteadily, I start to get to my feet, Lady Anne's eyes boring into my soul like she's looking at some kind of curiosity.  My eyes dart to the door, fighting the urge to make my way that direction.

"I'll be looking forward to our next visit,"  She purrs.

Like I was suddenly released by some invisible chain, I take an unsteady step towards the door.  Each consecutive step being a little more certain than the last.

Stopping at the door, I turn back towards Lady Anna, "Goodbye,"  I manage around my suddenly dry mouth.

The slight rise at the corner of her mouth wanders towards becoming a smile before I step out of her home.

[[Make your exit.|Nice part of town.]]
passages.content_zh:
我結結巴巴地想擠出話語，她提議背後的含意如寒冰般滲入骨髓。「不行，」我嘶啞地吐出字句，聲音聽起來微弱不堪。

見她眉頭微蹙露出不贊同的神色，我未經思考便慌忙補充：「夫人，真的不行。」

她丈夫臉上閃過複雜的神情——彷彿既感激我的拒絕，又為此感到慶幸。

我似乎說對話了，因為她嘴角微微上揚，那弧度換作任何人都是在微笑。

我搖搖晃晃地起身，Anne女士的目光如錐子般刺穿我的靈魂，彷彿在審視某種奇異標本。我瞥向門的方向，強壓下奪門而逃的衝動。

「期待下次見面呢，」她低聲輕語。

像突然掙脫無形鎖鏈般，我踉蹌地朝門口踏出一步，隨後每一步都逐漸穩健起來。

在門邊停步，我轉身面向Anna女士：「再會。」乾澀的喉嚨勉強擠出道別。

在我踏出她家門前，她嘴角那抹微揚的弧度終於綻成完整的微笑。

[離開此地。]

passages.name: Ruins5
passages.content:
Listening, I can hear an unsettling babble, in my mind I can clearly remember the tentacled thing, the image of it burned in my mind since the first time I laid eyes on it.  

I try to determine where the sound is coming from or where it might be going.  

While still very distant, I can hear the gibbering become more excited, rapidly reaching a fevered pitch.  A few seconds later I hear another sound.  

I've heard men die, I've heard their screams and wails as they die a slow death from violence.  I listened to one man scream like a banshee after being knifed in the gut, trying to hold his insides in with his bare hands as they spilled out around his fingers.  I remember how his intestines felt like rubber sausages covered in molasses as I attempted to help him.  He died before anyone who could help arrived.  //I couldn't eat sausage for months after that.// 

This new sound reminds me of that.  I shudder, feeling sick to my stomach as I try not to imagine what the tentacled thing might be doing to whatever it just caught.

It's several minutes before the noise ends, leaving only the cackling cacophony of voices babbling over one another as the tentacled thing continues on toward wherever it was going.

[[Continue.|Continue into the ruins.]]
passages.content_zh:
側耳傾聽，我聽見令人不安的囈語聲。腦海中清晰浮現那隻觸手生物的模樣——自從初次目睹後，那影像便烙印在記憶深處。

我試圖辨別聲音的來源與去向。

雖然距離仍遠，但那陣胡言亂語逐漸亢奮，迅速攀升至狂熱的頂點。幾秒後，另一種聲音響起。

我曾聽過人們死亡的聲音，聽過他們在暴力中緩慢斷氣時的尖叫與哀嚎。我聽過一個被捅穿肚腹的男人像女妖般慘嚎，徒手想塞回從指間溢出的內臟。我還記得試圖幫他時，那些腸子摸起來像裹了糖漿的橡膠香腸。他在救援趕到前便斷了氣，那之後我好幾個月無法直視香腸。

此刻的新聲響讓我想起那段記憶。我渾身顫慄，胃部翻攪，竭力不去想像觸手生物正如何對待牠剛捕獲的獵物。

數分鐘後，噪音終於停歇，只剩層疊交錯的喋喋怪笑聲，伴隨著觸手生物繼續朝某個目的地前行。

[繼續。]

passages.name: What about the man with the limp you mentioned before?
passages.content:
"You mentioned Wilson Crow when we talked out by the docks,"  I begin, "Can you tell me a little more about him?"

"He's a good man, a fisherman,"  She says, sounding like she's trying to convince herself, "He messed his ankle up good in a fishing accident when he was hardly a man.  Walked with a limp ever since.  He lives out by the old Church with his wife." 

"Think he's out in one of the fishing boats right now?"  I ask. <<if $Madness gte 1>>

//There are terrible things lurking in the ocean.//  The thought makes me shudder at the realization that eventually I will need to return to the mainland.<<endif>>

"It's possible,"  Ms Sherwood shrugs applologetically, "Maybe his wife, Rose, can tell you more?"

[[Did you think of anything you could add about the waterfront?]]

[[You said your brother was leaving on the fishing boat?]]

[[Tell me a little about yourself?]]

[[You seemed a little intimidated by Aaron?]]<<if $PastorErkon is "Played">>

[[Ask about Pastor Erkon|Sherwood Pastor Talk]]<</if>><<if $PastorBook is "Taken">>

[[Ask about the book from the Church basement.]]<</if>>

<<if $MotherTalk is "Played">>[[Ask Ms Sherwood what she knows about Azumi.]]

<</if>>or

passages.name: Ask Lady Anne about the Pastor.
passages.content:
//You never know until you ask,// I reason before opening my mouth, "I was wondering if you might have known the pastor, Pastor Erkon?"

Lady Anne's eyebrows raise slightly, "Distasteful little fellow,"  She says with a hint of annoyance, "Wrapped in his religion to tightly that he couldn't see the world for what it was until it swallowed him whole,"  Her eyes bore into my own, "Much like yourself though, he //had// his uses."

//Well, that just created more questions than it answered,"  I think darkly.  For several seconds, I struggle to come up with a way to continue the conversation, but the hard look in her eyes quickly changes my mind.

//Maybe another time I'll find an opening and be able to learn a bit more, but I'm not too proud to admit to myself that the woman frightens me, and I fear I may have already pushed her further on this than is safe to do so.


[[Ask Lady Anne about Wanton Cove.]]

[[Ask if she is Azumi's mother.]]<</if>>

passages.content:
"Sure thing,"  Phil says as he leans back against a piece of machinery, "I tend to stick to myself, but if I can answer your questions, I'll be happy to do so,"  <<if $Madness gte 2>>//Will you really?//  <<endif>>Waiting for your questions, he crosses his burly arms over his bare chest, seemingly oblivious to the sweat and sawdust that combine to make his harry chest a tangled mess.  <<if $Mutation gte 4>>I can see his eyes drifting over me, seeming to catalog my changes, though he does not say anything about them.  I'm unsure if his lack of comment is because he is trying to be polite, or if he simply doesn't want to talk about it though.<</if>>

passages.name: The Geography of Wanton Cove
passages.content:
[[return|$return]]

Wanton Cove is an island a little more than a mile across at its narrowest point, and nearly two miles across at its widest.

Much of the island is thickly forested, with many caves known, and yet to be discovered, on the island.  Near one of the largest of these cave system, known by the locals as 'the cathedral' is the remnants of an old city, one that appears to predate Columbus, though no official archaeological expedition has ever been mounted to learn more.  The island is littered with bits of old ruins, the best known of these is the large fountain in the town's park.

The southern portion of the island is dominated by a large cove.  The cove itself is deep and wide, allowing for fishing boats of substantial size to moor there.  As a natural port, the town of Wanton Cove grew in the northern most part of the cove.

Roughly a half mile from the cove is a large bluff that stretches along a large portion of the island's eastern edge.  The southern most point of the bluff rises up in a way that allows those standing on it to see all of the town and cove below.  The bluff falls off into rocky waters below.

[[Return to Table of Contents.|Wanton Cove History Book]]

[[return|$return]]<<set $Geography to "Played">>

passages.name: TwoLadies
passages.content:
Ahead of me, I see two ladies walking down the muddy street, the hems of their patchwork dresses cobbled together from ragged scraps of cloth and heavily stained with mud.

Heads together, the pair talks in hushed tones as they walk towards me.  When one of them sees me, she falls silent.  A moment later, the other lady looks to see what has distracted her companion.

By this time, the woman who spotted you first is biting her lip, her eyes seeming to drift towards my crotch.  One of her hands rises to her chest, resting lightly against her breast, her other hand begins to drift towards her crotch.  The lust is unmistakable in her eyes.

My pace slows slightly, uncertain.  

The second woman grabs the other's shoulder, pulling her in her wake as she starts briskly towards a nearby shack.  Her companions eyes are locked on my own as she stumbles along in her wake.

Once the visibly aroused lady is inside, the other woman looks my way one last time, an odd mix of sympathy and arousal in her eyes.

I take a few steps towards the shack the two ladies disappeared into when I hear the clear sound of a heavy lock sliding into place from the door.

Realizing that the pair is unlikely to talk, [[I continue on my way.|Run down part of town.]]
passages.content_zh:
在我前方，我看見兩位女士走在泥濘的街道上，她們拼布連衣裙的下襬由破布碎料粗糙縫製而成，且沾滿了泥污。

兩人頭靠著頭，一邊朝我走來一邊低聲交談。當其中一人看見我時，她突然沉默下來。片刻後，另一位女士也轉頭看向令同伴分心的事物。

此時，最先注意到你的那位女士正咬著嘴唇，她的目光似乎飄向我的胯下。她一隻手抬到胸前，輕輕按著自己的乳房，另一隻手則開始往自己的胯部移動。她眼中的慾望再明顯不過。

我的步伐略顯遲疑地放慢了。

passages.name: Approach the back door.
passages.content:
I glance nervously from side to side, making sure I am not being observed before stepping up to the mansion's back door and producing the old key from my jacket pocket.  

The key slips inside with barely a sound, but when I turn it I can hear a soft squeak followed by a dull thud from inside of the door itself.  Trying the handle, the door swings open easily.

I poke my head through the door to look around, and seeing nothing but a shadowy hallway with doors along it's length, I close the door behind myself and start to walk.

Every step along the way, I worry that I am going to be seen by someone, that someone will call out and alert the household to my presence.  

Half way down the hall, I step into a small alcove that leads to a servant's stairwell.  I take it up to the next floor, then glance both ways to ensure the hallway is clear before moving to Azumi's door.  

Reaching out, I try the handle and find it locked.  My hand slips into my pocket, grabbing the key within and sliding that into the lock.  The key turns easily and a soft click tells me that the door is unlocked.

Turning the knob, I glance both directions down the hall to ensure I am alone again before opening the door and stepping inside.

[[Enter.|AzumiRoom2]]
passages.content_zh:
我緊張地左右張望，確認沒人看見後，才走向宅邸的後門，從外套口袋掏出那把舊鑰匙。

鑰匙幾乎無聲地滑入鎖孔，但當我轉動它時，能聽到一聲輕微的吱嘎，接著是門內傳來悶沉的咔噠聲。試著轉動門把，門輕易地向外敞開。

我將頭探進門內環顧四周，只見一條陰暗的走廊，沿牆有數扇門。我關上身後的門，開始往前走。

每一步都讓我提心吊膽，擔心被人看見，擔心有人出聲驚動宅子裡的人。

走到走廊中途，我拐進一個通往僕役樓梯間的小凹室。我沿樓梯走上下一層樓，再次左右察看，確保走廊空無一人，才走向 Azumi 的房門。

伸手試了試門把，發現鎖著。我將手滑入口袋，抓住裡面的鑰匙，插入鎖孔。鑰匙輕易轉動，一聲輕微的咔嗒聲告訴我門鎖已開。

轉動門把，我又朝走廊兩端瞥了一眼，確認仍獨自一人，這才開門踏入。

[進入。]

passages.name: Seek out the source of the sound.
passages.content:
Inevitably, my footsteps lead me into the dense underbrush toward the sounds.

Then one step to the next, the sound abruptly stops.  I pause, my eyes drifting over my surroundings.  

The foliage seems darker here, thick vines hanging everywhere, //Why didn't I notice those before?//  Several large flowers are in full bloom, opened and glistening in a dark shade of red.  The patterns and shapes of the flowers remind me of a woman's vagina, spread and inviting.

Almost in perfect unison, the flowers quiver, the same moaning sound from before filling the air as they release some form of pink spore into the air.  I'm standing far too close to even consider trying to dodge.

<<if $glade is "true">>//This again,//  I think with a chill even though my body reacts with arousal.  I gasp, taking in more of the spores in the process of doing so.  The arousal that courses through my body is nearly enough to bring me to my knees

<<else>>//What the hell is this?//  I wonder, my body reacting to the spores with a surge of arousal that nearly drops me to my knees.

<<set $glade to "true">><</if>>All around me I can sense movement, but I'm too far gone already to put up more than a token resistance.  

[[Continue.|TentacleFlowers Rape]]

passages.name: CaveEndgame
passages.content:
I as much sense as see the void in the forest ahead of me.  The energy in the air making the hairs on the back of my neck stand on end.

//Something is //very// wrong here.//

From behind me, Vick begins to speak, his words causing the blood in my veins to chill like ice water, "The others, they thought it would be difficult to bring you here, to get you to play your part,"  He says, the sound of him chambering a round in his rifle roots me to the spot.  I slowly start to raise my hands, still holding the shotgun in one hand.  I'm uncertain if I should turn and try using it as a club, or if dropping it is the better choice, "But I knew better.  I //knew// you would be so busy trying to play the hero, trying to make sense of this fucked up little town, that you'd never see the truth until it bit you in the ass,"  He lets out a slow chuckle, "You played the part so well, its almost like you wanted this,"  There's a crunch of gravel as he moves behind me.  I brace myself in case he's preparing to shoot me in the back, but it never happens,  "Now, you're going to drop that shotgun then march down into the Cathedral until you come out the other side.  //She'll// be waiting for you."

[[Do as Vick says.]]
[[Turn the tables on Vick.]]

passages.name: Ruins14
passages.content:
<<if $Day gte 5>>The flash of lightning is muffled by the thick canopy overhead, but the rumble of thunder is so close on its heels that it makes me flinch.

My eyes flicker to the couple of buildings I can see from where I'm standing and wonder if I should consider taking shelter.

After a few seconds of consideration, I decide that with so many tall trees and tall buildings around me, odds are that those will act as lightning rods and that I'm probably just as safe walking these ancient streets as I would be huddled inside of one of the strange buildings in the area.

As I start to walk again, I hear the rumble of thunder from somewhere further in the distance.

<<else>>Looking at the strangeness of this place around me, I loose track of time.  Its not until I hear a chittering, babbling sound somewhere off to my side that I snap out of it and focus on where I am and why.  

Suddenly uneasy, I move to put my back to a nearby building as I listen.  

The //thing// must not be looking for me specifically though because I can hear the sound drifting into the distance.

Once I feel sure its passed, I hasten my trek in the opposite direction I had heard the sound going.

<</if>>[[Continue.|Continue into the ruins.]]

passages.name: Ruins12
passages.content:
Seeing a gaping broken hole in the side of one of the buildings, I move closer.  My eyes trail across the shiny surface of the building, noticing the way the rain water runs down the building's side.  

When I reach the opening, I lightly kick at a chunk of wall, noting how light it feels when it moves.  Turning my eyes to the hole, I look at the edge.  In spite of the jagged edges, I can see that the interior of the material looks just like the outside.

Curiously I reach up to touch the edges of the break, realizing that even though it looks the same that the texture because of being broken is different than the smooth surface on the outside.

My eyes shift to the dark interior of the building as I step inside.  The space appears to be a single room, I can see indentions in the walls that might have once served as storage spaces, or could have been for something completely different for all I know.  To one side I can see a sunken area filled with leaves and other forest refuse.  To the other I can see the oval opening that most likely served as a doorway to whoever or whatever once lived here.

Not seeing anything else of interest within the space, I exit the way I came in.

[[Continue.|Continue into the ruins.]]

抵達開口處時，我輕輕踢了踢一塊牆體碎片，察覺到它移動時異常輕盈。我將視線轉向破洞邊緣，儘管邊緣參差不齊，但仍能看出材料的內部看起來與外部完全相同。

我好奇地伸手觸摸斷裂的邊緣，意識到即使看起來相同，但由於斷裂造成的質地與外部光滑表面截然不同。

踏進建築內部時，我的目光轉向黑暗的室內空間。這裡似乎是單一房間，牆上有凹陷的痕跡，可能曾用作儲物空間，也可能是我完全無法想像的其他用途。一側有個凹陷區域，填滿了樹葉和其他森林廢棄物。另一側則可見橢圓形的開口，很可能曾是居住在此處的人或物進出的門道。

未在空間內發現其他值得注意的事物，我沿原路退出。

[繼續。]

passages.name: You have any idea who she was?
passages.content:
"You have any idea who she was?"  I ask, my gaze shifting towards the body for several seconds before slipping back to Aaron.

"Not a clue,"  Aaron says, his own gaze flickering towards the Barmaid's Daughter once more, "And Wanton Cove is a fairly small community, so I know everyone."

"In that case, you don't know where she might have come from, do you?"  I follow up on the first question, my tone curious.

Aaron grimaces, his eyes moving about as if searching for an answer, "I don't know, the currents around these parts can be tricky.  We get things washed up on shore from all kinds of places."

Sighing inwardly, I change the subject.

passages.name: General Store
passages.content:
Nadia's General Store has a slight musty smell about it.  My boots striking the wooden floor is loud in the otherwise quiet store.

A cursory check of the store reveals that the shelves are stocked with the kind of food and equipment that one would expect of a small store in a meager town like Wanton Cove.

Slowly, I make my way through the store, taking note of what is here in case I have need of anything later.

Towards the back of the store, I come across a large counter-top, an assortment of candies and trinkets on display around an old button operated till.  

Directly behind the till is a middle aged lady.  Her flowing brown hair hangs loose down her back.  Her dark tanned skin is weathered and leathery.  Her brown eyes meet my own, the smile on her lips just barely touching her eyes.

When she opens her mouth to speak, I see that she is missing one of her front teeth, "And what do I owe this unexpected pleasure?"  She purrs.  Leaning over the counter-top, her ample bosom threatening to spill out of her frilly white shirt.

<<if $Nadia is "Played">>[[Talk with Nadia.|Nadia]]<<else>>[[Talk with the shopkeeper.|Nadia]]<</if>>

粗略檢查店內，可見貨架上擺滿了食物和裝備，正是萬頓灣這類貧瘠小鎮常見的小店會有的貨品。

我緩緩穿過店鋪，記下這裡的物品以備不時之需。

走到店鋪後方，我看見一個寬大的櫃檯，圍繞著一台老式按鍵收銀機，陳列著各式糖果和小飾品。

passages.content:
I can hear the babbling of running water before I see it.  There in an intersection is a fountain.  Relatively clean compared to the one in the town of Wanton Cove, but otherwise identical.

Moving closer, I quickly look around for any sign of danger before focusing my attention on the fountain.

Clear water flows from the round pool at the top, cascading into the next, then the next, as the water makes its way down to the wide round base.  

I circle the fountain, looking with interest at the engravings around the base.  The images are familiar, the same as those in the books, and while some of the characters are the same as the fountain in Wanton Cove I am able to quickly determine that whatever the engravings say, it is something different than the fountain in town.

//The fountain in Wanton Cove was clearly built by whoever built this, but what does that mean?  Was Wanton Cove once the site of more ruins?//

After a couple of minutes, I sigh and step back from the fountain.  //The fountain is amazing, but staring at it isn't getting me any closer to any answers.//  

Casting one last glance at the fountain, I return my attention to my surroundings.

[[Continue.|Continue into the ruins.]]

我繞著噴泉走了一圈，饒有興味地觀察基座上的雕刻。這些圖像很熟悉，與書中記載的相同，雖然部分字符與萬頓灣的噴泉相同，但我很快斷定，無論這些雕刻訴說什麼，其內容都與鎮上的噴泉不同。

My sudden movement draws the attention of the figure, their face turning towards me.  The person's features, while male, have a distinctly feminine cast to them. //Not Tom then.// I think, my pace slowing slightly.

passages.name: Murder of Crows
passages.content:
As I approach the park, I hear a "Caw!" from one of the trees.

I turn my attention to the sound only to see a large crow perched in one of the nearest trees to me.

"Caw!"  From another tree.

"Caw! Caw! Caw!"  Other crows begin to cry out.  

I notice dozens of large crows around me in the park in groups of half a dozen or more. 

"Caw!"  I can see that all of the crows appear to be focused on me, their beady black eyes turned my direction.

With the hairs on the back of my neck beginning to stand on end, the crows suddenly stop crying out, their silent stares boring into me.

Uneasy, I take a hesitant step backwards, hoping to slip back out of the park in an effort to remove myself from the unnatural scene.

I barely finish taking the step when all the crows take flight all at once.  

I throw my arms up to shield my face as the large crows take to the air, I can feel the air around me moving with the beating of their wings.

When I finally lower my arms, I look around.  The only sign that the murder of crows was ever in the park is a single black feather in the grass near the fountain.

[[Continue on your way.|overgrown park]]

passages.name: Happy End 6
passages.content:
Two days later, I've flown out to the east coast, and boarded a ferry to an island just off the coast.  My destination is the little fishing village named Wanton Cove.

I find myself standing on the deck, looking out over the choppy waters.  The wind is coming from the south, bringing with it a sweltering heat, my brown leather duster cracking sharply around me as a particularly strong gust of wind catches it.

Ahead, I can see the island, my destination.  From where I'm standing, all I can see is a sea of green trees over a particularly hilly island.  The wide stony beach wraps itself around the island, except for the little cove we're pointed straight at.  

I can see three large docks jutting out into the water, the little town seeming to blend right into the trees behind it.  //There's a figure standing at the end of the dock, I don't remember them being there before,//  I think, //Before?//

Above and behind me, the ship's horn sounds, followed by the Captain announcing that we will be arriving shortly.

I remain on the deck, watching as the figure slowly draws closer until I can see them clearly.

[[Continue.|Happy End 7]]

The water moves gently, barely a rise and fall among the jagged rocks.  But more troubling still is the way the wispy tendrils of fog that seem to reach impossibly towards the cliffs.  <<if $Day gte 5>>

//You'd think the rain would do away with this fog quickly, but it somehow seems to linger everywhere on this damned island.<</if>>

For more than a minute, I watch as the fog seems to reach towards me, just a little closer with every little rise of the water.  An unexpected wave comes in through the rocks, crashing against the cliff face in a spray of salty water.  The wave shifts and breaks the tendrils of fog, causing them to drift back down the cliff.

As the last signs of the wave vanishes amongst the shifting water among the rocks, the fog seems to recede from the cliff face.

passages.content:
<<if $Day gte 5>><<if $Madness gte 2>>My eyes turn upwards through the rain, looking at the shapes moving in the trees.  Beedy eyes look back at me, an entire flock of crows //a murder of crows// I remind myself that they are called.  They all follow my movements with a single minded focus that makes my skin crawl. 

//Are these creatures somehow her conduit?  Can she see what they are seeing right now?//

Clenching my jaw, I force myself to look away.  <<else>>Even through the thundering rain, I can see several large dark birds, crows, perched in the sickly trees throughout the park.  

//Caw!//  A single crow calls, followed by a murmer of similar sounds from some of the others.  The sound is nearly lost in the sound of the falling rain.<<endif>><<else>>There is a rustling in the trees overhead, causing me to look upwards.  Among the branches, I can see a host of crows, all seeming to watch me.

For a moment I just look back before returning my scrutiny to the rest of the park.  <<endif>>

[[Continue on my way.|overgrown park]]
passages.content_zh:
[IF $Day gte 5][IF $Madness gte 2]我的目光透過雨水向上望去，看著樹林中移動的身影。一雙雙珠子般的眼睛回望著我，一整群烏鴉——我提醒自己它們被稱為「謀殺」。牠們全都以一種令人毛骨悚然的專注力追隨著我的動作。

這些生物難道是她的媒介嗎？她能透過牠們的眼睛看見此刻的景象嗎？

咬緊牙關，我強迫自己移開視線。[ELSE]即使雷雨交加，我仍能看見幾隻巨大的黑色鳥類——烏鴉，棲息在公園裡病懨懨的樹上。

嘎！一隻烏鴉鳴叫，隨後其他幾隻也發出類似的低鳴。這聲音幾乎被落雨的聲響淹沒。[/IF][ELSE]頭頂的樹叢傳來沙沙聲，使我抬頭望去。在枝椏間，我看見一大群烏鴉，似乎全都在注視著我。

有那麼一瞬間，我只是回望著牠們，然後才將注意力轉回公園的其他部分。[/IF]

[繼續前行。]

passages.name: Give it the book.
passages.content:
I really don't want to give up the book, but I fancy myself a man <<if $Taint gte 95>>//woman?// <<elseif $Taint gte 55>>//Though its hard to say how much longer I can say that.// <</if>>of my word.  Pulling the tome from my jacket, I hold it out towards her.  She snatches it from my hand and looks at it.  I can see her eyes straying over the old leather cover, then she clutches it suddenly to her chest.  

Looking at me again, she says something, but it seems to be in some strange guttural language.

Before I can react, she springs into motion spinning on her heel before running towards the treeline.

"Wait!"  My call falls on deaf ears, though I continue to try getting her attention anyway, "Please!  Wait!  Maybe we can still help each other?  You can tell me more, and I can help you decypher that book?"

If she hears me at all, she gives no sign of it.  Instead, I'm left standing in the rain, now lacking one of the few potential clues I have found since arriving in Wanton Cove.

[[Continue.|Bluff]]<<set $PastorBook to "Gone">>
passages.content_zh:
我實在不願交出這本書，但我自認是個守信之人[IF $Taint gte 95]女人？[ELSEIF $Taint gte 55]雖然很難說我還能這樣自稱多久。[/IF]。我從夾克中抽出那本厚書，朝她遞去。她一把從我手中奪過，仔細端詳。我看見她的目光掠過那陳舊的皮革封面，然後她突然將書緊緊抱在胸前。

她再次看向我，說了些話，但似乎是某種奇怪的喉音語言。

我還來不及反應，她已猛然轉身，朝著樹林邊緣跑去。

「等等！」我的呼喊石沉大海，儘管我仍持續試圖引起她的注意，「拜託！等等！也許我們還能互相幫助？你可以告訴我更多，而我可以幫你解讀那本書？」

就算她聽見了，也沒有任何表示。於是，我被獨自留在雨中，此刻失去了我來到萬頓灣後發現的少數潛在線索之一。

[繼續。]

passages.name: White Goo.
passages.content:
"Tell me about this White Goo I've seen around town."  

"What White Goo?"  He asks.

<<if ($PlayerIntelligence + $PlayerPerception) gte 8>>Something about his tone tells me that he knows exactly what I'm talking about.

<<endif>>"Thick, white and slimy?"  I prod.

He shrugs dismissively, "Must have been bird shit."  He grunts.

Ask about:
<<if $TomTanya is "Not Played">>[[I'm looking for a young man named Tom who might have passed through.]]
<</if>>[[Wanton Cove]]
<<if $FirstStatue is "Played">>[[Church]]
[[Statues]]
<</if>><<if $Pastor is "Played">>[[Tell me more about the pastor.]]
<</if>><<if $MotherTalk is "Played">>[[Ask Aaron about the Morichi Family.]]
<</if>><<if $Fountain is "Played">>[[Engravings on the fountain.]]
<</if>><<if $Body1 is "Played">>[[Body on the shore.]]
<</if>><<if $JackobsonBluff is 3>>[[Do you know where Mr Jackobson might have gotten off to?]]<</if>><<if $FountainInvestigated is "Played">>
[[Ask Aaron about the Ruins.]]<</if>>
or
[[Step away from the Bartender.|The Barmaids Daughter]]
passages.content_zh:
「告訴我關於我在鎮上看到的那些白色黏液。」

「什麼白色黏液？」他問道。

[IF ($PlayerIntelligence + $PlayerPerception) gte 8]他的語氣讓我感覺，他完全明白我在說什麼。[/IF]

「濃稠、白色又黏滑的？」我追問。

他輕蔑地聳聳肩，「肯定是鳥屎。」他咕噥道。

詢問關於：
[IF $TomTanya is "Not Played"][我在找一個可能經過這裡的年輕男子，名叫Tom。]
[/IF][萬頓灣]

[雕像]
[/IF][IF $Pastor is "Played"][告訴我更多關於牧師的事。]
[/IF][IF $MotherTalk is "Played"][向Aaron詢問Morichi家族的事。]
[/IF][IF $Fountain is "Played"][噴泉上的刻文。]
[/IF][IF $Body1 is "Played"][岸邊的屍體。]
[/IF][IF $JackobsonBluff is 3][你知道Jackobson先生可能去哪裡了嗎？][/IF][IF $FountainInvestigated is "Played"]
[向Aaron詢問關於遺跡的事。][/IF]
或
[離開酒保。]

passages.name: Take the North fork of the trail.
passages.content:
Taking the North fork of the trail, I've barely walked a dozen paces before the <<if $cave is 2>>tight path I carved out of the brush is before me. <<else>>apparently well used trail becomes overgrown and difficult to follow.
<<if $Geography is "Played">>
Off in the distance, I can see the hint of a dark void, but it's hard to determine what the darkness could be, the brush is just too dense to make out any details. <<if $Day gte 3>>Nadia may have a way to get through this bramble. <<set $cave to 1>><<else>>I make a mental note of the spot in case it turns out to be important at some point, but at the moment, I have more pressing matters to attend. <<endif>><<endif>><<endif>>
<<if $cave gte 2>>Head to the [[cave|Cave Entrance]] or back <<traillink "the way I came.">><<elseif $Machete is "true">>Its time to carve a path through this mess. [[Forward to the darkness|Path][$cave = 2]] or back <<traillink "the way I came.">><<else>>Frowning, and not wanting to get lost in these woods, I turn back <<traillink "the way I came.">><<endif>>
passages.content_zh:
走上小徑的北岔路，我才走了十幾步，[IF $cave is 2]之前從灌木叢中開闢出的狹窄小徑就出現在眼前。[ELSE]原本明顯常有人走的小徑就變得雜草叢生，難以追蹤。
[IF $Geography is "Played"]
遠處，我能瞥見一片黑暗的虛空，但很難判斷那黑暗是什麼，灌木實在太濃密，無法看清任何細節。[IF $Day gte 3]Nadia或許有辦法穿過這片荊棘。[ELSE]我記下了這個地點，以防它將來可能很重要，但此刻我有更緊迫的事情要處理。[/IF][/IF][/IF]
[IF $cave gte 2]前往[洞穴]或返回[ELSEIF $Machete is "true"]是時候在這片混亂中開出一條路了。[[朝黑暗前進|Path][$cave = 2]]或返回[ELSE]皺著眉，不想在這些樹林中迷路，我轉身返回[/IF]

passages.name: SailorPairMain
passages.content:
On the other side of the street, I can see a pair of sailors walking.  <<if $GooCounter is 0>>The male sailor playfully grabs the ass of the female.  The female letting out a sound that was only half protest as they continue towards the waterfront. <<set $GooCounter += 1>><<elseif $GooCounter is 1>>While one of the sailors is obviously a woman, the other is more difficult to tell.  The second sailor is wearing men's clothes that fit somewhat loosely, and their feminine features are framed by what is obviously a man's haircut.  

The couple barely take notice of my presence as they continue on their way.<<set $GooCounter += 1>><<elseif $GooCounter gte 2>>Both sailors are female, their expressions grim as they walk in silence.

//Strange, there seems to be a lot of females in this town.  Even a lot of the sailors appear to be female.//

One of the two glances my way, her expression never changing as they continue towards the waterfront.  <<set $GooCounter -= 2>><</if>>

[[Continue on my way.|Head into the center of town.]]
passages.content_zh:
在街道的另一側，我看見一對水手正在行走。[IF $GooCounter is 0]那名男性水手嬉鬧地抓了女性水手的臀部。女性發出一聲半推半就的抗議，兩人繼續朝碼頭方向走去。[ELSEIF $GooCounter is 1]雖然其中一名水手明顯是女性，但另一位的性別較難辨認。第二位水手穿著有些寬鬆的男裝，其女性特徵被明顯屬於男性髮型的髮式所框襯。

這對伴侶幾乎沒注意到我的存在，繼續走他們的路。[ELSEIF $GooCounter gte 2]兩名水手都是女性，她們表情嚴肅地沉默前行。

奇怪，這鎮上的女性似乎很多。連水手也有不少是女性。

其中一人朝我瞥了一眼，表情毫無變化，她們繼續朝碼頭方向走去。[/IF]

[繼續趕路。]

passages.name: B1E
passages.content:
"Do you know of anyone around Wanton Cove who might be a bit //rough// with the ladies?"  I ask carefully.

Ms Sherwood shakes her head, almost violently, "No!"  She says, her eyes locking with mine, "The men around here might be a bit randy, but there's no lack of willing ladies,"  She blushes, her expression awkward as if she's trying to find a non-awkward way to continue.

Finally, she adds, "No, nothing like that, ever."  She smooths her skirts, a hint of color still in her cheeks.

Ask about:
[[You have any idea who she was?|B1A]]
[[What are you doing out here on the beach today?|B1B]]
<<if $B1Limp is "Played">>
[[Anyone go missing who had a limp?|B1C]]
<</if>><<if $B1Goo is "Played">>
[[Any idea why there would be White Goo on the body?|B1D]]
<</if>><<if $B1Shoulder is "Played">>
[[Can you think of anyone who might try hurting someone like that?|B1F]]
<</if>>
[[If I have any further questions, where can I find you?]]

or

[[I'm done talking to Ms Sherwood.|Investigate the pale shape amongst the rocks.]]
passages.content_zh:
「你知道萬頓灣這一帶，有誰可能對女性比較粗暴嗎？」我謹慎地問道。

Sherwood女士幾乎是猛烈地搖頭，「沒有！」她說道，目光緊鎖著我，「這附近的男人或許有點好色，但從不缺自願的女性。」她臉紅了，表情尷尬，彷彿在試圖找一種不那麼尷尬的方式繼續說下去。

最後，她補充道：「不，從來沒有那種事。」她撫平裙子，臉頰仍泛著一絲紅暈。

詢問：
[你知道她可能是誰嗎？]
[你今天怎麼會來這片海灘？]
[IF $B1Limp is "Played"]
[有沒有跛腳的人失蹤了？]
[/IF][IF $B1Goo is "Played"]
[知道為什麼屍體上會有白色黏液嗎？]
[/IF][IF $B1Shoulder is "Played"]
[你能想到有誰可能會那樣傷害人嗎？]
[/IF]
[如果我還有其他問題，可以去哪裡找你？]

或

[我問完Sherwood女士了。]

passages.name: nextRuins1
passages.content:
The tentacled thing twitches and writhes on the ground, tentacles flailing about it as it thrashes about.

I find myself transfixed at the sight of it, the skin darkening significantly as it finally grows still.  The tentacles shrivel, curling in on themselves as they become thin and stiff.  The eyes loose their shape, running from their sockets in the form of yellow white slime.  All the while the thick lips pull back little by little to reveal sharp yellow beaks with serrated edges.

When the sickly scent of rot hits my nostrils I take an involuntary step back, covering my face in an ill fated attempt to ward off the stench.

I take several more steps back and the tentacled thing's body collapses in on itself until it is little more than a mass of brownish goo with spots of white and yellow.

Not wanting to be anywhere near the dead thing or the foul odor it is creating, I move away from the creature, or rather what remains of it.


[[Continue.|Continue into the ruins.]]<<if $CharHP lte 1>><<set $CharHP = 1>><</if>>
passages.content_zh:
那觸手生物在地上抽搐扭動，觸手狂亂揮舞，猛烈掙扎。

我發現自己怔怔地盯著它，皮膚顏色明顯變深，最終靜止不動。觸手萎縮，向內蜷曲，變得細瘦僵硬。眼睛失去形狀，化為黃白色的黏液從眼窩流出。同時，那厚唇一點一點向後咧開，露出邊緣帶鋸齒的尖銳黃色喙部。

當腐敗的惡臭衝入鼻腔，我不由自主地後退一步，徒勞地掩住臉試圖阻擋那氣味。

我又後退幾步，觸手生物的軀體便向內塌陷，直到只剩一團棕褐色黏液，夾雜著白黃色斑點。

我不想靠近這死物或它散發的惡臭，於是從這生物——或者說牠的殘骸旁走開。

[繼續。][IF $CharHP lte 1][/IF]

passages.name: A Brief History of Wanton Cove
passages.content:
[[return|$return]]

Wanton Cove began appearing in historical records around 1645.  The area was first officially settled in 1652 by Zadok Alhazred, though there is no known record of the man before his arrival in the region.

The town of Wanton Cove, and the island of the same name rests a little over 20 miles off the east coast of the United States.

The island is host to ruins that predate the town, though no archeological survey has ever been launched to investigate further.  No one knows who these early inhabitants were.

The Native Americans who originally lived in the region spoke of a cursed island off the coast where those who went there returned mad, if they returned at all.  However given the superstitions of the native people, and the fact that the island has been settled for more than a hundred and fifty years at the time of this writing, it is reasonable to assume that this is nothing more than local mythology.

[[Return to Table of Contents.|Wanton Cove History Book]]

[[return|$return]]
passages.content_zh:
[返回]

萬頓灣約在1645年開始出現於歷史記載。該地區最早於1652年由Zadok Alhazred正式拓居，但此人在抵達該地區前的紀錄已不可考。

萬頓灣鎮及其同名島嶼位於美國東海岸外約20多英里處。

島上存有早於城鎮的遺跡，但從未進行過考古調查以深入探究。無人知曉這些早期居民是誰。

原居於此地區的美洲原住民曾談及海岸外一座受詛咒的島嶼，前往那裡的人歸來時皆已發狂——如果他們還能歸來的話。然而，考慮到原住民的迷信，以及截至撰寫本文時該島已被拓居超過一百五十年的事實，合理推測這僅是當地神話。

[返回目錄。]

[返回]

passages.name: Anyone go missing who had a limp?
passages.content:
"Looks like she she has some pretty serious scarring around her ankle, she might have walked with a limp,"  I begin, "No one with a limp went missing recently?"

Aaron seems to think about it for a second, "Only person I know of around here with a limp is Wilson Crow.  He's a fisherman, left on a boat less than a week ago."  Aaron shrugs, "But then, he lacks certain //feminine// qualities that this girl has."  He adds with a bitter smile.<<set $Crow to "Played">>

[[You have any idea who she was?]]
[[What are you doing out here on the beach today?]]<<if $B1Goo is "Played">>
[[Any idea why there would be White Goo on the body?]]<</if>><<if $B1Sex is "Played">>

or

「看來她腳踝附近有相當嚴重的疤痕，走路可能有點跛，」我開口道，「最近沒有跛腳的人失蹤嗎？」

Aaron似乎思考了一下，「這附近我知道唯一跛腳的人是Wilson Crow。他是漁夫，不到一週前乘船離開了。」Aaron聳聳肩，「不過嘛，他缺少這女孩擁有的某些女性特徵。」他苦笑著補充道。

What are you doing out here on the beach today?
passages.content:
"Mind telling me what you are doing out here on the waterfront today?"  I ask, "Seems like you should have your hands full with the Barmaid's Daughter, getting it ready for this evening?"

Aaron's expression turns hard, "I'm not sure I like what you're trying to imply here."  His voice holds a hint of anger to it, "But if you must know, I take a walk every chance I can when its not raining.

I nod, jotting down a couple of notes in my notepad.

passages.name: Any idea why there would be White Goo on the body?
passages.content:
"Looks like some of that White Goo on the body, near the crotch,"  I probe.

Aaron curls his lip slightly, <<if ($PlayerPerception + $PlayerIntelligence) gte 10>>and I'm not quite sure if its in disgust or contempt,<<else>>perhaps a hint of disgust in his expression,<</if>> "Must be something from the ocean that got on the body,"  He says.

"In that case, any ideas what could have made that White Goo?"  I continue.

Aaron shakes his head slowly, "No idea."  He finally says.

passages.name: commonParkEvents
Ruins25
passages.content:
A smear of glistening white fluid draws my attention.  Of their own accord, my footsteps draw me toward it, to where I can see a puddle of the stuff on the ground,

<<if $SailorGoo is "Played">>I can see even before I reach the puddle that it's the same kind of White Goo I have seen before.

Inhaling, my nostrils are filled with the heady scent of the goo.  I can feel my arousal growing and for just a second I feel dizzy.

[[Investigate the White Goo further.|Ruins Goo 1]]

[[Continue into the ruins.|Continue into the ruins.]]<<else>>The milky white slime seems thick, a thick musty scent, heavy with salt wafts off of the puddle.  

I can feel myself flush with arousal, and my head swims at the smell.

For a moment I look at the stuff, even prodding it with my boot.  It juggles like a jello mold at my touch but clings to my book as I pull back.

//Strange,// I think, taking a step back from the White Goo.

[[Continue.|Continue into the ruins.]]<<set $SailorGoo to "Played">><</if>>
passages.content_zh:
一道閃著光的白色液體痕跡吸引了我的注意。我的腳步不由自主地朝它走去，來到地上那灘黏液前。

[IF $SailorGoo is "Played"]還沒走到那灘黏液前，我就看出它和我之前見過的白色黏液是同一種。

我吸了口氣，鼻腔裡充滿了那黏液濃烈的氣味。我感到慾望在增長，有那麼一瞬間我感到暈眩。

[進一步調查白色黏液。]

[繼續深入遺跡。][ELSE]那乳白色的黏液看起來很濃稠，帶著一股濃重、陳腐且充滿鹽味的氣息從那灘液體飄散開來。

我感到自己因興奮而臉紅，那氣味讓我頭暈目眩。

我盯著那東西看了一會兒，甚至用靴子戳了戳。它像果凍一樣在我觸碰時晃動，但當我收回腳時卻黏在我的靴子上。

真奇怪，我心想，向後退了一步，離開那白色黏液。

[繼續。][/IF]

passages.name: Dock1
passages.content:
When I near the docks, I pause, looking out over the ocean.  <<if $Day gte 5>>Rain falls hard, as if trying to pummel the waves crashing into the rocky shore into submission.   As I watch, one wave crashes over the end of one of the docks, water running from the dock’s sides in a solid sheet until the bulk of the water is gone.

Another wave crashes against the shore not too far away, a spray of salty water showering me.  I take a couple of involuntary steps back as I try to shield my face from a second breaking wave.  Once I’m far enough away to avoid the worst of it, I look back out at the horizon, trying to determine where the water ends and where the storm begins, but the scene merely seems to fade one to the other.<<else>> The gentle waves lap softly at the rocky shore, accompanied by a stuff, but not unpleasant breeze.  <<endif>>

I let my gaze linger on the scene before me for several seconds before returning my attention to my investigations.

[[Continue on my way.|the waterfront]]
passages.content_zh:
當我靠近碼頭時，我停下腳步，望向大海。[IF $Day gte 5]雨下得很大，彷彿試圖將拍打岩石海岸的浪頭擊潰。正當我注視時，一個浪頭打上了其中一座碼頭的末端，海水像一整片水幕從碼頭兩側傾瀉而下，直到大部分海水退去。

不遠處另一個浪頭拍擊海岸，鹹澀的海水濺了我一身。我下意識地後退了幾步，試圖擋住臉避開第二個破碎的浪頭。等我退到足夠遠，避開最猛烈的浪花後，我再次望向地平線，試圖分辨哪裡是海的盡頭、哪裡是風雨的開始，但眼前的景象彷彿只是從一種過渡到另一種。[ELSE]輕柔的波浪輕輕拍打著岩石海岸，伴隨著一陣沉悶但並不令人討厭的微風。[/IF]

我讓目光在眼前的景象上停留了幾秒，然後將注意力拉回調查工作上。

[繼續我的路程。]

passages.content:
"Any chance you've remembered something new about Tom?  What might have happened to him, or where he might have gone?"  I ask.

Phil grimaces, "Sorry friend, I don't know any more than the last time you asked."  With a little shrug he adds, "But I'll be sure to let you know if I hear anything.<<if $Madness gte 1>>

//Does he really know nothing, or is he messing with me?//  The thought comes unbidden, unsettling me.  <<endif>>
